    《耳食录》 初编 [清] 乐钧 著

    自序
    This is the first edition of The Book of Ears, compiled by Yue Jun of the Qing dynasty.

    吴兰雪序
    Preface by Wu Lanxue

    重刻耳食录序
    Preface for the Reprinted Edition of The Book of Ears

    吴山锡序
    Preface by Wu Shanshi

    　　自序
    　　搜神志怪，噫吁诞哉！虽然，天地大矣，万物赜矣，恶乎有恶乎不有恶乎知恶乎不知仆鄙人也，羁栖之暇，辄敢操觚迫记所闻，亦妄言妄听耳．已则弗信，谓人信乎
    　　脱稿于辛亥，灾梨于壬子。史公所谓“与耳食何异”者此也，遂取以名编。

    　　Tao Jinling
    　　Preface
    　　Searching for spirits and strange tales, how astonishing and fantastic they are! Yet, the heavens are vast, and the myriad things are profound; how can there be nothing, and how can there not be something? How can one know, and how can one not know? I, a humble person, during my leisure from wandering, dare to take up my pen and hastily record what I have heard, also speaking and listening in vain. If it is already not believed, should it be believed by others?
    　　Completed in the year Xin Hai, printed in the year Ren Zi. This is what Mr. Shi referred to as "no different from ear food," and thus I named the compilation after it.

    5

    乾隆壬于夏日，临川乐钧元淑甫撰．
    　　吴兰雪序
    　　天下至文，本无定质，譬诸夕霞布空，倏忽异态，飞英绣水，纵横成章，要须自出机杼，为一家言。虽墨卿游戏，三味可参，不必高文典册始克与金石并寿也．
    　　吾友莲裳．早负儁才，高韵离俗。以粲花之笔，抒镂雪之思，摭拾所闻，纪为—编，曰《耳食录》。事多出于儿女缠绵、仙鬼幽渺，间山里巷谐笑助其波澜。胸情所寄，笔妙咸轃，虽古作者无多让焉。同好诸君请付剞劂，适仆至都，因属为叙．
    　　夫隋璧汉珠，荫映山海，岂藉誉者以增重哉良以寄兴偶同，寓言多感，梦簧褥友，乐奏先声。残署初退，兀坐紫藤之荫，追忆旧闻，手草数则，还以质之作者，而为述其梗概如此。
    　　乾隆壬子六月立秋日．东乡吴嵩粱兰雪撰．
    　　重刻耳食录序
    　　吾友乐莲裳《耳食录》一书，脍炙人口逾二十年矣。辛巳之岁，其于浚重为刊板，面索序于余。

    Explanation

    5

    In the summer of the Qianlong era (1762), the text was written by Le Jūn, also known as Yuan Shu Fu, from Linchuan.

    Preface by Wu Lanxue

    The finest writings in the world inherently lack a fixed form, much like evening clouds that change shapes swiftly in the sky, or embroidered water that forms patterns in all directions. Such writings must be original and unique, expressing one's own voice. Even if the writing is playful, it can still be meaningful and enjoyable, and does not need to be formal or official to endure.

    My friend, Lotus Dress, has long possessed exceptional talent and a refined, unconventional style. With his vivid and intricate pen, he expresses his thoughts as clear and delicate as carved snow. He has compiled his writings into a collection titled Ersi Lu (Record of Heard Things). The stories in the collection often involve romantic entanglements and supernatural elements, and occasionally include humorous anecdotes from the countryside. The writing reflects his inner feelings and is skillfully crafted, rivaling that of ancient masters. Fellow enthusiasts have requested that the collection be printed, and as I arrived in the capital, I was asked to write this preface.

    Like the precious treasures from the Sui and Han dynasties, which shine brightly and illuminate the mountains and seas, such writing does not need praise to gain value. It shares common themes and evokes many emotions, like a harmonious melody that evokes the first notes of joy. After the summer heatwave, I sat alone under the shade of a wisteria tree, recalling past events, and wrote down a few entries. I have shared these with the original author and will now briefly outline the essence of the collection.

    On the day of the beginning of autumn in the sixth month of the Qianlong era (1762). Written by Wu Songliang, also known as Lanxue, from Dongxiang.

    Preface for the Reissued Ersi Lu

    My friend, Lotus Dress, has compiled his writings into a collection titled Ersi Lu (Record of Heard Things), which has been widely praised for over twenty years. In the year 1801 (Xin Si), the collection was reissued in Jun, and I was asked to write this preface.

    6

    呜呼！吾何忍序吾莲裳之书乎！虽然，则尝闻之于莲裳矣：考信必本于六经，著书要归于有用。《上林》、《子虚》之赋，导十而惩一，君子弗尚也。况其下焉者乎少年绮语之过，吾自知之而悔之也久矣。夫学与年而俱进者也．莲裳之诗，至四十而益工．其辞元本忠孝，明达体用，知之者独曾宾谷侍郎耳。他人虽誉莲裳，而不能尽知。嗟乎！莲裳既塞于遇，复绌于年，卒不克一伸其志。世以才于目莲裳，而莲裳之心乃愈伤矣！
    　　犹忆甲于、乙丑间，余与刘芙初、陆祁孙。金手山辈同客曾侍郎两淮官署，一时琴樽文史之盛冠于江南。今余与侍郎发毵耗白矣，手山、祁孙或相见，或不相见，大率寥落无欢悰：而芙初、莲裳乃至不获享下寿以殁．今读莲裳遗书，不胜今昔存亡之感。即其书果无足重轻，犹将宝而传之，况诙诡奇恣之才终不可掩，固末可尽以莲裳所自言者一例而绳之者耶！

    Explanation

    6

    Alas! How can I bear to write the preface for my friend Lotus Dress's book! Nevertheless, I have heard from Lotus Dress himself: To verify truth must be based on the Six Classics, and writing books should ultimately serve a practical purpose. The "Shanglin" and "Zixu" rhapsodies, which guide ten but punish one, are not esteemed by the virtuous. How much more so for those below them! The mistake of youthful, ornate speech, I have long known and regretted. Learning advances along with age. Lotus Dress's poetry, by the age of forty, becomes even more skillful. His words fundamentally embody loyalty and filial piety, and he clearly understands principles and applications. Only Ceng Shilang, the Attendant, truly understands him. Others may praise Lotus Dress, but they do not fully comprehend him. Alas! Lotus Dress was blocked by opportunity and limited by age, finally unable to fully express his aspirations. The world regards him as talented, yet his heart is even more wounded!

    I still remember the years 1884 and 1885, when I and my friends Liu Fuchu, Lu Qishan, and Jin Shoushan were guests at the official residence of Ceng Shilang in Lianghuai. At that time, the flourishing of music, wine, literature, and history was unmatched in Jiangnan. Now, I and the Attendant have gray and thin hair, while Shoushan and Qishan sometimes meet and sometimes do not, mostly lonely and lacking joy. Liu Fuchu and Lotus Dress even failed to live out a normal lifespan. Now, reading Lotus Dress's posthumous writings, I cannot help but feel the profound contrast between past and present, life and death. Even if his writings are not of great weight, I will still treasure and pass them on. How much more so, then, for a talent that is whimsical, strange, and unrestrained, which cannot be entirely hidden, and which cannot be judged by the same standard as he himself sets!

    7

    聊书数语于筒端，以念世之读是书考，知莲裳之所自喜乃绝不在此，当亦莲裳之所心许焉已。
    　　道光元年十月，钱塘徐承思序。
    　　吴山锡叙
    　　《山海》徵奇，《齐谐》志怪，遐哉尚矣！下至张茂先《博物志》、王子年《拾遗记》，以及李冗《独异志》、赵磷《因话录》，孙光宪《北梦琐言》、宋永亨《搜采异闻录》，皆矜奇俶诡者所滥觞也．
    　　夫人寓形宇宙间，老死牖下者无论矣。其怀奇握异之士，胸中有万卷书，足迹行万里路。所莅之区，名公巨卿拥彗倒屣，词客骚人揽环结佩。酒酣耳热，挥麈雄潭。每遇可惊可愕可泣可歌之事，拈豪伸纸，发为新奇可喜之文。此虽才人之馀事，然非才人不能作也。

    7

    I casually write a few words on the end of a bamboo tube, to remind those who read this book, to know that Liánshang's true delight is not in this, and it should also be what Liánshang inwardly approves of.

    In the first year of the Daoguang era, October, Xú Chéngsī of Qiantang wrote this preface.

    Wú Shān Xī wrote this preface.

    The Shānhǎi Jīng (Classic of Mountains and Seas) records wonders, and the Qíxié (Records of the Strange) documents marvels—how remote and ancient they are! Down to Zhang Maoshan's Bówù Zhì (Record of Marvelous Things), Wang Zinián's Shíyí Jì (Record of Lost Things), Lǐ Rǒng's Dúyì Zhì (Record of Unique Things), Zhào Lín's Yīnhuà Lù (Record of Conversations), Sūn Guāngxian's Běimèng Suǒyán (Trivial Talk of the North), and Sòng Yǒnghéng's Sōucǎi Yìwén Lù (Record of Sought-after Strange Stories)—all of these are the origin of those who value the strange and bizarre.

    People living in the universe, those who die in their homes are not considered. Those who hold unique and extraordinary talents, having a thousand volumes of books in their minds, traveling ten thousand miles with their feet. The places they visit, high officials sweeping the ground and removing their shoes to welcome, poets and literati gathering and tying their rings and pendants. Drunk and flushed with heat, waving a brush to discuss grand topics. Every time encountering surprising, shocking, tear-inducing, and song-worthy events, picking up a pen and extending paper, producing new and delightful writings. This is an idle pursuit of talented people, but it is something only talented people can do.

    8

    临川乐莲裳先生，抱沉博瑰丽之才，弱冠后即担铅椠以游历四方。所过名山大川、通都古迹，一一记之以诗。出其绪馀，著《耳食录》前后编共二十卷，付诸剞劂。凡生平所闻、所传闻者悉载焉。殆莲裳殁后，版庋多年，间有蠹蚀漫漶而不可辨识者．令似滋亭重为刊刻刷布，以彰厥先人之美。
    　　余受而读之。其事之怪怪奇奇，固足赏心骇目．而文章之妙，如云霞变幻、风雨离合。其悲壮激昂者，真可敲缺唾壶，其缠绵婉丽者，又令人消魂欲死．然阐幽显微，醒愚祛惑之用，即隐寓其中，斯乃一片婆心，不可作游戏三昧观也．
    　　噫！莲裳虽逝，有子克家，能传播遗书而不使磨灭，则当年著书立说之愿斯可慰矣。刊既成，滋亭丐叙于余。余学识谫陋，不娴古作，乃钦其孝，勉撰弁语．无任主臣

    Explanation

    8

    Mr. Yue Lianchang of Linchuan, endowed with profound, extensive, and splendid talent, began traveling and carrying his writing materials to explore the world upon reaching adulthood. He recorded his journeys through poetry, documenting the famous mountains and rivers, major cities, and ancient sites he visited. From the remnants of his travels, he compiled "The Record of Heard Stories," which was published in two volumes, totaling twenty scrolls. Everything he had heard or heard about in his lifetime is recorded within. After Mr. Lianchang's passing, the printed copies were stored for many years, some becoming damaged by insects and illegible. Mr. Ziti ordered a reprint and distribution to honor the beauty of his ancestors.

    I received and read it. The stories within are strange and wonderful, certainly pleasing to the heart and shocking to the eyes. The beauty of the writing is like the changing clouds and winds, dynamic and unpredictable. The sad and grand passages can truly make one bang their spitting jar in anger, while the tender and graceful ones can melt one's soul and nearly bring them to death. Yet, the deeper purpose of revealing the hidden and the subtle, awakening the ignorant and dispelling confusion, is subtly implied within. This is a heart full of compassion, not to be seen as a game or trivial matter.

    Ah! Although Mr. Lianchang has passed away, he has a son who can manage the family and spread his legacy, preventing it from being forgotten. Thus, the wish to write books and establish ideas can now be fulfilled. After the publication was completed, Mr. Ziti asked me to write a preface. My knowledge is shallow, and I am not familiar with classical works, but I admire his filial piety and have written this preface with reluctance. Without limit, master and servant.

    9

    道光四年，岁在甲申八月朔日，平江吴山锡并书．
    　　耳食录卷一
    　　夕芳
    　　宜川张伊理，邃于学而不偶，家故贫．一于名露，年十三而伊理卒。露幼颇慧，善读父书。伊理以不遇感愤，竟令露废业。藉樵牧以奉母三载矣。
    　　同邑黄生，伊理旧友也。以文学教授乡里，怜而收之，今就学。露母泣而谓黄曰；“公惠救藐孤，实起死人而肉白骨。虽然，露之勿学，夫子之志也。”黄曰；“嫂氏休矣！郁而不发，岂理也哉张吾军者非露而谁？故人有知，当亦首肯。”卒教之．三年而文成，令就郡试。
    　　试有期矣，偶登城阙，兴发高吟，得“夕阳片石明羊角，芳草孤洲暗虎头”之句。一儒巾少年神骨清迥，前揖之曰：“君非宜川张君者欤？余云林郑玉也。适闻佳句，不减义山《锦瑟》之调。”露怪其拟议不伦，且问何以相识。玉笑曰：“仆固识君，君自不识仆耳。”露终茫然，阳谢之而心仪其人，渐与浃洽。

    9

    In the fourth year of Daoguang's reign, in the year of Jia Shen, on the first day of the eighth lunar month, Wu Shanxi of Pingjiang wrote.

    Volume One of Ears of Food Record

    Evening Fragrance

    Zhang Yili of Yichuan was deeply learned but not fortunate, and his family was originally poor. One of his sons, named Lu, gained fame at the age of thirteen, while Yili passed away. Lu was young but very intelligent and good at reading his father's books. Yili, feeling frustrated by his lack of success, eventually made Lu abandon his studies. Lu has been supporting his mother by cutting wood and herding animals for three years.

    Huang Sheng, a fellow villager and an old friend of Yili, taught literature to the villagers and pitied Lu, taking him in. Now, Lu is studying. Lu's mother wept and said to Huang, "You have kindly saved this poor orphan, truly reviving the dead and restoring flesh to the bones. However, Lu's not studying is your intention." Huang replied, "Sister-in-law, rest assured! Suppressing and not expressing oneself, is it right? Who else but Lu can lead my army? Those who know me will surely agree." He finally taught Lu. After three years, Lu's writing was completed, and he was sent to take the county examination.

    The exam has a set date. Lu happened to climb the city gate and, inspired, recited a poem, coming up with the lines "The evening sun illuminates a stone with a sheep's horn, fragrant grass on a lonely islet hides a tiger's head." A young scholar with a clear and distant aura approached and bowed to him, saying, "Are you not Zhang Jun from Yichuan? I am Zheng Yu from Yunlin. I just heard these beautiful lines, which are no less than the style of Li Shangyin's 'Jinse.'" Lu was surprised by the young scholar's comparison and asked how he knew him. Zheng smiled and said, "I definitely know you, but you don't recognize me." Lu remained puzzled, politely thanked him, and admired him inwardly, gradually becoming close and familiar with the young scholar.

    10

    日既暮，邀归旅舍．偶及试事，玉曰：“君程文太高，恐不谐俗．盍揣摩时好，以图一当”露笑曰：“伯牙不以里耳改弦，王良不为获禽易辙，况文章不朽之业，安敢自贬”玉唯唯，比晓辞去。数日，杳不复至．
    　　试既毕，玉忽来，袖出露试卷掷案上．露失色，问所从来．玉指其卷曰：“此岂利器哉吾为君谋易之，已获售矣。”露意疑．已而果售，始异其盲．及视所易卷，文甚劣，然心德之，向玉称谢。玉叹曰：“吾非逐臭名场者。以君禄命太薄，又得文名，恐造物见怒，姑为此态．君先世有清德，厥后必大。”露由是愈奇之。居数日，别去。露亦归，往见黄，未言也。

    Explanation

    10

    As the day turned to dusk, they were invited back to the inn. During their conversation, the topic of the examination came up. Yu said, "Your essay is too lofty; it might not conform to popular tastes. Why not adjust to current preferences in order to succeed?" Lou laughed and replied, "Bo Ya would not change his playing style even if it was not appreciated by the common people, and Wang Liang would not alter his driving technique to catch animals. How could I dare to lower the standards for an immortal work of literature?" Yu nodded silently and left by morning. For several days, he did not return.

    After the examination was completed, Yu suddenly appeared, pulling out Lou's essay from his sleeve and throwing it onto the table. Lou turned pale and asked where it came from. Yu pointed to the essay and said, "Is this a sharp tool? I have helped you revise it, and it has already been accepted." Lou felt suspicious. Soon enough, the revised essay was indeed accepted, and he began to marvel at Yu's blindness. When he looked at the revised version, the writing was very poor, but he felt grateful in his heart and thanked Yu. Yu sighed and said, "I am not one who chases after fame. Your fate is thin, and you have gained a reputation for literature, which might anger the heavens. I temporarily adopted this attitude. Your ancestors had pure virtue, and in the future, there will be great success." Lou found him even more remarkable after this. After several days, Yu left. Lou also returned home and went to see Huang, but did not speak.

    11

    勿报玉至，仆从甚都，即执贽见黄，愿为弟子。黄既以霹为先容，深器之。玉复与露约为兄弟，玉长一岁，露兄之。明日，玉至露家，升堂拜母，以金帛数事为羔雁．露及母坚辞不受．玉曰：“以吾弟空乏，且当周赠无己；戋戋之奉，何乃却之弟母即我母也，又何间焉？”露始谢而受之．嗣是，日用衣食之需悉取给于玉。而赠黄者亦甚厚．
    　　居数月，玉曰：“已为弟谋得佳妇，六礼无所需，吉期在迩，弟当往赘。如以慈母暌隔为辞，一月后俱归可也。”露问为淮氏之女，五日：“即去当知之。”问之再三，终不告．露疑，以白母及黄。黄曰：“郑生固良士，其所谋当不妄。秘之，故以示奇耳．否则，为尔惜费也．尔第行。”

    Explanation

    11

    Do not report that Yu has arrived, for his attendants are very numerous. Immediately, he took a gift and presented it to Huang, wishing to become his disciple. Huang, having introduced Pib as a preliminary introduction, highly valued him. Yu then made an agreement with Lu to be brothers, with Yu being one year older, and Lu treated him as a brother. The next day, Yu went to Lu's house, ascended the hall to pay respects to his mother, and presented several items of gold and silk as a gift. Lu and his mother firmly declined to accept it. Yu said, "Because my younger brother is in need, and I should provide for him without end; such a small offering, why should you refuse it? Your mother is my mother too, and why should there be any difference?" Lu then thanked and accepted it. From then on, all daily needs for clothing and food were provided by Yu. And the gifts given to Huang were also very generous.

    After several months, Yu said, "I have already arranged a good wife for my younger brother, and there is no need for the six rites; the wedding date is near, and my younger brother should go to her family. If you use your mother's separation as an excuse, you can both return home in a month." Lu asked if it was a daughter of the Huai family, for five days: "If you leave now, you will know." Asked repeatedly, he finally did not tell. Lu was suspicious and reported to his mother and Huang. Huang said, "Zheng is indeed a good man, and his plans should not be baseless. Keep it secret, so as to show it as something extraordinary. Otherwise, it would be a waste of money for you. Just go ahead."

    12

    乃与玉俱，不三十里而至。朱门洞启，院宇轩华。玉下马径入，使露候于外。
    　　须臾，主人出，年五十许，仪服伟然，肃客入。露趋拜，主人熟视而称曰：“真英特也！”既坐，主人曰：“旧奉贤尊命，许与老夫结朱陈，今以次女夕芳侍君子箕帚．荆妇舐犊之爱，不欲弱女遽隔晨昏，故累君下贲耳。”露起面谢曰：“叨近冰清，极知欣幸。然露先人早背，安得与大人联姻且露之来也，郑兄实媒之。适奉尊教，诚所未喻。”主人笑曰：“未识郑玉耶老大贱息也。数岁前受业贤尊，老夫心契，嗣是有婚姻之约，故令玉访君。而君‘夕阳芳草’之句，适隐道小女之名，故玉得相遇于城闼也。”

    Explanation

    12

    And went with the jade together, arriving without traveling thirty miles. The grand red gate was wide open, and the courtyard and buildings were splendid. The jade dismounted and entered directly, while Lu was instructed to wait outside.

    After a short while, the host came out, around fifty years old, with dignified attire, and respectfully invited the guests in. Lu hurriedly bowed, and the host looked at him closely and said, "Truly extraordinary!" After they sat down, the host said, "Formerly, I received the command of the noble elder to marry my daughter to you. Now, I am offering my second daughter Xi Fang to serve the gentleman with broom and dustpan. My wife has the love of a mother for her child, and she does not want her young daughter to be separated from us so soon. Thus, I have troubled you with this matter." Lu stood up and bowed to thank him, saying, "To be close to someone as pure as ice, I am extremely happy. But my ancestors passed away early, how can I marry into the family of a great person? Moreover, I came here because Vang Yu, my friend, was the matchmaker. Just received your instruction, truly I do not understand." The host smiled and said, "Do you not recognize Zheng Yu? He is my humble eldest son. Several years ago, he studied under the noble elder, and my heart was in agreement with him. Thereafter, there was a marriage agreement, so I sent Yu to visit you. And your poem 'Sunset and Fragrant Grass' just happened to match the name of my daughter, thus Yu met you at the city gate."

    13

    露再欲启问，忽传夫人出见婿。颜貌如三十许人，衣饰华洁。侍者数人，序坐少时，多作家人怜惜之语。而堂角帘小诸女窥客，吃吃笑语。露颇踧踖，莫知措对。已而玉整衣而出，笑曰：“昔为盟弟，今则内兄矣。”露亦笑。遂引入宾馆，设食。有数女，或称青站，或称云阁夫人，或称素英姊，或称阿丽，往来戏乐。窥瞰其门，风态妖冶。言词谑浪，窃窃私语。露颇闻之，而莫敢支应，腹议而已。
    　　次日，庭施供帐，工奏音乐。女仆执烛捧香，拥夕芳出．严妆袨服，珠翠荧煌。成礼于金碧之堂，同牢于绮罗之席。既成眷属，情好笃洽。玉相得之欢，亦更愈于往日。甫匝月，露乃告归，将迎妇以觐母．夕芳了无难辞，而玉父母皆有怅恨之色。

    Explanation

    13

    Lu wanted to ask again, but suddenly it was announced that the lady came out to meet her son-in-law. Her appearance was like that of a woman in her thirties, and her attire was splendid and clean. Several attendants sat in order for a short while, mostly speaking words of familial affection and pity. Meanwhile, several young ladies peeped at the guest from behind the curtains at the side of the hall, giggling and chatting. Lu felt quite embarrassed, not knowing how to respond, only discussing it in his mind. Soon, Jade (the lady) adjusted her clothes and came out, smiling and saying, "Once I was your sworn younger brother, now I am your brother-in-law." Lu also smiled. She then led him into the guesthouse and arranged for a meal. There were several women, some calling her Qingzhan, some calling her Lady Yunguo, some calling her Sister Suying, and some calling her Ali, who came and went, playing and having fun. Peeping at her door, she had a charming and seductive appearance. Her words were playful and flirtatious, and she whispered to herself. Lu heard some of it but dared not respond, only discussing it in his mind.

    The next day, the courtyard was set up with supplies and tents, and musicians played music. Servants held candles and incense, escorting Xi Fang out. She was dressed in elaborate attire, adorned with pearls and jade that shone brightly. The ceremony was held in the golden and jade hall, and they shared a meal on the brocade and silk mat. After becoming husband and wife, their feelings were deep and harmonious. Jade's joy in being together was even greater than before. After a month, Lu then announced his return, intending to bring his wife to meet his mother. Xi Fang had no difficulty in saying goodbye, but Jade's parents all had looks of sadness and regret.

    14

    露归告母，母甚喜悦。黄闻之，亦先来问状．既言及其父约婚授徒之事，露疑昔时父或为之．母愕然曰：“尔父平昔家居交接殊募，安得有是是必有异。”翌日．同往迹之。至则斜阳一片，秋草离离，愁白杨之悲风，咽空山之流水。知逢鬼物，举家惊讶。露心伤目断，殆亦离魂，踯躅而归，耿耿终日．
    　　一夜，梦门外簿卤驺从，肩舆中一人乃其父也，谓露曰：“余向馆郑氏，获订姻事。今年蒙上帝授地府司宪，迫于公剧，末暇视尔。兹因凤皇山苞元神君邀赴赏花之宴，故迂道来家，告尔以由。郑氏偶为避难，移家阳曲．新妇贤淑，终当合并．吾已使人筹画，尔无忧也．尔师厚德，吾所深铭．玉前奉尔师金帛，皆吾俸馀之物。为吾谢之，匪以云报，亦故人地下之情耳！尔母吾不及晤，尔其告之”言讫，挥泪而去。露觉而异之，闻者亦莫不嗟叹．然“合并”之语，莫得而明，意谓非死不能也，露殆不永矣，反更忧之。

    Explanation

    14

    Lu returned to tell his mother, who was very happy. Huang heard about it and also came to inquire about the situation. After mentioning his father's arrangements for marriage and teaching, Lu suspected that his father might have done it in the past. His mother was startled and said, "Your father usually interacted with people in a very different way; how could he have done this? There must be something unusual." The next day, they went together to trace it. When they arrived, they saw a patch of setting sun, scattered autumn grass, the sad wind of the white poplar, and the choked flow of the mountain stream. They knew they had encountered a ghostly being, and the whole family was surprised. Lu felt heartbroken and his vision blurred; he seemed to have lost his soul, hesitating on his way back, and remained troubled all day.

    One night, he dreamed of officials and servants outside the door, and a person in a sedan chair was his father. He told Lu, "I was staying at the Zheng household and arranged a marriage. This year, I was appointed by God as the judge in the underworld, and I am too busy with official duties to see you. Now, because the Immortal of Phoenix Mountain invited me to a flower-viewing banquet, I took a detour to come home and inform you of the reason. The Zheng family has temporarily moved to Yangqu to avoid danger. Your future wife is virtuous and beautiful, and you will eventually be united. I have already arranged things, so you have nothing to worry about. Your teacher's great virtue is deeply etched in my memory. I previously gave your teacher gold and silk, which were all from my salary. Please convey my thanks to him; it is not a return for his kindness, but a gesture of friendship from the afterlife. I did not have the chance to meet your mother; please tell her." After he finished speaking, he shed tears and left. Lu woke up and found it strange; those who heard about it also could not help but sigh. However, the meaning of "merger" is unclear; it seems to imply that it can only happen after death. Lu may not live long, and instead, he became even more worried.

    15

    一日，露傍徨郊外，遥见彩舆一秉，后一人，从数十骑，来甚建．既近，则玉也。即下马相持痛哭，语别后事，不胜呜咽，云：“自君去之夕，即为仇家所攻，几投凶暴之烬．赖与府帅有旧．遣兵救护，老幼家口仅以获兔，避患他州。极知爽信始忧，无由陈达，比得尊人书谕，伸玉送舍妹于归。今已送至君家，君宜速归，加意调护。玉亦从此别矣，幽明道隔，相见伺期！”言罢，复痛哭．留之，不可，上马驰去．
    　　露踉跄至家，寂然无所见。神色黯悴，径投卧内，将以啼痕长渍衾枕矣。既入，乃见锦帐低垂，奁具盈室。亟呼母，搴帷视之，一丽女卧于绣榻，气息如缕，乃夕芳也。
    　　有顷，目开四顾，见露而泣，半晌能言，曰：“妾形体初复，宜令人环坐，以受生气。”于是邻女骈集，皆言国色。夕芳故善言词，虽卧息寡言，而偶尔酬答，悉出意表。诸女咸悦之，昼夜更番守坐，争以气嘘其口中。

    Explanation

    15

    One day, Lù wandered aimlessly in the outskirts and saw a colorful carriage followed by a person and dozens of horsemen. As they got closer, it was Yù (玉). Lù immediately dismounted and embraced Yù, weeping bitterly as they talked about the events after their separation. Yù could not help but sob, saying, "Since the night you left, I was immediately attacked by enemies and almost thrown into the ashes of violence. Thanks to an old acquaintance with the governor, soldiers were sent to rescue us, and the elderly, children, and family members barely escaped. We had to flee to another state to avoid danger. I was extremely aware of the initial worry of breaking faith and had no way to express it. Recently, I received a letter from a respected elder, which instructed Yù to send my younger sister back home. She has already been sent to your home. You should return quickly and take good care of her. Yù will also bid farewell from here, as the path between the living and the dead is separated. We will meet again at some future time!" After speaking, Yù wept again. Lù tried to keep Yù, but Yù could not be persuaded, mounted the horse, and galloped away.

    Lù staggered back home and found the house empty and silent. With a somber and weary expression, he went directly to his bedroom, intending to leave tears staining the bedding. As he entered, he saw a brocade canopy hanging low, and the room filled with trousseau items. He immediately called his mother, who pulled aside the curtain. A beautiful girl lay on an embroidered bed, breathing faintly. It was Xīfāng (夕芳).

    After a while, Xīfāng opened her eyes and looked around, saw Lù, and wept. After a while, she could speak and said, "My body is just recovering. It is advisable to have people sit around me to receive the vital energy." Then, neighbors' daughters gathered, all saying she was a national beauty. Xīfāng was good at speaking, although she spoke little when resting, but occasionally responded, all exceeding expectations. All the girls were pleased with her and took turns watching over her day and night, competing to blow air into her mouth.

    16

    七日而起坐，半月而能行，一月而饮食起居，丁与人无异。
    　　乃言其父郑氏，名洛，云林人，家颇丰。男女仆婢数十人，悉死于疫。妾时年十六，其司言阳数未终，当再生为君妻。风雷启墓，舆马护行，迷罔之间，遂已至此。前者一月缱绻，犹能记忆了了。所谓仇家肴，盖疫鬼也。
    　　夕芳性质柔婉，伉俪甚笃，事堂上尤以孝称．常念其父母兄弟不置。每岁寒食，辄与露扫其墓，恸哭而归．后举一子，仕至州刺史．
    　　一—此前明天启时事．
    　　邓无影
    　　邓乙年三十，独处，每夜坐，一灯荧然，沈思郁结．
    　　因顾影叹息曰：“我与尔周旋日久，宁不能少怡我乎”其影忽从壁上下，应曰：“唯命。”乙甚惊，而影且笑曰：“既欲尔怡，而反我慢，何也”乙心定，乃问：“尔有何道而使我乐？”曰：“惟所欲。”

    Explanation

    16

    After seven days, she could sit up; after half a month, she could walk; and after one month, her daily life, including eating and sleeping, was indistinguishable from that of others.

    She then spoke of her father, Vang, whose surname was Zheng, and who was a man from Yunlin. His family was quite wealthy. There were dozens of male and female servants and maids who all died from an epidemic. At the time, she was sixteen years old, and it was said that her yang number had not yet ended, and she should be reborn as the man's wife. The wind and thunder opened the tomb, and chariots and horses protected her journey. In the midst of confusion and bewilderment, she arrived here. The previous month of their intimate relationship was still vividly remembered. What was referred to as the "enemy's dish" was actually the plague spirit.

    Xifang's nature was gentle and soft, and she was very devoted to her husband. She was especially known for her filial piety. She often thought of her parents and siblings. Every year on the Cold Food Festival, she would sweep their graves with dew and return home, weeping bitterly. Later, she gave birth to a son who became a Prefect of a state.

    This is a story from the Ming Dynasty during the Tianqi era.

    Deng Wuying
    Deng Yi, aged thirty, lived alone. Every night, he sat with a dimly lit lamp, deep in troubled thoughts.

    He looked at his shadow and sighed, saying, "I have been with you for so long, can you not bring me some comfort?" The shadow suddenly descended from the wall and replied, "I will obey your command." Deng Yi was greatly surprised, and the shadow laughed and said, "If you want comfort, why are you disrespectful to me?" Deng Yi calmed down and asked, "What can you do to make me happy?" The shadow replied, "Whatever you desire."

    17

    乙曰：“吾以孤栖无偶，欲一少年良友长夜晤对，可乎”影应曰；“何难”即已成一少年．鸿骞玉立，倾吐风流，真良友也．乙又令作贵人。饿顷，少年忽成官长，衣冠俨然，踞床中坐，乃至声音笑貌，无不逼肖．乙戏拜之，拱受而已。乙又笑曰：“能为妙人乎”官长点头下床，眨眼间便作少女，容华绝代，长袖无言。乙即与同寝，无异妻妾．
    　　由是日晏灯明，变幻百出，罔不如念．久之．日中亦渐离形而为怪矣．他人不见，唯乙见之．如醉如狂，无复常态。人颇怪之，因诂而知之。视其影，果不与形肖也，形立而影或坐，形男而影或女也．以问乙，而乙言其所见则又不同．一乡之人以为妖焉．
    　　后数年，影忽辞去。问其所之，云在寓次之山，去此数万馀里。乙泣而送之门外，与之诀。影凌风而起，顷刻不见。乙自是无影，人呼为“邓无影”云．

    Explanation

    17

    乙曰：“吾以孤栖无偶，欲一少年良友长夜晤对，可乎？”影应曰：“何难？”即已成一少年。鸿骞玉立，倾吐风流，真良友也。乙又令作贵人。饿顷，少年忽成官长，衣冠俨然，踞床中坐，乃至声音笑貌，无不逼肖。乙戏拜之，拱受而已。乙又笑曰：“能为妙人乎？”官长点头下床，眨眼间便作少女，容华绝代，长袖无言。乙即与同寝，无异妻妾。

    由是日晏灯明，变幻百出，罔不如念。久之，日中亦渐离形而为怪矣。他人不见，唯乙见之。如醉如狂，无复常态。人颇怪之，因诂而知之。视其影，果不与形肖也，形立而影或坐，形男而影或女也。以问乙，而乙言其所见则又不同。一乡之人以为妖焉。

    后数年，影忽辞去。问其所之，云在寓次之山，去此数万馀里。乙泣而送之门外，与之诀。影凌风而起，顷刻不见。乙自是无影，人呼为“邓无影”云。

    18

    ——徐懋庵言之．
    　　云阳鬼
    　　云阳之东有丛林，素传多鬼魅，往往白昼搏人．
    　　一健儿过其地，心甚怖．忽一少年奔而逐之，健儿骇呼曰：“鬼！鬼！……”因疾走，为梗绊而仆，几伤足．少年追既及，搀健儿起，谓之曰：“无畏。”语未毕，健儿奋拳击之，少年驻呼曰：“鬼……鬼……”乃亦攘臂击健儿。
    　　正斗间，一人岸帻昂然而来，问二人何斗，各应曰：“鬼！鬼！”岸帻者笑曰：“惑哉！是乌有鬼哉！”理论再三，二人始释手．各通姓名乡里，盖平昔相知而未相识者，遂相视面笑，且曰：“今三人同行，不复畏矣。”
    　　不数武，岸帻者在后大笑曰：“二公真雅量，如某之丑陋，犹不畏耶”二人回顾，见岸帻者身长丈馀，面大如方相，黑白各半。二人齐呼曰：“鬼！鬼！……”骇绝仆地，鬼亦遭灭．

    Explanation

    18

    A Woman with Two Heads and Four Arms

    During the reign of Emperor Ling, a woman from Luoyang was born with two heads and four arms.

    19

    非非子曰：甚矣，鬼之难识也！当二人疑惧之际，彼此互观，觉衣服、手足、耳目事事皆鬼，而实则非鬼．而为之居间而排难者乃真鬼，而反亲之而求助也．甚矣，鬼之难识也！
    　　使鬼不乘衅而出，或见于二人独行之时，则毒手饱拳，鬼当之矣。此鬼之所熟筹而万万不出于此者也．呜呼！鬼亦狡谲矣哉！
    　　石室虎
    　　里人陈献，无赖子也，多阴恶，而外复无状．人惮之，莫敢谁何。
    　　一夜，梦一人来曰：“大王召尔。”献问：“汝何人？”曰：“吾伥也。”献不觉随之行。至一山，林本深邃。入一石室，一虎头人踞石而坐。伥前曰：“献至。”使跪于前．虎头人曰：“汝知我山中无食乎”献叩头乞哀。虎头人曰：“汝性犷悍，行复腥秽，实无人味。虽当果吾腹，而惧汙吾。”曰：“今召尔魂至，暂充庖厨，作蔬菜耳。若以为吾粮，非历千劫不可也。”献惶惧，不知所对。

    Explanation

    19

    非非子曰：甚矣，鬼之难识也！当二人疑惧之际，彼此互观，觉衣服、手足、耳目事事皆鬼，而实则非鬼。而为之居间而排难者乃真鬼，而反亲之而求助也。甚矣，鬼之难识也！

    使鬼不乘衅而出，或见于二人独行之时，则毒手饱拳，鬼当之矣。此鬼之所熟筹而万万不出于此者也。呜呼！鬼亦狡谲矣哉！

    石室虎

    里人陈献，无赖子也，多阴恶，而外复无状。人惮之，莫敢谁何。

    一夜，梦一人来曰：“大王召尔。”献问：“汝何人？”曰：“吾伥也。”献不觉随之行。至一山，林本深邃。入一石室，一虎头人踞石而坐。伥前曰：“献至。”使跪于前。虎头人曰：“汝知我山中无食乎？”献叩头乞哀。虎头人曰：“汝性犷悍，行复腥秽，实无人味。虽当果吾腹，而惧汙吾。”曰：“今召尔魂至，暂充庖厨，作蔬菜耳。若以为吾粮，非历千劫不可也。”献惶惧，不知所对。

    20

    伥鬼已前褫其衣，执之璺室，牛刀缕切之，馀其骨而已。痛楚万状，哀呼，终不顾。切既毕，盛以大盘。旁系二封豕。虎头人取食之，每食一蹄，或一脔，辄以盘中物少许下之。献虽痛极昏晕，知识终不昧。食尽，乃醒矣。
    　　次夜，又梦之如初。如是将三年，无间夕。心甚恶之，未尝告人。
    　　一夜，虎头人谓曰：“汝千劫已满，骨味当少佳，可以饭我矣。明日正午，可沐浴俟我。”献请曰；“某虽不善，当获罪；面大王若此，不太虐乎”虎头人曰：“是犹未足为甚也。尚有万劫、数千劫者，下而至于数劫、数十劫不等。皆天曹所命，视其恶而斟酌焉。”因指伥鬼曰：“此万劫者也。以汝所为，尚不指千劫．然汝妻娐贱娼且二十年，丑声四播，故汝得从末减耳。”因取书一册，令观之．皆列当食者姓名，颇有平日相识者，而献名亦在，劫亦符合。

    20

    A ghost, previously known as a伥鬼, stripped off his clothes and took him to a room with cracks or fissures. He sliced him with a sharp knife, leaving only his bones. Enduring extreme pain in countless ways, he cried out in sorrow, but finally not looking back. After slicing was completed, he placed the remains in a large plate. Two pigs were tied beside it. The tiger-headed person took to eat, each time eating a hoof or a piece of meat, and would then take a little from the plate and swallow it. Although Xian was in extreme pain and nearly fainting, his consciousness remained clear. After eating everything, he woke up.

    The next night, he dreamed of it again as before. Like this, for three years, without missing a night. He deeply disliked it, but never told anyone.

    One night, the tiger-headed person said to him, "You have completed your thousand rebirths, so your bone flavor should be somewhat better. It can be used to feed me. Tomorrow at noon, you can bathe and wait for me." Xian asked, "I am not good, but I will be punished. To treat me like this, isn't it too cruel?" The tiger-headed person replied, "This is not the worst yet. There are those with ten thousand rebirths and thousands of rebirths. Down to several rebirths and dozens of rebirths, varying. All are decreed by the Heavenly Court, according to their sins." Then he pointed to the ghost and said, "This is one with ten thousand rebirths. Because of what you have done, you are not even at a thousand rebirths. But your wife has been a lowly prostitute for twenty years, and her bad reputation has spread far and wide. Therefore, you receive a lesser punishment." Then he took a book and made him look at it. It listed the names of those to be eaten, many of whom were familiar to him. And your name was also on it, with the corresponding number of rebirths.

    21

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔”献又哀请，曰：?某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎’虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦。奈汝孽太重，天曹有命：千劫已上者例不赦，万劫者例不减。吾哀汝一念之复，且佐吾盘餐者三年，岂得无情？当从例减汝。汝分于明日当食，今且宽一月．一月之后，汝当自经死；吾来啖汝尸，不致汝生受支解之苦：此所以减也．汝姑去。”献倏然已醒。

    Explanation

    21

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下。”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔。”献又哀请，曰：“某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎？”虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦。奈汝孽太重，天曹有命：千劫已上者例不赦，万劫者例不减。吾哀汝一念之复，且佐吾盘餐者三年，岂得无情？当从例减汝。汝分于明日当食，今且宽一月。一月之后，汝当自经死；吾来啖汝尸，不致汝生受支解之苦：此所以减也。汝姑去。”献倏然已醒。

    Translation:

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下。”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔。”献又哀请，曰：“某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎？”虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦。奈汝孽太重，天曹有命：千劫已上者例不赦，万劫者例不减。吾哀汝一念之复，且佐吾盘餐者三年，岂得无情？当从例减汝。汝分于明日当食，今且宽一月。一月之后，汝当自经死；吾来啖汝尸，不致汝生受支解之苦：此所以减也。汝姑去。”献倏然已醒。

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下。”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔。”献又哀请，曰：“某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎？”虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦。奈汝孽太重，天曹有命：千劫已上者例不赦，万劫者例不减。吾哀汝一念之复，且佐吾盘餐者三年，岂得无情？当从例减汝。汝分于明日当食，今且宽一月。一月之后，汝当自经死；吾来啖汝尸，不致汝生受支解之苦：此所以减也。汝姑去。”献倏然已醒。

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下。”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔。”献又哀请，曰：“某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎？”虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦。奈汝孽太重，天曹有命：千劫已上者例不赦，万劫者例不减。吾哀汝一念之复，且佐吾盘餐者三年，岂得无情？当从例减汝。汝分于明日当食，今且宽一月。一月之后，汝当自经死；吾来啖汝尸，不致汝生受支解之苦：此所以减也。汝姑去。”献倏然已醒。

    献遂执书辨曰：“天下如某者亦岂少哉东村余阿三者，罪与某略同，何不列名此书而考终牖下。”虎头人曰：“天曹之刑，不可备知。然所云余阿三，吾固闻之：彼已为豕矣，宰割千生，岂减于尔。”献又哀请，曰：“某今知罪矣，愿改行从善。大王独不能赦某乎？”虎头人曰：“颇亦有所赦

    22

    自是神色沮丧，知死期将至．每为众人言之，闻者皆股憟。
    　　一日晨起，缢于庭椽。妻惊见，解救，——已死矣，——大呼邻人。众方集视，忽一黑虎冲门来，众皆辟易。虎竟攫献尸以去。
    　　非非子曰：李青莲先生自称诲上钓鳌客，谓以天下无义丈夫为饵．而辨之者曰：“是饵也，鱼且不食，而况于鳌哉”此《巷伯》之六章诗人之旨也．然悬之虹蜺之丝，挂之明月之钩，临东海而漂之三年之久，则腥秽略除，气味少变，以之享鳌，鳌其吐之乎斯虎也，其知之矣！

    Explanation

    22

    From that point on, his demeanor became dejected, and he knew that the time of his death was approaching. Whenever he spoke of this to others, those who heard him all shuddered in fear.

    One day, in the morning, he hanged himself on a beam in the courtyard. His wife saw this in shock and tried to rescue him, but he was already dead. She shouted for the neighbors. Just as the people were gathering to look, suddenly a black tiger rushed through the door. Everyone scattered in terror. The tiger finally grabbed the corpse and left with it.

    Nonononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononononon

    23

    青州贾
    　　贾人有丙丁相善者，皆青州人也。约至长沙合资贸易，订以某日启行，会于某所．既而丙至，而丁不来．候之十日，丙谓丁爽约，心非之，遂独往。
    　　三年而丁至，时丙已饶于财，将卜归．乃迎谓丁曰：“来何暮也我且归矣。”丁深谢后期之罪，而不言后期之故，且曰：“君归我亦归耳。”丙问故，丁曰：“恐君道远孤行，或有不利，愿伴君以赎前愆也。”丙谢曰：“君勿尔。君千里远赴，必有所为。今不终朝而归，乃以我故也，我则累君。”丁固请同行，丙乃许，虽感之，亦复疑之，谓有故而稽迟者情也，无故而旋反者非情也，虽友生之谊笃，爽约之悔深，不宜至此，是必有异。而丁于道途之间、旅居之际，金兰之情、云霞之谊逾于往昔；又时道人生聚散之感、朋友离别之恨，使人凄然，如睹寒冰而听哀笛，对落月而闻断琴也．

    Explanation

    23

    Merchants from Qingzhou
    There were two merchants, C and D, who were good friends and both from Qingzhou. They agreed to go to Changsha to jointly engage in trade, planning to depart on a certain day and meet at a certain place. Afterward, C arrived, but D did not come. C waited for ten days. C thought that D had broken the agreement and was unhappy with it, and then went alone.

    Three years later, D arrived. At that time, C had become wealthy and was about to return home. C then went to greet D and said to him, "Why did you come so late? I am going to return now." D deeply apologized for being late but did not explain the reason for being late. He said, "You return, I will return too." C asked the reason. D said, "I am afraid that if you travel alone over such a long distance, it might be dangerous. I wish to accompany you to make up for my previous mistake." C thanked him and said, "You should not do that. You have traveled a thousand miles, you must have a purpose. Now returning in the morning, it is because of me. I am burdening you." D firmly requested to travel together. C then agreed, although he was grateful, he also suspected. He thought that being late due to a reason is understandable, but returning without a reason is not understandable. Although the friendship between friends is deep, the regret of breaking the agreement is profound, it is not appropriate to go this far. There must be something unusual. And during the journey and while staying in a temporary place, the deep friendship and noble sentiments exceeded those of the past. Also often talking about the feelings of gathering and scattering in life, and the sorrow of parting with friends, making people feel sad, like seeing cold ice and hearing a sad flute, or facing the setting moon and hearing a broken zither.

    24

    既至青州，丁距丙居近百里，邀丙三日后过其家，当相待．因执手歧途，恸哭言别．丙亦为之潸然．不知涕之何从也．
    　　三日往访，丁妻出见，抆泪而言曰：“先夫捐馆已近四年。其没也，在公南行之前夕，故不及讣。弥留之际，犹谆谆以失约于公为辞．昨梦至家，言公明日当来，宜鸡黍俟之。家以公方远行，未信，今果然矣。”
    　　丙闻大哭，命其子引至墓所，持尊酒而告之曰：“故人故人，已至此乎向犹谓君寒盟，不意已隔泉壤。而君不远千里，省我而同行，故人于某生死厚矣！形泯情亲，千古所仅。今酹酒故人之宅，能使猿鹤旧侣，更望颜色乎”言罢大恸，子亦踊哭。行道见之，无不陨涕．忽阴风刺骨，山叶惊飞，见丁于尘雾之中挥泪拱手，须臾而灭。

    Explanation

    24

    Having arrived in Qingzhou, Ding lived nearly 100 li away from Bing. Ding invited Bing to visit his home three days later, and they would meet there. At the parting way, they held hands, weeping bitterly and saying goodbye. Bing also shed tears. Not knowing where the tears came from.

    Three days later, Bing went to visit. Ding's wife came out to see him, wiping away her tears and saying, "My husband passed away nearly four years ago. He died the night before you left, so I didn't have time to inform you. On his deathbed, he repeatedly spoke of breaking an agreement with you as his final words. Last night in a dream, he came home and said you would come tomorrow, so we should prepare chicken and millet to await you. The family did not believe because you were far away on a journey, but now it has come true."

    Hearing this, Bing wept bitterly. He asked his son to lead him to the tomb, where he poured wine and said, "Old friend, old friend, have you really gone? I still thought you had broken your promise, but now I see that you have already crossed the divide. You traveled a thousand miles to visit me and accompany me, and the old friend's kindness to me is immense! Though the form is gone, the affection remains, rare in a thousand years. Today, I pour wine at the old friend's house, can we see the faces of our old companions like apes and cranes again?" After speaking, they wept bitterly, and Bing's son also jumped and cried. Those who saw it on the road could not help but shed tears. Suddenly, a cold wind pierced the bone, and the mountain leaves flew in alarm. They saw Ding in the dust and mist, waving tears and bowing, vanishing in an instant.

    25

    非非子曰：昔延陵季子挂剑徐君之墓，曰：“吾心已许之，岂以死而背吾心哉”君子曰：“延陵季子之于信也，其至矣乎，”然人之信于鬼，非鬼之信于人也．丁之于丙也．其信乎死矣，而三年至焉，不可谓不信也．斯鬼也，其诸古张元伯之流欤抑亦鬼之季札欤
    　　张将军
    　　圣人受命，河海安澜．百馀年来，啸聚之徒洗心革面，无复梗化。
    　　尝闻故老言：昔有明之季，有张将军者，逸其名．尝出海捕盔，驾大舟一，从健卒数人．自恃武勇，欲探虎穴．
    　　有少年书生，形仪稚饬，言有事他国，厚赂舟人，求附舟．将军故有令：附舟者以谍论，杀无赦。舟人利其金，私纳之．

    Explanation

    25

    Master Feifei said:
    "In ancient times, Master Yi of Yanling hung his sword on the tomb of Xu Jun, saying, 'My heart has already been pledged to you; how could I break my word even if I were to die?' The gentleman said, 'Master Yi of Yanling's integrity was indeed the highest.' However, a person's faith in ghosts is not the same as a ghost's faith in a person. As for Ding and Bing, their faith in each other was so strong that even after death, Ding returned to Bing's tomb after three years, which cannot be considered lacking in faith. Is this person a historical figure like Zhang Yuanbo or a legendary figure like Master Yi of Yanling?

    During the reign of a sage ruler, the rivers and seas were calm, bringing peace and stability. Over the past hundred years, former rebels have reformed and no longer cause trouble. I have heard from ancient sayings that in the late Ming dynasty, there was a general whose name is unknown. He once went out to sea to capture a helmet, commanding a large ship and several soldiers. Confident in his martial prowess, he wanted to venture into dangerous territory.

    There was a young scholar with a refined appearance who said he had business in another country and offered a generous bribe to the boatman, asking to join the expedition. The general had a strict rule: anyone who boarded the ship without permission was considered a spy and would be killed without mercy. The boatman, enticed by the bribe, privately allowed the scholar to board the ship."

    26

    行数日，将军闻香烟扑鼻，命索舟中，曰：“必有盗。”得书生，将置之刑。书生自陈非盗，欲之海外省父，无舟自达，故敢昧死来，惟将军仁恕．将军视其状貌不类盗，且怜其孝，赦而与之言。书生能作学问语、才语、仙佛语、农桑经济语，俳优谐谑语，出风入雅，吐史谈经，随事酬应，动中窾会．将军素长于文学，竟莫能屈，往往反为所难，大加叹服，自谓得书生晚也。
    　　一日，及捕盗之事，书生曰：“盗可服，不可捕也。盗能见将军，将军不能见盗。”将军不平，乃大言曰：“尔书生敲枯砚，翻蠹简，乌知将军之能乎专制一方，扬威千里，长鞭所指，遐陬恐慑，区区海盗，何足膏其斧刃哉”书生曰：“将军亦知海盗之能乎”将军曰：“海盗之能，解衔刀弢火，夜趁丛泊，猝闻捕诛，潜窜薮泽耳！”书生曰：“以某所闻，固不仅此。”将军问：“汝何以知之”书生笑曰：“以盗言盗，安得不知姑请试之．”将军愕然．

    Explanation

    26

    After traveling for several days, the General smelled a strong fragrance and ordered a search of the boat, saying, "There must be a thief." Upon finding the scholar, the General intended to punish him with death. The scholar confessed that he was not a thief, explaining that he wished to go to the overseas province to visit his father but had no boat to reach there, so he dared to come at the risk of his life, only because the General was merciful. The General saw that his appearance did not look like a thief and pitied his filial piety, pardoning him and speaking with him. The scholar could speak in the language of scholarship, talent, immortals, agriculture and economy, and comic humor, moving from the vulgar to the refined, reciting history, and discussing classics. He could respond appropriately to any situation, always hitting the mark. The General was always skilled in literature but was unable to refute him, often finding himself challenged by the scholar, and greatly admired and respected him, saying that he had met the scholar too late.

    One day, when discussing the matter of catching thieves, the scholar said, "Thieves can be subdued, but cannot be caught. Thieves can see the General, but the General cannot see thieves." The General was not satisfied and said loudly, "You scholar, pounding old inkstones, flipping through worm-eaten scrolls, how could you know the General's abilities? Ruling a region, displaying power over a thousand miles, wherever the long whip points, distant corners tremble in fear. A mere pirate is not worth the effort to cut with the axe." The scholar replied, "Does the General also know the abilities of pirates?" The General said, "The abilities of pirates, they can wield knives and torches, sneak into crowded harbors at night, and upon hearing of a sudden capture, they can hide in marshes and swamps!" The scholar said, "According to what I have heard, it is not just this." The General asked, "How do you know?" The scholar replied with a smile, "To talk about thieves with thieves, how could I not know? Let's try it." The General was surprised.

    27

    时繁星丽空，海波碎月，万里无片帆只舶．书生取筚荜篥，自船头吹之，不数声，小舟千百悉自波中涌出，明炬雪刀，须臾环集．将军失色．书生笑曰：“盗不可捕也。虽然，为国供职，自应尔尔。吾辈岂得犯将军聊与将军戏耳，将军无恐。”复吹荜篥数声，大呼曰：“将军珍重，某去矣！”书生及小舟皆不见．将军亟命回舟，丧魄者累日，自是不复捕盗．
    　　方比部
    　　京师正阳门内关帝庙，最灵显。乾隆丙午，方比部体入都应北闱乡试，诣庙拈神筊，卜文战利钝。筊语云：
    　　“常羡人间万户侯，只知骑马胜骑牛。

    Explanation

    27

    At the time, the stars were bright in the sky, and the moonlight broke on the waves, with no sail or ship visible for miles. The scholar took a bamboo flute and played it from the bow of the boat. After a few notes, thousands of small boats emerged from the waves, each equipped with bright torches and sharp knives, gathering around in an instant. The general turned pale. The scholar smiled and said, "Thieves cannot be captured. Although, serving the country is our duty, and this is how it should be. We cannot offend the general; we are just playing with the general. Do not be afraid." He then blew the flute a few more times and shouted loudly, "General, take care, I am leaving now!" The scholar and the small boats disappeared. The general immediately ordered the boats to return, and the frightened ones remained so for several days. From then on, the general no longer pursued the capture of thieves.

    方比部

    The Guan Yu Temple inside the Zhengyang Gate of the capital city was the most renowned and powerful. During the reign of Emperor Qianlong, in the year of Bingwu (1766), Fang Bibo entered the capital to take the northern metropolitan examination. He went to the temple and picked up the divine calabash, divining the success or failure of his literary battle. The words of the calabash said:

    "Often I envy the great lords of the world, but I only know that riding a horse is better than riding a cow."

    28

    今朝马上看山色，争似骑牛得自由。”
    　　盖汤临川《牡丹亭》传奇中诗也。佥谓神语太廓，与科名事无涉．已而揭晓，方获售，列名第十八，十九名乃牛姓人也。始悟骑牛之说。
    　　——友人彭坦斋云．
    　　樊黑黑
    　　有屠者娶一妇，貌奇丑，蓬发历齿，睇鼻深目，面颟顸而黑色，肩高于项，左后耸而右前垂，腹睬大如瓜，腰以下肉肬坟起者三四寸，足复蹙行，步蹒跚。颇好涂饰。见者莫不辟易，而屠者爱之不啻毛嫱，郑袖也．
    　　有戏问之者，曰：“何子钟情之深也”屠者曰：“吾每夜于俙帷中，微灯闪烁之际，则殊见为丽人，蛾眉巧笑，鬓颊多姿，令人猿马大动．既与合体，并觉纤腰一握，肌理细腻，两股之间有香气袭袭扑人，不禁神骨之俱解也．怪以问妇，妇亦不自知．间或持烛就照之，即亦无异其本形，而去烛则复如是。以是爱之而忘其丑。”闻者不信，传为笑噱，群谓天下固有如是之溺于淫者，面复饰此说以诳人也．

    Explanation

    28

    "Riding a horse to view the mountain scenery today, how can it compare to the freedom of riding a cow?" This is a poem from the Peony Pavilion by Tang Xianzu, a playwright from the Ming dynasty. Everyone said that the words were too vague and had nothing to do with passing the exams. However, when the results were announced, the person finally passed, ranking 18th, with the 19th place holder being someone with the surname Niu (牛). Only then did he understand the meaning of the line about riding a cow.
    — A friend, Peng Tanzhai, said this.
    Fen Heihei
    There was a butcher who married a woman who was extremely ugly, with disheveled hair, protruding teeth, a squinting nose, deep-set eyes, a broad and dark face, shoulders higher than her neck, a hump on her left back and a drooping right front, a belly as large as a melon, and a protruding area of flesh below her waist, making her walk with difficulty. She liked to put on makeup. Everyone who saw her would turn away in disgust, but the butcher loved her as if she were the beautiful women Maoqiang and Zhengxiu.
    Someone jokingly asked him, "Why are you so deeply in love?" The butcher replied, "Every night in the dimly lit room, she appears to me as a beautiful woman, with delicate eyebrows and a charming smile, and her cheeks are full of grace, which makes me feel a strong desire. When we are together, I feel her slender waist, her smooth and delicate skin, and the fragrant scent between her legs, which makes me completely overcome with desire. I asked her about it, but she didn't know either. Sometimes when I hold a candle to look at her, she looks no different from her usual appearance, but without the candle, she looks like the beautiful woman again. Because of this, I love her and forget her ugliness." Those who heard this didn't believe it, and it became a joke. People said there must be someone in the world who is so obsessed with lust, and they fabricated this story to deceive others.

    29

    屠者无以明其言，大恚愤，乃日引乡里诸恶少入其室，令历试之．果如所谓．于是欲淫其妻者故言不信，屠者便令与宿焉．所交几遍一邑，不啻名娼矣。
    　　一日方寝，有人自床头谓之曰：“尔家合为娼，惧无以致客，故吾为尔妇易形．吾乃樊黑黑也，今去矣。”言讫，寂无所见．
    　　而视其帷中之妇，丑态毕露矣。屠者憎其形，一夜三四起，不能寐。久之，遂别榻焉。向时往来其家者，至是皆绝迹。
    　　非非子曰：美恶之无定也久矣，矧屠贾恶少之目哉苟眉下不嵌慧珠，其不看丹成碧也几希矣，何必易形不然，登徒子之好色又何以称焉

    Explanation

    29

    The butcher could not prove his words and became greatly enraged. He then daily brought various wicked youths from the neighborhood into his house and made them test it. It turned out as he had claimed. Therefore, those who wanted to rape his wife began to doubt his words, and the butcher ordered them to spend the night with her. Those who had dealings with her were nearly everyone in the town, and she was no less than a famous prostitute.

    One day, as they were about to sleep, someone from the bedhead said to him, "Your family is destined to be prostitutes. Afraid that there would be no way to attract customers, I have changed the form of your wife. I am Fan Heihei. Now I am leaving." After speaking, there was no one to be seen.

    Looking at the woman in the curtain, her ugly appearance was fully revealed. The butcher hated her appearance and got up three or four times a night, unable to sleep. After a long time, he finally slept in a separate bed. Those who had previously come to his house now all disappeared.

    Feifeizi said, "The instability of beauty and ugliness has been long recognized. How much more so for the eyes of butchers, traders, and wicked youths! If there is no wisdom embedded in their brows, how few are those who do not see red as green! Why bother to change form? Otherwise, how could the lecherous desires of a man like Deng Tusi be explained?"

    30

    谭襄敏夫人
    　　宜黄谭襄敏公纶夫人某氏．初配于李氏。于归之日遗矢轿中，臭不可迩．李氏丑之，即反诸其母家，与绝婚焉。由是乡里闻之。无肯委禽者．时襄敏贫而未娶，夫人之父使人喻意，愿不索其聘，以女归之．遂纳为妇．夫人既淑且慧，不类愚妪．叩其遗矢之故，终不肯言。
    　　后襄敏登嘉靖甲辰进士，历官太子少保、兵部尚书．夫人以事召至宫中，出而笑谓襄敏口：“数十年之梦，今日始醒矣向嫁李氏日，中途假寐，梦二女使如内家妆束，导吾至一处，宫阙巍焕。历门闼数重，忽觉腹涨，欲如厕。女使引至一室，因据红桶而遗，觉而秽物汙襟矣．心甚耻恨，然知所梦之必非无故，姑隐忍待之．向所以不告人者，惧人有谓我饰词而包其羞也．今至宫中，悉符所见，既而登溷，亦俨然故处。使吾无当日之梦，不能有今日之事．然无今日之事，又安得有当日之梦哉月下老弄人，何狡狯也！”襄敏噱然。

    Explanation

    30

    Tan Xiangmin's Wife

    The wife of Tan Xiangmin, a man from Yihuang, Jiangxi, was originally married to a man surnamed Li. On the day of her wedding, she left feces in the sedan chair, and the smell was unbearable. Li found it ugly and immediately returned her to her mother's family, severing the marriage. As a result, no one in the village was willing to give a dowry. At the time, Tan Xiangmin was poor and unmarried. His father-in-law, however, sent a messenger to convey his intention to give his daughter to Tan Xiangmin without demanding a dowry. Tan Xiangmin accepted her as his wife. The wife was both virtuous and wise, unlike a foolish old woman. When asked about the reason for leaving feces, she finally refused to speak.

    Later, Tan Xiangmin passed the imperial examination in the Jia Chen year of the Jiajing era and held high-ranking positions such as Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince and Minister of War. The wife was summoned to the palace for an event. Upon leaving, she smiled and told Tan Xiangmin, "A dream spanning several decades, today it finally awakens. When I was married to Li, on the way, I took a nap and dreamed of two maids dressed in imperial attire, leading me to a place with towering and splendid palaces. We passed through several gates, and suddenly I felt bloated and wanted to use the restroom. The maids led me to a room, and I sat on a red bucket and left feces. I woke up and found the filthy substance on my clothes. I felt very ashamed and resentful, but I knew that the dream must have a reason, so I endured and waited. I did not tell anyone before because I feared that people would say I was fabricating words to cover up my shame. Now that I am in the palace, everything matches what I saw. When I used the restroom, it was also clearly the same place. If I had not had that dream, I would not have this event today. But without this event, how could there have been that dream? The matchmaker under the moon is playing tricks, how cunning!" Tan Xiangmin laughed.

    31

    蕊宫仙史
    　　乾隆癸卯春，金溪杨孝廉英甫为扶鸾之戏．有女仙降坛，署曰“蕊宫仙史”，自叙为宋祥符间人，赍恨早逝，游于阆风之苑，获遘上元夫人，命居蕊珠宫，掌玉女名箓，云云．为诗词，抄笔立就，凄艳绝伦。叩其生时事迹，终不肯言．固请再三辄书曰：“噫！”篆烟灯穗中，隐隐有弹泪声．继有黄索水者至，亦女仙也，于仙史为中表姐妹，并有文藻，遂杂书仙史闺中轶事敷十条，皆隽异可喜．予从兄木虚手录成帙，惜不尽记忆，今纪其略云：

    Explanation

    31

    The Immortal Historian of the Rui Palace
    In the spring of the year of the White Earth Hare (癸卯) during the Qianlong era, Yang Yingfu, a scholar from Jinxī, performed a divination ritual with the use of a "luán" (鸾). A female deity descended to the altar, inscribing her name as "Rui Palace Immortal Historian" (蕊宫仙史). She recounted that she was a person from the Xiangfu era (祥符间) of the Song dynasty, who died with unresolved sorrow and now resides in the Liangfeng Garden (阆风之苑). She encountered the Lady of the Upper Yuan (上元夫人) and was assigned to the Rui Palace (蕊珠宫) to oversee the Register of Jade Maidens (玉女名箓). She composed poetry and prose, writing them down with a brush in an instant, creating works of exquisite beauty and melancholy. When asked about her earthly life, she refused to speak. After repeated pleas, she wrote: "Ah!" The curling incense smoke from the lamp wick seemed to carry the faint sound of tears being shed. Soon after, another female deity named Yellow Sowater (黄索水) arrived. She was also a deity and a cousin of the Rui Palace Immortal Historian. She shared her literary talents and wrote down ten anecdotes about the Immortal Historian's life in the celestial realm, all of which were elegant, strange, and delightful. My elder brother, Muxu, recorded these stories by hand, though he could not remember all of them. I now recount the highlights.

    32

    仙史姓薛氏，名琼枝．湘潭人．年十七，才艳绝世。随父某守杭州，遂家焉。所居曰“问花楼”，俯临西湖，云树烟波，凭槛可接。性爱兰，手植千百本。衣袖裙衩，皆喜绣之．或画为册卷，花叶左右题句殆遍。尝谓人曰：“此花逸韵幽香，自是我辈后身，当倍加珍护，毋令与众芳伍也。”阁中置书数百函，竟日靓妆，焚香展对。
    　　风日清美，辄命画舫造万花丛中，叶赏忘倦。既恐有踪迹者，遂于清夜易装，紫衣乌帽，乘白雪驹，侍女数十人，皆绿衫短剑，累骑从行。于时芙蓉秋放，笙管暮停，镜水澄鲜，佳月流素。徙倚湖亭，自制新曲，联袂歌之，声振林樾，鸥鹭惊翔。兴酣，更拨佩剑起舞，陆离顿挫，与歌声相应．于是剑光月光，花光水光，交相映发，湖中—草—木，皆有歌舞之态。万舟如蚁，集观亭外，寂然无哗。翌日，争传以为真仙下临，皆莫知其为太守女也。

    Explanation

    32

    The Celestial Historian, surnamed Xue and named Qiong Zhi, was from Xiangtan. At the age of seventeen, she possessed extraordinary beauty and talent. She followed her father, who was stationed in Hangzhou, and settled there. Her residence was called "Flower Inquiry Tower," which overlooked West Lake, with its clouds, trees, and misty waters visible from the railing. She had a fondness for orchids and planted thousands of them herself. Her sleeves and skirts were adorned with embroidered orchids, and she sometimes painted flowers and leaves in albums, writing poems around them. She once told others, "This flower has a unique and subtle fragrance, which is the essence of my being. It should be cherished and protected, not allowed to be associated with common flowers." Her study contained hundreds of volumes of books, and she spent the entire day in elegant attire, burning incense and reading.

    On clear and beautiful days, she would order a painted boat to navigate among the myriad flowers, enjoying the leaves without tiring. Fearing that there might be too many witnesses, she changed her attire at night, wearing a purple robe and a black hat, riding a white horse, with dozens of attendants dressed in green shirts and short swords, following her in succession. At that time, the lotus flowers were blooming in autumn, the music had stopped at dusk, the lake water was clear and fresh, and the beautiful moon cast its light. She would linger by the lakeside pavilion, composing new songs, and singing them together with her attendants, the sound echoing through the forest. The birds were startled and flew away. In a state of high spirits, she would draw her sword and dance, her movements dynamic and varied, matching the rhythm of the songs. Thus, the light from the sword, the moon, the flowers, and the water interplayed, and everything in the lake—grass and trees—had the appearance of dancing and singing. Thousands of boats gathered around the pavilion, silent and orderly. The next day, people spread the story, believing she was a true immortal descending. None knew that she was the governor's daughter.

    33

    久之，从湖上得画卷一，旁有题句云：
    　　“梦里胡山是也非，向人杨柳自依依。
    　　六桥日暮花成雪，肠断碧油何处归”
    　　惘然神伤，遂不复出。每当疏雨垂帘，落英飘砌，对镜自语，泣下沾襟。疾且笃，强起索笔，自写簪花小影，旋即毁去。更为仙装，倒执玉如意一柄，侍儿旁立，捧胆瓶插未开牡丹一枝。凝视良久，一恸而绝．
    　　著有《问花小稿》四卷，今无传本。降坛诗甚多，众尤爱其绝句。《怀湘君》云：
    　　“数行征雁起平沙，暮雨江寒杜若花。
    　　欲拨空舲迎帝子，湿云封处竹枝斜。”
    　　《答黄素水》云：

    Explanation

    33

    After a long time, she received a painting scroll from the lakeside, with a poem written beside it:
    　　"In dreams, the Hu mountains are both real and unreal, willows by the waterbank sway gently.
    　　At sunset over the Six Bridges, the flowers turn into snow, heartbroken, where does the green oil return?"
    　　Deeply saddened and dispirited, she never went out again. Whenever light rain fell and the curtains hung down, petals drifted onto the steps, she would talk to herself in front of a mirror, tears wetting her sleeves. Though seriously ill, she forced herself to sit up and ask for a writing tool, writing a small portrait of herself with a hairpin, and immediately destroyed it. She dressed in celestial attire, holding a jade wish-granting scepter upside down, with a servant standing beside her, holding a gourd-shaped vase with an unopened peony branch. She gazed for a long time, then died from a great sorrow.
    　　She wrote four volumes of Questioning Flowers, now there are no surviving copies. She wrote many poems on the altar, the audience especially loved her couplets. Longing for the Lady of Xiang:
    　　"Several lines of migratory geese rise from the flat sand, evening rain over a cold river with doruohua.
    　　Wanting to paddle an empty boat to welcome the emperor's son, wet clouds obscure the view, causing bamboo branches to droop."
    　　Responding to Huang Sushui:
    　　"Several lines of migratory geese rise from the flat sand, evening rain over a cold river with doruohua.
    　　Wanting to paddle an empty boat to welcome the emperor's son, wet clouds obscure the view, causing bamboo branches to droop."

    34

    “归真犹许住蓬莱，回首前尘亦可哀。
    　　莫问问花楼外树，六朝金粉已成灰。”
    　　又有“片云同我坠，明月向谁多春日媚杨柳，野风香菜花”之句。仙乎仙乎！
    　　——此篇得于吴君兰雪，余绝爱之，并录于此。
    　　耳食录卷二
    　　刘秋崖
    　　临川刘秋崖先生，旷达士也。冬夜读书甚勤，常忘寝。邻有少扫，亦夜纺不辍，声相闻也。
    　　一夕漏二下，闻窗外窸窣有声响。于时淡月微明，破窗窥之，见一妇人傍徨四顾，手持一物，似欲藏置、恐人窃见者，屡置而屡易其处，卒置槁稻中而去。秋崖烛得之，乃一麻绳，长二尺许，腥秽触鼻。意必缢鬼物也，入室闭户，以绳压书下，静以待之。
    　　已闻邻归辍纺而叹，叹不已，复泣。穴壁张其状，则见缢鬼跽妇前，再拜祈求，百态怂恿。妇睨视数四，遂解腰带欲自经。缢鬼喜极踊跃，急自牖飞出。妇则仍结其带，有踌躇不行之状。秋崖知鬼觅绳也，无绳必不能为厉，遂不呼救，而还坐读书。

    Explanation

    34

    "Returning to one's true nature still allows residence in Penglai, looking back on the past is also sorrowful.
    Do not ask about the trees outside the flower-laden pavilion, the opulence of the Six Dynasties has turned to ashes."
    There are also lines such as "a piece of cloud falls with me, to whom does the bright moon shine more? In spring, the willows are charming, the wild wind carries the fragrance of wildflowers." Divine indeed, divine indeed!
    — This piece was obtained from Mr. Wu Lansnow, I deeply love it and have recorded it here.
    Records of Heard Stories, Volume Two
    Liu Qiuya
    Mr. Liu Qiuya of Linchuan is a broad-minded and open-hearted scholar. He reads diligently on winter nights, often forgetting to sleep. A neighbor has a young woman who also weaves at night without stopping, their sounds can be heard from each other.
    One night, when the water clock struck two, he heard a rustling sound outside the window. At that time, the dim moon was slightly bright, so he broke the window to look. He saw a woman wandering and looking all around, holding an object, as if wanting to hide it, afraid that someone might secretly see it. She repeatedly placed it and repeatedly changed its location, finally placing it among the withered rice stalks and leaving. Mr. Qiu崖 lit the candle and found it, it was a hemp rope, about two feet long, the smell of decay and filth was pungent. He must be an object belonging to a hanged ghost, so he entered the room and closed the door, placed the rope under the books, and waited quietly.
    He already heard the neighbor return and stop weaving, continued to sigh, then cried. Peering through a hole in the wall to see the situation, he saw the hanged ghost kneeling in front of the woman, kneeling and praying, using various gestures to persuade. The woman glanced several times, then untied her belt and wanted to hang herself. The hanged ghost was so delighted that it leaped up, quickly flew out through the window. The woman then tied her belt again, showing a hesitant and indecisive posture. Mr. Qiu崖 knew the ghost was looking for a rope, without a rope, it cannot cause harm, so he did not call for help and continued to sit and read.

    35

    有顷，闻鬼款其门，秋崖叱曰：“尔妇人，我孤客，门岂可启乎尔能入则入。”鬼曰：“处士命我入，我入矣。”则已入。曰：“适亡一物，知处士藏之。幸以见还。”秋崖曰：“尔物在某书下，尔能取则取。”鬼曰：“不敢也。”曰：“然则去耳！”
    　　鬼曰：“乞处土去其书，不然，恐处士且惊。”秋崖笑曰：“试为之，看吾惊否。”鬼乃喷血满面，散发至腰，舌长尺馀，或笑或哭。秋崖曰：“此尔本来面目耳，何足畏！技止此乎”鬼又缩舌结发，幻为好女，夭袅而前，示以淫媚之态。秋崖略不动。
    　　鬼乃跪拜而哀恳，秋崖问：“欲得绳何为”曰：“藉此以求代，庶可转生。无此则永沈泉壤。幸处士怜之”秋崖曰：“若是，则相代无已时也。吾安肯为死者之生，使生者死乎？冥间创法者何人执法者何吏乃使生者有不测之灾，而鬼亦受无穷之虐也，庸可令乎吾当作书告冥司，论其理，破其例，使生尔。”鬼曰：“如是则幸甚，不敢复求代矣！”

    Explanation

    35

    Soon, a knock was heard at the door, and the ghost called out. Qiu Ya shouted, "You, woman, and I, a lone traveler, how can the door be opened? If you can enter, then enter." The ghost replied, "The scholar commands me to enter, so I have entered." Then, the ghost had already entered. Qiu Ya said, "I have just lost something, and I know the scholar has it. Please return it to me." Qiu Ya replied, "Your item is under a certain book; if you can take it, then take it." The ghost said, "I dare not." Qiu Ya said, "Then leave!"

    The ghost said, "Please remove the book, scholar, or I fear you will be startled." Qiu Ya laughed and said, "Try it, and see if I am startled." The ghost then spat blood all over its face, let its hair down to its waist, and extended its tongue to a foot in length, laughing and crying alternately. Qiu Ya said, "This is your true form, what is there to fear? Is this all you can do?" The ghost then retracted its tongue, tied its hair up, and transformed into a beautiful woman, swaying forward and displaying a seductive and alluring demeanor. Qiu Ya remained completely unmoved.

    The ghost then knelt and pleaded earnestly. Qiu Ya asked, "What do you want the rope for?" The ghost replied, "I need it to be substituted, hoping to be reborn. Without it, I will remain in the underworld forever. Please show mercy, scholar." Qiu Ya said, "If so, then the substitution will never end. How could I allow the dead to live at the expense of the living? Who created the laws of the underworld, and who enforces them? Why do they make the living suffer unpredictable disasters, while the ghosts also endure endless torment? How can this be allowed? I will write to the underworld, argue the case, and break the rules, so that you may live." The ghost said, "If so, then that would be fortunate indeed, and I will no longer ask to be substituted!"

    36

    秋崖取朱笔作书讫，付之。鬼曰：“乞焚之，乃能持。”焚之而书在鬼手，复乞绳，因去其书，绳亦在鬼手，乃欣喜拜谢而去。还视邻妇，亦无恙。
    　　煤夫
    　　祟仁三十九都，有山产煤。村民穴而取之，地道数里。洞口为大棚，以防霖雨。洞内每十数步支以木桩，以防崩塌。然葬其中者不少。
    　　一日，取煤者闻洞壁中人语曰：“速出我，迟则死矣。”佥谓遇鬼，相顾骇愕。有雄于胆者应之曰：“尔死此，数也，毋为厉出当祭尔。”壁中人又曰：“我某村某人，固未死。”其姓名，则众中某甲之父、三年前压于山中者也，其家久延僧道招魂追荐矣，于是众益骇，曰：“今日竟遇活鬼耶尔子在此，勿得作怪！”相与急奔。
    　　壁中人闻之，亟呼某甲名曰：“尔既在此，忍不救父耶”甲大号，因寻其声所在，挥锄开土。壁既穿，一人蛇行而出。甲携至洞口，呼众；众犹不信，调鬼当畏日，急拆棚露日以验之，果甲父也。

    Explanation

    36

    Qiu Ya took a red pen to write, and after finishing, handed it to the ghost. The ghost said, "Please burn it, and then I can hold it." After burning the document, it was in the ghost's hand. The ghost then asked for a rope, and with it, took the document away. The rope was also in the ghost's hand, and the ghost left, happily bowing and thanking before departing. When Qiu Ya returned to look at the neighbor's wife, she was also unharmed.

    Coal Miner

    In the 39th district of Suiren, there is a mountain that produces coal. Villagers dig tunnels to extract it, and these tunnels extend for several miles. The cave entrances are covered with large canopies to prevent heavy rain. Inside the caves, every ten or so steps are supported by wooden stakes to prevent collapses. However, many have been buried there.

    One day, while extracting coal, a miner heard a voice from the wall say, "Quickly, get me out, or I will die!" All thought they had encountered a ghost and looked at each other in shock and fear. Someone brave enough responded, "If you died here, it was fate, and do not cause harm when you leave; you should be honored with a proper burial." The voice from the wall replied, "I am someone from a certain village, and I am definitely not dead." The name mentioned was that of a man in the crowd, whose father had been buried in the mountain three years ago. His family had long invited monks and Taoists to perform rituals to summon his soul and offer sacrifices. Thus, the crowd was even more frightened, saying, "Today, we have encountered a living ghost! Your son is here, do not cause any trouble!" They ran away together in panic.

    The voice from the wall heard this and immediately called out the name of the man, saying, "Since you are here, how can you not save your father?" The man cried out loudly and searched for the location of the voice. He used a hoe to dig through the wall. Once the wall was broken, a person emerged, crawling like a snake. The man carried him to the cave entrance and called out to the others. Despite their doubts, they believed that ghosts feared the sun, so they quickly removed the canopy to expose the sun for verification. It turned out to be the father of Jia.

    37

    叩其不死之故，曰：“山塌之日，我适立于支木之下，得不压。然前壅不能出，自分长埋矣，悲泣不已。倦而倚木成熟睡，适闻挥锄声而觉，故相呼耳。”既闻已历三年，其人乃言：“吾如片刻也。”遂与子俱归。后三十馀年始死。
    　　——闻诸巨材吴君云。
    　　钱氏女
    　　郭氏子，聘钱氏女。亲迎之日，鱼轩至门，得二女自轩中出，声音、笑貌，服饰无纤毫差异，彼此互相争辨。
    　　其家惊怪，亟召其母家。既至，二女皆泣，就母怀与兄弟通款，皆曰：“请除妖妄。”母家亦竟莫能辨。因令各诉母家事，纤悉皆知。其母曰：“吾女左足跟有小黑点。”就验，则皆有之。复各验左臂红印，印亦宛然。以于手足箕斗，无不符契。或私谓曰：“是妖怪所为，形声之间何难尽肖彼必为淫媚而来。若于床第间试之，则或庄或谑，或淫或贞，真伪立见矣。”郭氏子挟二女就寝，观其所为，亦竟莫能辨。

    Explanation

    37

    Knocking on the door to inquire about the reason for his survival, he replied, "On the day the mountain collapsed, I happened to stand under the supporting beam and was not crushed. However, the front was blocked, and I could not get out, so I thought I would be buried alive forever and cried incessantly. I grew tired and leaned against the wood, falling into a deep sleep, and woke up when I heard the sound of a hoe, so I called out to him." After hearing that three years had passed, the person said, "It felt like a moment to me." So he and I returned together. He died more than thirty years later.

    — Heard from the great scholar Mr. Wu.

    The daughter of the Qian family
    The son of the Guo family, betrothed to the daughter of the Qian family. On the day of the wedding, the carriage arrived at the door, and two women emerged from the carriage. Their voices, smiles, and attire were identical, and they argued with each other. The family was surprised and quickly summoned the bride's family. When they arrived, the two women wept, embraced their mother, and spoke with their brothers, all saying, "Please remove this evil deception." The bride's family could not determine the truth either. So they asked each to recount the events in their mother's family, and they knew every detail. The mother said, "My daughter has a small black spot on the heel of her left foot." Upon examination, both had it. They also examined the red mark on their left arms, and it was the same. By comparing the patterns on their hands and feet, there was no discrepancy. Someone privately said, "This must be the work of a demon. It's not difficult to perfectly mimic the form and voice. They must have come to seduce. If you test them in the bedroom, you will see whether they are serious, joking, lewd, or chaste, and the truth will be revealed." The son of the Guo family took the two women to bed, observed their behavior, but still could not determine the truth.

    38

    试验之法殆穷，母忽心设一策，命立机于地，约曰：“能超过者，为吾女，不能者，杀之。”四掣剑以俟。一女惶惑无策，涕泣自陈。一女闻言，即跃而过，因前砍之，应手而灭。盖深闺弱女，步履艰难，安能跃机而过哉？其跃者之非女明奂。此妖不及思，而为人所卖也。
    　　邻虎
    　　某贵人微时，有邻人猎南山，柙二虎而归，一牝一牡。饲之既久，虎甚驯。开柙出之，昂头扇尾，若猫犬然。
    　　有私议者曰：“虎也至暴，奈何狎之宜早为之所。”意劝邻人杀之也。牝虎遂人立而言曰：“将以我为噬公者耶则何为迟迟面与猫犬辈伍也？”时观者如堵，贵人亦在。虽异之，而各不畏。

    Explanation

    38

    The Methods Were Nearly Exhausted, the Mother Suddenly Devised a Plan

    The methods were nearly exhausted, when the mother suddenly devised a plan in her mind. She commanded that a frame be set up on the ground, and stipulated: "Whoever can leap over it will be my daughter; whoever cannot will be killed." She held four swords in readiness. One daughter, frightened and without a plan, wept and pleaded. Another daughter, upon hearing this, immediately leaped over the frame, and stepped forward to strike, causing it to be extinguished instantly. How could a delicate woman from the inner chambers, whose steps are difficult, possibly leap over the frame? It is clear that the one who leaped is not a woman. This demon did not have time to think, and thus was deceived by others.

    The Neighbor's Tiger

    When a certain nobleman was not yet prominent, a neighbor hunted in the south mountain and returned with two tigers in cages, one male and one female. After feeding them for a long time, the tigers became very tame. When the cage was opened and they were let out, they held their heads high and fanned their tails, like cats and dogs.

    Those who privately commented said: "Tigers are extremely fierce; how can we be so familiar with them? It is advisable to deal with them early." They intended to persuade the neighbor to kill them. The female tiger then stood upright and said: "Will you consider me as one who will bite you? Why then are you delaying and treating me like a cat or dog?" At the time, the spectators were like a wall, and the nobleman was also present. Although they were surprised, they did not fear.

    39

    虎于是如人行，历抱数人起，而复置之，若欲试其体之重轻者。诸人皆辟易而奔。复将抱贵人，贵人乃直其体，正其首，定神默虑，瞋目而视虎。虎以爪微触贵人手，贵人不动，又微触其喉，复不动。虎相视良久，遂咆哮而奔。牡虎随之，邻人追之莫及。
    　　其为虎所抱数人，旬日内皆死。贵人后官至尚书。
    　　胡好好
    　　天津何生，有别业临河干，距所居三里许。妻张氏，美而妬。生素狎邪，而惧为张觉，每托故居别业，乃得一宿妖妓，再纳淫妪，而张不知也。
    　　候值清明，天气晴朗，花柳烂漫撩人。生河上翱翔，逢一少女，淡妆素服，袅娜而前。生注盼略不移睛，女亦目成。生挑之曰：“何处丽人独行何处”女低应曰：“觅渡。”生曰：“地非秦淮，人如桃叶，渡江不楫，乌能不畏横流哉”女笑曰：“既已知之，不解迎接，饶舌伺为”生狂喜曰：“迎接久矣，乞降芳趾。”女以目示生，生会意，乃前行，女遥遥相从，途人莫之识。

    Explanation

    39

    The tiger then behaved like a person, lifting and placing several people, as if testing their weight. All the others scattered in fear and ran away. It then tried to lift the nobleman, but he stood firm, straightened his body, fixed his gaze, and calmly considered the situation, glaring at the tiger. The tiger gently touched the nobleman's hand with its paw, but he did not move. It then lightly touched his throat, and still, he remained still. The tiger stared at him for a long time before roaring and running away. A male tiger followed, and the neighbors tried to catch it but failed.

    Those who were lifted by the tiger all died within ten days. The nobleman later rose to the position of Minister of Works.

    Tr. "The Good Good Hu"

    In Tianjin, there was a man named He who had a separate residence near the riverbank, about three miles from his main home. His wife, Zhang, was beautiful but jealous. He was fond of women of loose morals and feared being discovered by his wife. He often pretended to be at his old home or his separate residence to meet prostitutes and courtesans, and his wife did not know about it.

    On a clear day during the Qingming Festival, the weather was sunny, and the flowers and willows were beautiful and inviting. He was wandering by the river and met a young woman dressed simply and elegantly, who walked gracefully towards him. He looked at her intently, and she also showed interest. He flirts with her, asking where such a beautiful woman is walking alone. She replies softly that she is looking for a ferry. He jokes that she is like the famous courtesan Tao Ye of Qinhuai, and that without a boat, she would be afraid of crossing the river. She laughs and says that since he knows, he should not keep talking and should just help her. He happily responds that he has been waiting to help her, and asks her to step down. She signals to him with her eyes, and he understands, leading the way while she follows at a distance, unnoticed by others.

    40

    既至别业，不瑕问姓氏，成欢而后言。女自云胡氏，名好好，新寡。夫族单门，邻童暴横。惧为所辱，将逃之母家。不意阻影津梁，复见诱于吉士，幸勿见委，虽列身妾媵，所不辞矣。生心畏妻妬，嗫嚅难言，不觉有负心之语。女频顑曰：“薄命之人，不自持慎，荡情再辱，亦复何憾？诚恨既觏君子，旋复弃损，遂如杨柳东西，客舟空绊，含冤茹叹，惭恧无穷，惟有赴长流以自洁耳！”言罢泪零，不胜悲悼。生不得已，告之故。女收泪言曰：“郎诚见采，是亦何难妾母家近在河西，一苇可杭。花月之宵，便与郎会，风雨之夕，便与郎离。以此地为王司空别业，宁有犊车麈尾之诮乎”生曰：“计亦甚善。第一水盈盈，屡使夭孙夜渡，奈罗襦之沾露何”女曰：“君勿忧。家有渔舟，少习水性，伺家众就寝后，便可击揖渡江矣。”

    Explanation

    40

    Having arrived at the secluded estate, there was no time to ask about names and surnames; they enjoyed each other's company before speaking. The woman introduced herself as Lady Hu, her given name was Hǎohǎo, and she was newly widowed. Her husband's family was poor and of low status, and the neighboring children were violent and unruly. Afraid of being insulted by them, she intended to flee to her mother's home. Unexpectantly, she was detained at the ferry crossing and then lured by the good gentleman. Please do not abandon me; even if I become a concubine, I would not hesitate. The man was worried about his wife's jealousy and hesitated to speak, unconsciously uttering words of betrayal. The woman sighed repeatedly and said, "A woman of ill fate, not self-restrained, indulged in emotions and suffered again, what is there to regret? Truly, I regret meeting a gentleman, only to be abandoned again. It's like a willow swaying east and west, an empty boat tied up. Suffering in silence, with endless shame, the only way to purify myself is to drown in the long river!" After she finished speaking, tears streamed down her face, and she could not bear the sorrow. The man had no choice but to tell her the reason. The woman wiped away her tears and said, "If you truly value me, what is there to be difficult about? My mother's home is near the western river, and a single reed can cross it. On moonlit nights, we can meet, and on stormy evenings, we can part. If we consider this place as the secluded estate of Grand Secretary Wang, would there be any criticism of a carriage and a dust brush?" The man replied, "The plan is quite good. Firstly, the water is deep, and it often requires a young boy to ferry us at night. How can we avoid the silk robe getting wet with dew?" The woman said, "Do not worry, sir. Our family has a fishing boat, and I have learned to swim. After the family has gone to bed, we can row across the river."

    41

    既而期会数月，殊无失约。生自清明之游，托以读书辞家长居别业，不复归卧张所。张亦不往，唯一介往还而已。
    　　久之，生恐张见疑，昼日归家，将图荐夕。遥见一少年书生，形容都丽。径入其室。生怪之，伏门外而察焉。闻妻迎谓书生曰：“胡郎今日来正好，吾正念尔也。”遂闻共入寝室，淫声媟语，殆不可听。生大怒，突入中门，求剑不得，求仆婢不得，乃厉声呼妻，问：“尔室何人？”时张方与书生交欢，猝闻生至，股栗不能出声，欲推起藏匿。书生坚抱张，不令脱，且淫且笑，神色自如。事终，仍抱张而卧，不令著衣裈。张窘极，因大呼“有贼”。书生亦大呼曰：“吾尔夫也，贼安在”

    Explanation

    41

    After a few months of meeting, there were no broken appointments. The young man, after his spring outing, claimed to be studying and thus stayed at a distant residence, no longer returning to sleep at Zhang's place. They only maintained minimal contact.

    After a long time, the young man feared that Zhang might suspect him, so he returned home during the day, planning to visit in the evening. Suddenly, he saw a young scholar, handsome and well-groomed, entering his room. The young man was surprised and hid outside to observe. He heard his wife welcoming the scholar and saying, "Hu Lang came today just in time; I was just thinking of you." Then he heard them entering the bedroom, with lascivious sounds and indecent words, which were almost unbearable to listen to. The young man became extremely angry and suddenly entered the inner gate. Unable to find a sword, he also could not find servants. He then loudly called out to his wife, asking, "Who is in your room?" At that moment, Zhang was being held by the scholar, who did not allow her to escape. The scholar continued his inappropriate behavior while laughing, maintaining a calm demeanor. After the deed was done, he still held Zhang and lay down, not allowing her to put on clothes. Zhang was in extreme distress and shouted, "There's a thief!" The scholar defended himself, saying, "I am your husband, where is the thief?"

    42

    时生已冲入寝室，启帷发被，将执书生面斩之。瞪视大惊，连曰“怪事”，第见抱妻而裸卧者，非书生，乃好好也。好好见生，回眸微笑，仍抱张不释手。张怖惧稍定，乃见书生化为丽女，转复惊骇。生瞪视呆立，半晌无言。倏忽之间，好好又变书生矣，对生而淫张。张羞惭无地，宛转娇啼。生知是妖怪所为，攘臂登床，从中繋之。书生舍张而抱生，张手足仍若束缚，略不能运。而生眩惑之际，视抱己者，则又好好也。谓生曰：“郎乃忘我，不记别业共枕时耶”遂以一手按生胸，一手褫生衣，与交欢焉。生初力挣，竟不得动，而为好好所拨，颇复不自持；既而力竭僵卧，侧身于二女之间，恍惚如梦，左抱右拥，转觉欢治，而怖怒之情顿消。

    Explanation

    42

    At that moment, the person rushed into the bedroom, lifted the curtain and opened the quilt, intending to seize and cut off the scholar's face. They stared in shock, exclaiming repeatedly, "Strange thing!" But they only saw the wife holding her husband and lying naked. It was not the scholar, but the wife (好好). When the wife saw the person, she smiled back and still held her husband tightly. The husband's fear slightly subsided, and then he saw the scholar transform into a beautiful woman. He became even more frightened. The person stared blankly and remained silent for a while. Suddenly, the wife transformed back into the scholar and engaged in sexual acts with the husband. The husband felt ashamed and cried softly. The person realized it was the work of a demon, raised his arm, climbed onto the bed, and restrained them. The scholar left the husband and embraced the person. The husband's limbs remained restrained, barely able to move. But when the person was confused, they saw that the one embracing them was the wife. She said to the person, "You have forgotten me, do you not remember when we shared a bed in another place?" Then she pressed the person's chest with one hand and removed their clothes with the other, engaging in sexual activity. The person initially struggled but could not move. But they were overcome by the wife and lost control. Eventually, they exhausted themselves and lay motionless, on their side between the two women. They felt as if in a dream, held by one side and embraced by the other, gradually feeling pleasure. Their fear and anger dissipated.

    43

    好好乃笑曰：“与君同寝者半载，与君妻同寝者亦半载。日夕之间，两地酬酢，曾无休暇。虽挹彼注此，于我无与，然本图合内外、博兼宠，以为同类光；今既交恶矣，尚何留渭我索性廉洁，不欲媚人而有所取。适所受于君者，愿仍还之君妻，吾事毕矣。”眨眼之际，复成书生，与张媾精。张无如何，唯有顺受。生亦倦惫己极，睨视面巳。夫妇相对，各有惭色。
    　　良久，书生整衣下床，鼓掌大笑，举手高揖曰：“吾去矣！”
    　　变为野狐，腾跃而出。遂不复至。
    　　初，生以清明之游而不返也。次日，书生造访其家，张见而悦之，三见而通焉。邻人咸闻而丑之，而生不知也。至是狐乃自泄其事。

    Explanation

    43

    Hao Hao laughed and said, "I shared a bed with you for half a year, and I also shared a bed with your wife for half a year. Day and night, I was engaged in social interactions in two different places, never having a moment of rest. Though I transferred things from one place to another, it had no effect on me.
