    夫人笑而许之，遂置酒祖行。召众花神至，告以意，则皆惘然有可怜之色。酒数巡，卢女抱琴面前曰：“本不妙于音律，先生远去，愿献一曲，代渭城之唱。”元称谢。卢女抚弦动操，为鼓《霓裳序》。不数声，梁尘欲飞，落花起舞，四座寂然无语。琴罢，夫人复谓众神曰：“诸卿妙技，各于今日试之。过此以往，则天上之曲，人间不可得闻矣。”于是崔茞奴理筝，宋伟吹笛，关小红奏琵琶，徐月华弹箜篌，檀扳土箫，更番迭奏。歌舞纷沓，大都丽娟绛树、飞鸾轻风之俦，尽欢而罢。明日遂行，夫人及花神各以花片为赆，元拜而受之。送之管春门外而返。

    Explanation

    80

    The lady smiled and agreed, then set up wine for a farewell feast. She summoned all the flower spirits and told them her intentions, and they all looked bewildered and pitiable. After several rounds of wine, Lu Nu held a qin in front of her and said, "I am not skilled in music, but since Sir is about to depart, I wish to present a piece of music as a substitute for the 'Wei Cheng' song." Yuan thanked her. Lu Nu played the strings and began the performance, playing the prelude of "The Rainbow Dress." After just a few notes, the dust on the beams seemed to fly, and the falling flowers danced; everyone in the room was silent. After the qin performance ended, the lady again addressed the flower spirits, saying, "All of you with your wonderful skills, please demonstrate them today. After this, the heavenly music will no longer be heard on earth." Thus, Cui Chai Nu tuned her zither, Song Wei played the flute, Guan Xiaohong played the pipa, Xu Yuehua played the konghou, and Tan Ban played the shawm, taking turns and playing in succession. Dancing and singing in a flurry, mostly like the beauties of Li Juan and Jiang Shu, the flying phoenixes and gentle winds, they enjoyed themselves and then finished. The next day, they departed, and the lady and the flower spirits each gave flower petals as gifts. Yuan bowed and accepted them. They sent him to the Guanchun Gate and then returned.

    81

    元既至家，妻子皆惊怪。亲旧闻之，咸来同讯，闻言莫不嗟异。明年，复往寻之，则武陵之旧路迷矣，悔恨不已。自是爱花愈笃，花前多作伤心语，益无意于人此。后数年，无疾而终。
    　　临终，谓其家人曰：“吾始以花生，终以花死。死而有知，魂魄犹应恋此也。身没之后，当以落花葬我。且吾以花隐，毋著我名，但题石曰“故长春苑催花使者元生之墓”。”葬之日，棺轻如蜕，识者以为尸解云。
    　　梅花美人
    　　如皋陈肖生嵩，以画名于时，尤长于梅。冷蕊蔬枝，嫩寒春晓，见者辄作孤山篱落之想。
    　　有金陵李某，僦居京师内城。斋壁红梅一轴，肖生笔也，一日仰卧观书，童子侍焉。忽有老翁出于画中，龙钟伛偻。俄而枝叶动摇，花蕊怒放，每一花现一美人，步虚而下。须臾，次第入花去，老人亦遽隐，画如故也。后凡三见，亦无他异。

    Explanation

    81

    Once Yuan returned home, his wife and children were all surprised and bewildered. His relatives and old friends, upon hearing of this, all came to inquire about it, and upon hearing the account, they could not help but sigh and marvel. The following year, he tried to seek out the place again, but the old path to Wuling had vanished, and he was filled with regret and sorrow. From then on, his love for flowers became even more profound, and he often spoke mournful words in the presence of flowers, growing increasingly detached from worldly affairs. After several years, he passed away without any illness.

    On his deathbed, he told his family, "I began my life because of flowers and ended it because of flowers. If I retain consciousness after death, my spirit should still be attached to this place. After my passing, I should be buried with fallen flowers. Moreover, I lived in seclusion because of my love for flowers, so do not inscribe my name. Simply inscribe the stone as 'The Tomb of Yuan Sheng, Former Flower-Calling Messenger of the Former Changchun Garden.'" On the day of his burial, the coffin was as light as a snake shedding its skin, and those who understood such things believed it was a case of spiritual transcendence.

    Plum Blossom Beauties

    Chen Xiaosheng from Rugao was renowned for his paintings during his time, especially for his depictions of plum blossoms. His works featured delicate buds and sparse branches, evoking a sense of early spring's chill and the solitude of a bamboo fence on a lonely mountain. There was a man named Li from Jinling who rented a house in the inner city of the capital. On the wall of his study hung a scroll of red plum blossoms painted by Chen Xiaosheng. One day, while lying on his back reading a book, a young servant waited upon him. Suddenly, an old man emerged from the painting, feeble and bent over. Soon, the branches and leaves began to move, and the flower petals burst open. Each flower revealed a beautiful woman, who descended gracefully. In a moment, they entered the flowers in order, and the old man quickly disappeared, leaving the painting as it was. Later, this happened three more times, and there were no other strange occurrences.

    82

    ——李言之肖生，肖生述之吴茗香，茗香述之余。
    　　非非子曰：梅之神也旧矣。高髻大袖，蜀阁凭栏，素服淡妆，罗浮对酒。不意丹青之妙更能化亿万美人身也。王丹麓有言：美人是花真身，花是美人小影。此其美人耶花耶？真身耶？小影耶是耶非耶余又乌能测之哉！
    　　耳食录卷四
    　　紫钗郎
    　　有冯生居郡城，郊外闲步。花木丛萃中，一宅双扉半掩，有美人倚门斜盼，如有所待。见生徐徐掩门，如不胜情。生怅然而归。
    　　次日复往，又见焉，遂低徊驻足，挑之以目。女低语曰：“蛱蝶亦恋花枝耶”生应曰：“蝶不恋花而更谁恋但未识花恋蝶否”女笑曰：“蝶既恋花，何不飞上梢头，栩栩何为”生遂入，面门遽掩。闲馆云虚，惟女独处。生问：“宅上无人乎”女曰：“吾有新妇，何谓无人”生笑问：“卿安有妇”女曰：“吾族纳婿，均谓之新妇，今卿是也。吾名紫钗郎，卿宜郎我，勿得卿我，我乃得卿卿。”生笑颔之。

    Explanation

    82

    ——Li Yan told Xiao Sheng, Xiao Sheng told Wu Mingxiang, Wu Mingxiang told me.
    Nononzi said: The spirit of the plum blossom has been ancient. With a high hairstyle and wide sleeves, she stands by the railing of a pavilion in Shu, dressed simply and lightly made up, drinking wine on the Luofu Mountain. Who would have thought that the wonders of painting could transform into countless beautiful human forms! Wang Danlu once said: The beauty of a woman is the true form of a flower, and the flower is a small reflection of the woman. Is she a woman or a flower? Is she the true form or the reflection? Is she right or wrong? How could I possibly know!
    Volume Four of Ershi Lu
    Purple Hairpin Man
    There was a man named Feng who lived in the county town. While taking a leisurely walk in the outskirts, he saw a house with two half-closed doors in a grove of flowers and trees. A beautiful woman stood by the door, looking sideways as if waiting for someone. Seeing Feng, she slowly closed the door, as if overcome with emotion. Feng returned home with a sense of longing.
    The next day, he went there again and saw her once more. He lingered, hesitating, and made eye contact with her. The woman whispered, "Does the butterfly also long for the flower branch?" Feng replied, "If the butterfly does not long for the flower, then who does it long for? But I do not know if the flower longs for the butterfly." The woman laughed and said, "If the butterfly longs for the flower, why not fly up to the branch and flutter about?" Feng then entered, and she quickly closed the door. The quiet hall was empty, and she was alone. Feng asked, "Is there no one in the house?" The woman said, "I have a new wife, so how can there be no one?" Feng smiled and asked, "How can you have a wife?" The woman said, "In our family, we take in a husband, and we all call him a new wife. You are that new wife now. My name is Purple Hairpin Man. You should call me husband, do not call me by my name, and I will call you 'my dear'." Feng smiled and nodded in agreement.

    83

    紫钗向壁曰：“新妇恶岑寂，兰奴车蒲奴可出侍。”俄有二青衣自壁中出，妩媚可观。生大惊，知其非人矣，疾趋欲遁。紫钗追捉其臂曰：“既为夫妇，不啻骨肉，何相弃之速也？”遂命青衣：“将酒来，与夫人压惊。”酒至，连酌奉生，每杯自饮其半，两颊盈盈然如桃花之冶艳矣。生初甚畏怖，至是心动，渐狎嫟之。紫钗复命青衣：“往请诸姊妹及魏姑姑来陪夫人花烛宴。”凡称新妇及夫人，皆谓生也。生亦戏自称曰“妾”。
    　　须臾，青衣报曰：“请姑至矣。”有自东壁出者，有自西壁出者，共四人，皆韶颜艳质。指生问曰：“此新贵人乎”乃自巾领下及袜履，一一审视，威敛袂向紫钗曰：“贺汝得佳妇！”生颇羞惭面赤，俨然如新妇之腼腆者。
    　　青衣又传：“魏家姑姑至。”则一美人自南壁出，年稍长，迎紫钗笑曰：“偷香贼乃敢延客，劳我远涉！”紫钗亦笑，问：“阿素何不教来”魏姑曰：“小蛮女累人难行，已命小婢将饼馅饵之矣。”

    Explanation

    83

    The purple hairpin spoke to the wall, saying, "The new wife dislikes silence; Lan Nu and Pu Nu can come out to serve." Soon, two green-clad attendants emerged from the wall, their beauty captivating. The protagonist was greatly startled, realizing they were not human, and quickly tried to flee. The purple hairpin caught his arm, saying, "Since we are husband and wife, no different from family, why abandon me so quickly?" She then ordered the attendants, "Bring wine to calm the lady's nerves." When the wine arrived, they drank together with the protagonist, each taking half a cup. Her cheeks were rosy and charming like peach blossoms. Initially, the protagonist was very frightened, but now he felt a stirring in his heart and gradually became more intimate with her. The purple hairpin then ordered the attendants, "Go and invite the other ladies and Aunt Wei to join the lady in the wedding feast." Whenever the term "new wife" or "lady" was used, it referred to the protagonist. The protagonist also playfully called himself "concubine."

    After a short while, the attendant reported, "Aunt has arrived." Four women emerged from the east and west walls, all of them beautiful and graceful. They pointed at the protagonist and asked, "Is this the new nobleman?" They examined him from his headband to his shoes, one by one, and then, with a respectful gesture, said to the purple hairpin, "Congratulations on finding such a fine wife!" The protagonist felt somewhat ashamed and blushed, looking as shy as a new bride.

    The attendant then announced, "Aunt Wei has arrived." A beautiful woman emerged from the south wall, slightly older. She greeted the purple hairpin with a smile and said, "The thief of fragrances dares to invite guests, troubling me to travel far!" The purple hairpin smiled back and asked, "Why didn't you teach her to come?" Aunt Wei replied, "The little girl makes it hard for people to move; I've already ordered a small maid to feed her with pastry."

    84

    于是叙札就席，佥曰：“新人宜首座。”生逊谢，诸女共挽生坐之。复挽紫钗坐于次，曰：“新郎君宜此位也。”紫钗亦谢而后坐。巳而诸女以次皆坐。一女名小琼，年最少，居婪尾焉。兰奴奉壶，蒲奴进馔。琼盏雕盘，无复凡器。芳洁充筵，咄嗟而办。
    　　酒数巡，一女拽爵而起曰：“吾观夫人眉黛，风雅新妆，妙咏可得闻乎”一女曰：“此吾辈亭，奈何以苦夫人”生素自负，不觉愠见，曰：“诗岂苦人之具乎妾虽不才，原有所献，请即席赋之。”诸女微哂曰：“愿聆佳句。”取笺笔授生。吟哦久之，不能就，雨汗浃两頬。小琼曰：“吾为夫人解围，可乎”遂夺笔书曰：
    　　“海内青莲死，谁为倚马才一言难返汗，点点落吟腮。”
    　　盖生姓冯氏，诗拆其宇以嘲之也，一座哄然。方哗笑间，南壁一婢抱三岁小女儿出，曰：“阿素寻母来也。”魏姑抱置膝上，将乳之。诸女群起弄儿曰：“能作一催妆诗，便当乳尔。”儿应声而就，诗曰：

    Explanation

    84

    Then, the seating list was arranged, and everyone agreed, saying, "The new guest should sit in the first seat." The young man humbly declined, but the other women collectively guided him to sit down. They then guided the woman named Zicai to sit next, and she said, "The new groom should sit in this position." Zicai also declined and then sat down. Shortly thereafter, the other women took their seats in order. One woman named Xiaoqiong, the youngest, sat in the last position. The servant named Lan served the wine, and the servant named Pu served the food. The cups and plates were beautiful and not ordinary. The table was filled with fragrant and clean dishes, prepared quickly.

    After several rounds of wine, a woman pulled her wine cup and stood up, saying, "I observe the lady's eyebrows, her elegant new makeup, and a beautiful poem can be heard?" Another woman said, "This is our pavilion, why should we trouble the lady?" The young man, who always believed in himself, unconsciously showed annoyance and said, "How can poetry trouble people? Though not talented, I am willing to offer something, please compose it on the spot." The other women smiled slightly and said, "Willing to listen to beautiful words." They took paper and pen and gave them to the young man. He recited for a long time but could not complete. Sweat like rain soaked both cheeks. Xiaoqiong said, "I will relieve the lady, can I?" She then took the pen and wrote:

    "The lotus in the sea has died, who is the talented horse rider? A word is hard to return to sweat, drops fall on the cheeks of the reciter."

    The young man's surname was Feng, and the poem split his name to mock him, causing everyone to laugh. As they were laughing, a maid from the south wall carried out a three-year-old girl, saying, "Aisu came looking for her mother." Aunt Wei held her and placed her on her lap, about to feed her. The other women gathered around and played with the child, saying, "If you can compose a poem to hasten the wedding, we will feed you." The child responded immediately with a poem.

    85

    “妆阁整巾衫，菱花笑相见。
    　　脂凝杜子唇，粉傅何郎面。”
    　　诸女咸喜曰：“真彗种也！”生惊愕愧赧，殆无人色。紫钗颇怜之，对众曰：“吾妇新来羞怯，故文思偶踬。再言诗者裸罚之，律无赦。”众笑而戢，生亦少安。
    　　紫钗又曰：“今日宴者，阿素之外，凡七人，适符竹林之数。吾有觞政，名占一筹，得五君者勿饮，得山公者罚一爵，惟钻核儿最为污鄙。若得阿戎，当以大斗酌之，而能有辞者仍勿饮。”众皆曰善。青衣具牙筹，书七贤姓名各一，以紫金筒贮之。
    　　紫钗探得王戎，生得山涛，诸女意在沛公，哗曰：“今日为二人合欢之酒，第一筹便是佳偶，宜行合卺礼。”乃引满一斗，令同饮各半。爵亦如之。饮讫，贮筹复探。生得王戎，酌大斗矣。一女得山涛者，索笔戏书曰：“臣山公启事：臣以斗筲，狠窃鼎钟。状见王戎，梵林猺竖，风尘小物，臣不敢滥爵，愿荐戎自代。”举爵向生，生无词以报，遂并饮之。

    Explanation

    85

    Neatening her cap and robe in the dressing room, she met with a smile in the mirror.
    Her lipstick was like Du's lips, and her powdered face resembled He's.
    All the women were delighted and said, "A true descendant of the stars!" The young man was startled, embarrassed, and pale. Purple Hairpin, however, pitied him and said to the group, "My wife is newly shy, so her thoughts are temporarily hindered. Those who speak again shall be punished by being stripped, no leniency in the law." The crowd laughed and quieted down, and the young man felt a little more at ease.

    Purple Hairpin then said, "Today's guests, excluding Asu, total seven, exactly matching the number of the Seven Sages of the Bamboo Grove. I have a drinking rule: named in a draw. Those who draw the Five Sages shall not drink, those who draw Shan Gong shall be punished with one cup, and only the one who draws the 'nut-cracker' is the most vulgar. If one draws Arong, they shall be served with a large cup, and those who can offer a reason shall still not drink." Everyone agreed. A servant prepared the toothpick for the draw, wrote the names of the Seven Sages on it, and stored it in a purple gold tube.

    Purple Hairpin drew Wang Rong, and the young man drew Shan Tao. The women were focused on Peigong, and they exclaimed, "Today is a wine for the union of two, the first draw is the perfect match, and the marriage ceremony should be performed." They then filled a cup with one dou and ordered them to drink half each. The cups were the same. After drinking, they stored the toothpick and drew again. The young man drew Wang Rong again and was served with a large cup. One woman who drew Shan Tao took a pen and playfully wrote, "Subject Shan Gong reports: I am a small vessel, yet I dare to hold a grand vessel. I see Wang Rong, a small creature in the world, a trivial matter in the dust. I dare not abuse the cup, I wish to recommend Wang Rong as my substitute." She held the cup towards the young man, who had no words to respond, and they drank together.

    86

    最后生复得王戎，不胜其虐，而紫钗得刘伶，生因谓之曰：“妾闻刘伶以酒为名，一饮一石，五斗解酲，郎当代妾饮。”紫钗不欲忤其意，将饮之，时阿素方卧母怀见之，亟代钗答曰：“妇人之言，慎不可听也。”众皆失笑，紫钗遂不饮。生怒甚，瞋目叱素曰：“乳臭儿，安敢尔”而小琼得阮籍，白眼而起，揶揄曰：“君等视浚冲，双目真闪闪如岩电矣。”众复大噱。
    　　生是日虽置身罗绮间，而为众所播弄，神气沮丧，赖紫钗常袒护之。然终觉口众我寡，遂力求罢席。魏姑曰：“新人欲入温柔乡，吾辈纠缠何为者”诸女皆起别，各向四壁中而去。生时已被西，不暇谁何。黼帐锦衾，烂设东阁，遂与紫钗缱绻焉。次日晨起，诸女以酒肴来会。复纵饮至暮而散。

    86

    The protagonist repeatedly encountered Wang Rong, who tormented him greatly. Meanwhile, Zicai met Duan Linge. The protagonist then said to her, "I have heard that Duan Linge is known for his love of wine, drinking a stone of wine at a time, and five dou of wine to cure a hangover. You should drink on my behalf." Zicai did not want to offend his wishes and was about to drink it when Su, who was lying in his mother's arms, saw this and quickly spoke on Zicai's behalf, saying, "A woman's words should not be trusted." Everyone laughed, and Zicai then did not drink. The protagonist became very angry and glared at Su, scolding him, "How dare you, you young and inexperienced brat!" Xiaoqiong, who had met Ruan Ji, raised his eyebrows and got up, mocking and saying, "You all look at Junchong, his eyes truly flash like lightning from the rocks." Everyone laughed again.

    That day, though the protagonist was among the beautiful women, he felt dejected as he was being toyed with by everyone. Thanks to Zicai's constant support, he managed to endure. However, he ultimately felt outnumbered and tried to end the gathering. Wei Gu said, "The new person wants to enter the land of pleasure, why are we纠缠with him?" All the women got up and left, each heading to their secluded places. The protagonist was already overwhelmed and had no time to question. Luxurious bedding was set up in the east hall, and he then spent the night intimately with Zicai. The next morning, the women came with wine and food. They continued to drink until evening and then dispersed.

    87

    生既住半载，亦能行壁中无碍，因过从诸女家。皆华屋幽闺，更无杂客，乃次第与诸女通焉。觉脂肤玉体，井殊凡艳，巫山洛浦不过矣，而小琼与生情好尤笃。紫钗知之，亦不问也。
    　　如是数年，键户而居，足不履阈。一日忽思归，言于紫钗。
    　　紫钗黯然不言，而愁怨之容可掬。生慰之曰：“归即来耳，何不释乃尔”紫钗强颔之，泪珠荧荧然落襟袖矣。将行之夕，诸女毕至，惨怛惆怅，无复欢容。时阿素稍长，鸦头绿衣，随母而至，亦牵衣喃喃叙别也。而紫钗及小琼，执手呜咽，断肠哀怨之语，至不可闻。生虽不胜其悲，而私怪儿女之情过于牵恋，谓数日便当重会，何至如木落水流相诀也遂别而行。

    Explanation

    87

    The youth had lived there for half a year, and he also acquired the ability to walk through walls without hindrance. He began to visit the homes of various women. All of these places were luxurious houses with secluded inner chambers, and there were no other guests. Thus, he had relations with the women one by one. He felt their flesh and skin like jade, far surpassing ordinary beauty, no more than the beauty of Mount Wushan and the banks of the Luo River. Xiao Qiong and the youth had especially deep affection. Zicai knew about it but did not ask either.

    For several years, he lived a secluded life, locked the door, and did not step over the threshold. One day, he suddenly thought of returning home and spoke to Zicai.

    Zicai was silent and gloomy, with a face of sorrow and resentment that can be held. The youth comforted her, saying, "I will return soon. Why are you so sad?" Zicai nodded reluctantly, and tears glistened and fell onto her sleeves. On the eve of departure, all the women came, sorrowful and melancholy, no longer showing joyful faces. At that time, A Su was a bit older, with black hair and green clothes, and she came with her mother, also tugging at clothes and speaking softly about parting. Zicai and Xiao Qiong held hands and sobbed, with heart-wrenching and sorrowful words, to the point of being unbearable to hear. Although the youth could not bear the sorrow, he secretly wondered if the affection between children was too excessive. He thought that within a few days they would meet again, but how could it be like leaves falling and water flowing apart? They then parted and left.

    88

    至家，妻见之若不相识，但言此妇何来。生大骇，急言“吾乃冯某也”。妻亦骇曰：“吾夫久出无踪，而此妇假其名，得毋妖乎”将欲走避。生猛然追忆，恍惚如梦，记紫钗故戏我，曾以巾帼遗我矣。乞镜自照，宛然好女也，亟白其故。妻不之信，生因笑谓曰：“不记双桥钓鲤时耶”妻曰：“竿头鱼饵安在”答曰：“藏于狮山浅泽中。”盖当年闺中隐语也。语既符，妻熟视其状。犹可识，遂纳之同寝。床第之间，固犹是藁砧风度。明日，重改衣装，本来之面目始见。
    　　居旬日，往访紫钗，风景不殊，道逾犹是，而仙村人面，俱不知何处所矣。茂林丛莽之间，猿鸟悲鸣，若有弹指而泣者。生回念当时情况，雨散云飞，欲再求阿郎呼我作新妇，了不可得，而泣别伤离之状，耿然在心目间也。遂悼痛而归，感疾迷离，数月而卒。

    Explanation

    88

    When he arrived home, his wife saw him as if she did not recognize him, and asked, "Who is this woman?" He was greatly startled and quickly declared, "I am Mr. Feng." His wife was also startled and said, "My husband has been out for a long time without any trace, and this woman is using his name, could she not be an evil spirit?" She wanted to run away to avoid it. He suddenly recalled and felt as if he were in a dream, remembering the old joke with the purple hairpin, she once gave me a woman's headband. He asked for a mirror to look at himself, and he looked like a beautiful woman, so he quickly explained the reason. His wife did not believe him, and he then smiled and said, "Do you not remember when we fished for carp at the Double Bridge?" His wife asked, "Where is the fish bait on the rod?" He replied, "It is hidden in the shallow marsh of Lion Mountain." It was a hidden language from that year in the inner chambers. When the words matched, his wife carefully examined his appearance. He was still recognizable, so she accepted him and they slept together. In the bedroom, he was still the same as before, with the same manner of a soldier. The next day, he changed his clothes again, and his original appearance was finally revealed.

    After ten days, he went to visit the purple hairpin. The scenery was unchanged, and the path was still the same, but the faces of the fairy village people were all unknown where they were. In the dense forest and thickets, apes and birds mourned sadly, as if they were crying with a snap of fingers. He recalled the situation at that time, like rain and clouds dispersing, wanting to ask my lord to call me his new wife, but it was no longer possible. The sad farewell and separation, still vivid in his heart and mind. He then mourned deeply and returned, feeling ill and confused, and died after several months.

    89

    非非子曰：冯生，丈夫也，而女子妇之；紫钗，妇也，而男子郎之：以为戏耳，岂意易形哉方其为妇也，不忆其尝为丈夫也。方其为丈夫也，不知其已为妇也。
    　　楚材
    　　金溪之印山有徐君开，邑诸生。与余族祖某公，甥舅也。相距十馀里，有村曰塘下，为两家往来之捷径。其西石山劈立，中隔一港，阔二丈许，最为险峻，罕有人行。
    　　徐一日省其外舅，挟一雨盖而行。经此地，日己瞑。逢一鬼修修而来，——状甚丑恶，腰以下长五六尺，——径来捉其臂。徐举雨盖奋击之，鬼亦反搏。徐将不胜，大呼救人，亦无应者。忽见其亡友楚材者奔来助之，鬼乃却，长啸数声，跨港而去。楚材亦倏忽不见。
    　　徐至舅家，已三鼓矣，口噤不能言。翼日乃述之，云云。
    　　画师
    　　徐氏群夫者，忘其郡邑矣。家号素封，而好接异客，技术之士多往依之。

    Explanation

    89

    Feifei Zi said: Feng Sheng, a man, was treated as a woman; Zicai, a woman, was treated as a man. They took it as a joke, how could one have thought the form would change? When she was a woman, she did not remember being a man before. When he was a man, he did not know he had already become a woman.

    Chucai

    In Yinxian, Mount Yinx, there lived Xu Junkai, a scholar of the county. He was the uncle of my clan's ancestor, a certain gentleman. They were separated by more than ten miles, and there was a village called Tangxia, which served as a shortcut for the two families to travel between. To the west of the village stood a stone mountain, and in the middle ran a river about two zhang wide, the most dangerous and steep, rarely traveled.

    One day, Xu visited his father-in-law, carrying an umbrella and walking. As he passed through this place, the sun had already set. He encountered a ghost approaching slowly—it looked very ugly, with a body length of five or six feet below the waist—it came straight to grab his arm. Xu raised the umbrella and struck it fiercely, and the ghost also retaliated. Xu was about to be overwhelmed, so he shouted for help, but no one responded. Suddenly, he saw his deceased friend Chucai rushing to help him. The ghost then retreated, let out several long cries, and crossed the river and left. Chucai also disappeared suddenly.

    Xu arrived at his father-in-law's house, and it was already the third drumbeat of the night. His mouth was tightly closed, and he could not speak. The next day, he recounted the events.

    The Xu family's group of men, whose county and city are forgotten, were known for their wealth and were fond of hosting unusual guests, so many skilled people came to rely on them.

    90

    有画师客其家，自言有奇画，愿与众观之。乃张画于壁，见楼台亭馆，重叠缦回，类西洋画。徐问画有何奇，画师曰：“吾能身入其中，故奇也。”徐以为妄，笑曰：“噫！帖然一纸，涂以丹碧，公乌能置身其间乎”画师乃向画咒之，即有一旁门洞开，耸身而入，门亦随合。扪之如故，索之不可得也，共相惊怪。门复启，而画师瞥下矣。
    　　众遂问画中之状，画师曰：“何问为第同往观之。”众皆喜。
    　　画师指画中门最大者曰：“当令开此正户，以迓诸君。”户应手而开。画师先立门中，一一以手捷之入。第见粱栌丹鹱，鸟革翠飞，埒于王侯甲第。每历一门闼，便觉改观：或粉壁森然，忽启双摩；或画栋巍然，忽开叠阁；或窗间缒下，别有亭池；或石罅穿入，另开园圃。其堂室之形制，矩方之外，有似月者，有似圭者，有似弓者，有似扇者，有似蕉叶者，有似香炉者，有似钟者，有似环玦者，有似壶瓮各器者。瑰形诡制，无一雷同。

    90

    A painter was a guest in his house, claiming to have a strange painting, and wished to show it to everyone. He hung the painting on the wall, revealing pavilions, towers, and halls, layered and winding, resembling Western-style paintings. Xu asked what was so strange about the painting, and the painter replied, "I can enter it myself, which makes it strange." Xu thought this was false and laughed, saying, "Ah! It's just a flat sheet of paper, painted with red and blue, how can you possibly place yourself in it?" The painter then recited a spell over the painting, and suddenly a side door opened. He leaped into it, and the door closed immediately. Touching the wall, it felt as usual, and searching for him was not possible. They all felt surprised and strange. The door opened again, and the painter suddenly appeared below.

    The crowd then asked about the state of the painting, and the painter said, "Why ask? Let's go see it together." Everyone was delighted.

    The painter pointed to the largest door in the painting and said, "Let this main door open to welcome you all." The door opened with a touch. The painter first stood in the door, pulling each person in quickly with his hands. Inside, they saw beams and pillars painted red and blue, birds' feathers and green flying, on par with the mansions of princes and nobles. Each time they passed through a gate, they immediately felt a change: sometimes there were white walls standing in a dense manner, suddenly opening two doors; sometimes there were painted beams standing tall, suddenly opening a layered pavilion; sometimes there was a window from which something was lowered, revealing separate pavilions and ponds; sometimes there was a crack in the stone through which one could enter, opening another garden. The shape and structure of the halls and rooms, outside the square, were some like the moon, some like a gu, some like a bow, some like a fan, some like banana leaves, some like incense burners, some like bells, some like rings and jade pendants, some like pots and jars. Exquisite shapes and strange designs, none were the same.

    91

    最后启一偏门，众皆入，乃徐妻卧室也。时方盛夏，徐见妻裸卧白绡帐中，皓体毕呈，急不及掩避。诸客见之，无不掩口奔出。至画所，画固俨然在壁也。
    　　徐大怒画师辱己，寻刀将杀之。画师复耸入画中，画与人俱失所在。
    　　荆州女
    　　明末时，荆州有许氏民，生三子一女。子以射猎为生。女最幼，年十六，嫁北村卢氏子，甚敬其夫家。自舅姑以下，悉得其意，乡里称顺妇焉。而父母尤绝怜之，诸兄亦各爱此妹。岁时往来，音问甚数。每猎得麇鹿獐兔诸物，或鲜而馈之，或腊而致之，虽一割之甘，未尝不共。女归宁父母，岁辄数四。

    Explanation

    91

    Finally, they opened a side door, and everyone entered, which turned out to be Xu's wife's bedroom. It was the height of summer, and Xu saw his wife lying naked in a fine white silk curtain, her pure body fully exposed. He was too shocked to cover her up. The guests who saw this could not help but cover their mouths and rush out. When they reached the place where the painting was, the painting was still firmly on the wall.

    Xu was furious that the painter had disgraced him and looked for a knife to kill him. The painter then re-entered the painting, and both the painting and the people disappeared from sight.

    A Woman from Jingzhou
    During the late Ming dynasty, there was a family surnamed Xu in Jingzhou who had three sons and one daughter. The sons lived by hunting. The daughter was the youngest, aged sixteen, and she married a man surnamed Lu from a village to the north. She highly respected her husband's family. From her mother-in-law and father-in-law down, she fully understood their intentions, and the villagers called her a well-behaved wife. Her parents especially loved her, and her brothers also loved this sister. They communicated frequently throughout the year. Whenever they hunted and caught deer, elk, hares, and other animals, they would either send fresh meat or dried meat to her. Even a small piece of meat, they always shared it. The daughter visited her parents several times a year.

    92

    一日，偕婿来母家，女忽发狂，走入室，闭户良久，母于隙间窥之。忽一虎突门而出，攫母，啮杀之。父惊走，虎复攫父，又杀之。其婿在旁，震骇仆地，虎不之顾。时三子方游猎归，遇虎于门。虎欲搏三子，咆哮而前。三子正持猎具，因共格虎，得不伤。虎复奔入室，三于追视之，则虎方人立，其皮豁落，乃其妹也。
    　　三子哀号，并其婿执女以诣县官。女自辩形变则心变，故不识父母，当无罪。令曰：“不然。心者身之宰也，心变故形变，即形变而心不宜变。且真虎杀人亦法所不贷，况女而虎其父母者欤”进置女于法。
    　　非非子曰：令之言然哉！夫虎，猛者也，亦灵者也。人而虎也，宜不猛面更灵君子于是知女之处心积虑而后成于虎，不然，何昧于亲而辨于婿也荆人之死于虎欤荆人之死于女也。而牛哀之变又不足言也，噎！

    Explanation

    92

    One day, accompanied by her husband, she came to her mother's home. Suddenly, the girl went mad, entered the room, and closed the door for a long time. Her mother looked through a crack. Suddenly, a tiger rushed out of the door, grasped her mother, bit her, and killed her. The father ran in fright, and the tiger again grasped him and killed him. Her husband was beside her, frightened and fell to the ground, but the tiger did not look at him. At that time, the three sons were returning from hunting and met the tiger at the door. The tiger wanted to attack the three sons, roared, and advanced. The three sons were holding hunting tools, so they together struck the tiger and did not get hurt. The tiger rushed back into the room, and the three sons followed and looked at it. The tiger was standing upright like a person, its fur was torn off, and it was her sister.

    The three sons cried out in grief, and along with her husband, they took the girl to the county official. The girl defended herself, saying that if the form changes, then the heart changes, so she did not recognize her parents and should not be guilty. The county official said, "Not so. The heart is the master of the body. If the heart changes, then the form changes, but even if the form changes, the heart should not change. Even if a real tiger kills someone, the law will not spare it, how much more so if a girl turns into a tiger and kills her parents?" He sentenced the girl to the law.

    The commentator said: Is the county official's statement right? A tiger is fierce and also intelligent. If a person turns into a tiger, they should not be fierce but more intelligent. Gentlemen understand that the girl had been thinking and planning and turned into a tiger. Otherwise, why would she not recognize her parents but recognize her husband? Did the people of Jing die at the hands of the tiger or the girl? And the transformation of Niu Ai is not worth mentioning, sigh!

    93

    竹冠道人
    　　乐安有士人，偶宿土地庙，梦土地饬其属曰“明日仙人过此，宜洒扫涤除，以迓仙跸。”复谓其媪曰“尔我亦宜沐浴以待，勿以慢误取罪也。”士人趋问曰：“仙人为谁”曰：“田真人也。”士人曰：“某亦愿迎之。”土地曰：“何与尔事且尔凡骨尸居，安得见真人”士人曰：“某慕道亦有年矣，反逊于君之纪纲耶”土地卒不许。士人大嚷，土地命逐之。出户而觉，颇以为异。
    　　归以语同学者，次日同往庙前伺之。有竹冠道人自西来，朗目修髯，丰度飘洒；葫芦长剑各一，系于腰下；过庙门而拱手焉。诸人私计，殆田真人也，径前牵其衣，请大仙过我。道人笑曰：“某姓宫，名去绵，游方道士耳。安知仙术何于之误耶”诸人请益坚，道人曰：“既诸君款留过厚，某漫无短长之人，安敢峻拒聊且过子。”诸人喜跃，导之以归。共洁一斋而奉之，饮食器具，效至甚诚。

    Explanation

    93

    A Taoist Hermit with a Bamboo Hat

    There was a scholar from Le'an who happened to stay overnight at a land god temple. He dreamed that the land god instructed his subordinates, saying, "Tomorrow a divine being will pass by here; you should clean and prepare the temple to welcome the divine being." He then said to his wife, "We should also bathe and prepare ourselves to welcome him, and do not let our negligence or carelessness incur punishment." The scholar rushed to ask, "Who is the divine being?" The land god replied, "It is Master Tian." The scholar said, "I also wish to welcome him." The land god said, "What does this have to do with you? You are just an ordinary mortal body, how can you see a divine being?" The scholar said, "I have been pursuing the Tao for many years, should I be more humble than your subordinates?" The land god finally refused. The scholar loudly protested, and the land god ordered his subordinates to drive him away. Upon exiting the temple, he woke up and found it quite strange.

    He returned and told his fellow scholars. The next day, they all went to the temple to wait for him. A Taoist hermit with a bamboo hat came from the west, with bright eyes and a long beard, and a graceful and elegant demeanor. He had one gourd and one long sword, tied to his waist. As he passed by the temple door, he bowed respectfully. The others privately thought, "It must be Master Tian," and went forward to pull his clothes, asking him to pass through. The hermit smiled and said, "My surname is Gong, my given name is Quan; I am just a wandering Taoist priest. How could I possibly know what kind of mistake I made about your divine powers?" The others insisted even more strongly. The hermit said, "Since you have treated me so kindly, I am just an ordinary person, how dare I refuse so rudely? Let's just pass through." The others were very happy and led him back. They prepared a clean room and served him, providing food, drink, and utensils with great sincerity.

    94

    居十馀日，诸人屡求度世及黄白导引之事。道人茫然，略无所答，忽染沉疴，淹淹欲毙。诸人始犹奉汤药，渐见其疾不支，颇悔之，咸咎士人之妄。士人亦自以为冒昧也。将谋迁之于他室，道人曰：“念某客死他乡，乞以此斋为葬地，饮食而祝我，则公等始终之德也。”众怒曰：“我辈误识尔，耗费不少。此吾辈清修之所，顾以为尔宅兆耶”
    　　忽一童子造门请曰：“吾师在此否”众问为谁，童以道人对。众喜，告以病。且给之曰：“尔师日望尔来护病归去，今始来耶？”童闻之，趋面入见，道人已不能言，但以目视童，泪隐隐承睫。童大恸躃踊，呕血数声而绝。道人见之，长吁一声，亦死榻上。诸人慌乱，不知所为。惧为人所觉，亟具薄材，谋以夜半瘗之粪壤之下。解槐腰间剑，剑光艳发；视葫芦中，皆黄金。众大喜，汉瓜分之。

    Explanation

    94

    After staying for more than ten days, the others repeatedly asked about matters of immortality, alchemy, and spiritual guidance. The monk was lost and could barely answer them. Suddenly, he contracted a serious illness and was on the verge of death. Initially, the others still provided him with medicine and care, but as his condition worsened, they gradually lost hope and began to regret their involvement, blaming the scholar for his rash actions. The scholar also felt that he had acted recklessly. They planned to move the monk to another room, but the monk said, "I am a guest who has died away from home. I ask that this temple be used as my burial ground. If you continue to provide me with food and drink and pray for me, it will be a sign of your consistent kindness." The crowd angrily replied, "We were deceived by you, and we spent a lot of money. This is our place of quiet cultivation. How dare you think this is your burial ground?"

    Suddenly, a young boy arrived at the door and asked, "Is my master here?" The crowd asked who he was, and the boy referred to the monk as his master. They were pleased and told him about the monk's illness, saying, "Your master has been waiting for you to take care of him and bring him back home. Why are you just arriving now?" The boy heard this and quickly went inside to see the monk. The monk was unable to speak, but he looked at the boy with tears in his eyes. The boy wept bitterly, fell to the ground, and died after vomiting blood. The monk saw this and let out a long sigh before dying on the bed. The crowd became panicked and didn't know what to do. Fearing being discovered, they quickly prepared a simple coffin and planned to bury the monk in the middle of the night in the manure heap. They took the sword from the scholar's waist, which emitted a brilliant light. They looked inside the gourd and found it filled with gold. The crowd was delighted and divided the gold among themselves.

    95

    道人忽大笑而起，谓童子曰：“可去矣！”童子亦起，因掷其剑化为龙，掷葫芦化为鹤，各乘其一，飞腾空中，冉冉而去。诸人相顾骇愕，悟仙人之游戏矣。
    　　其卧室有香，经月不散。壁间有五言古诗一首、七绝二首，宝墨淋漓，向莫之见也。抄而读之，随抄随灭。五古云：
    　　“琼树三千丈，种在流霞阙。
    　　朝浥瑶台露，暮挂糇山月。
    　　风霜饱更荣，花叶无衰歇。
    　　梁洪花下倾，醉倒胭脂雪。”
    　　七绝云：
    　　“空山明月照莓苔，剑匣匏尊久不开。黄鹤未知尘世险，等闲载我出蓬莱。”
    　　“海云千片散幽襟，昌老无心却有心。仙侣相逢好归去，碧桃花下抚瑶琴。”
    　　雷葬妇
    　　新建有妇人顾氏。其夫洪某，车慵也，事母甚孝。母老且盲。洪每慵得钱，则市酒肉归，使妇为母作食。妇素不职而好馋。夫不在，辄凌君。夫所市食，辄先自啖过半，以少许食姑。姑虑儿怒将虐妇，秘不以告，叹息而已。洪卒不知。

    Explanation

    95

    The Taoist suddenly laughed loudly and stood up, saying to the young boy, "It's time to go!" The young boy also stood up, and the Taoist then threw his sword, which transformed into a dragon, and threw his gourd, which turned into a crane. Each rode one, flying and ascending into the air, slowly departing. The others looked at each other in shock and astonishment, realizing it was the Taoist's playfulness.

    There was a lingering fragrance in his bedroom, which did not dissipate for a month. On the wall, there were one five-character ancient poem and two seven-character quatrains. The calligraphy was rich and abundant, something that had not been seen before. Copying and reading it, it disappeared as soon as it was copied. The five-character ancient poem reads:

    "A jade tree three thousand feet tall, planted in the palace of flowing clouds.
    Morning dew from the Jade Terrace, evening moon hanging over the mountain of provisions.
    After enduring wind and frost, it thrives even more; the flowers and leaves never wither or rest.
    Under the flowers, Liang Hong pours wine, and falls drunk in the red snow."

    The seven-character quatrains read:

    "In the empty mountain, the bright moon shines on the moss and lichen; the sword scabbard and gourd cup have not been opened for a long time. The yellow crane does not know the dangers of the mortal world; it casually carries me out of the fairyland of Penglai."
    "Thousand clouds of the sea scatter the inner feelings; Master Chang appears to have no mind but actually does. When immortals meet, it is good to return; playing the jade zither under the peach blossoms."

    The Thunder Burial of the Woman

    In Xinxian, there was a woman named Gu. Her husband, Hong, was a lazy carriage driver, but he was very filial to his mother. His mother was old and blind. Hong would often buy wine and meat and return home, instructing his wife to prepare food for his mother. The wife was usually lazy and greedy. When her husband was not around, she would bully her mother-in-law. The food her husband bought, she would eat more than half herself, and only give a little to her mother-in-law. The mother-in-law feared that her son would be angry and mistreat the wife, so she kept it secret and only sighed about it. Hong was unaware of this.

    96

    后妇产一男，数月矣，馋愈甚。洪市切面归，令妇熟以进母，而洪仍御车他所。面既熟，妇食毕。姑索禽，妇无以应，欺其盲不辨物，乃烹蚯蚓而进之。姑食，大呕吐。忽烈风暴雨，雷震一声，失妇所在。
    　　雨既霁，洪御而归。过石山，见一裸体妇人——乃其妻也，——腰以下葬于石中，周身皆石，宛如熔铸，力拔不得出。问其故，喑哑不能盲。亦不死，双目炯炯，环转视人，蠢然一物矣。石旁雷书二十四字深入石中，类携刻，其文云：
    　　“埋半身以存孤，豁双乳以哺子。日一食以延生。终三年而击死。”
    　　洪日抱儿就乳，井挈饮食而饲之，日止一餐，遵雷教也。
    　　观者如堵。环视不得近，近则有物拽之仆，亦可异也。
    　　三年，雷复击妇死，暴尸于外。遍体焦烂，蚯蚓嘬之。而石缝仍合如故。

    Explanation

    96

    After the wife gave birth to a son, several months passed, and the mother-in-law's craving became more intense. Hong went to the market to buy sliced noodles and returned home, ordering his wife to cook them and serve them to the mother-in-law, while Hong continued to drive to another place. Once the noodles were cooked, the wife ate them all. The mother-in-law asked for meat, and the wife had nothing to offer, thinking that the mother-in-law couldn't see and wouldn't know what was being served, so she cooked earthworms and served them. The mother-in-law ate them and vomited violently. Suddenly, a fierce wind and heavy rain, and a thunderclap struck, and the wife disappeared.

    After the rain stopped, Hong drove back home. Passing by a stone mountain, he saw a naked woman—his wife—buried below the waist in the stone, her entire body encased in stone, as if melted and cast, and she could not be pulled out. When asked why, she could not speak and was mute. She was not dead, with bright eyes that turned to look at people, like a beast. Beside the stone, twenty-four characters were written by lightning, deeply etched, and the text read:

    "Bury half her body to save the orphan, expose her breasts to feed the child. Eat once a day to prolong life. Finally die after three years from a lightning strike."

    Hong would carry the child to nurse, and bring food and drink to feed the child, eating only once a day, following the lightning's command.

    Onlookers gathered like a wall. Looking around, they could not approach, and anyone who tried was pulled down, which was also strange.

    After three years, lightning struck the woman again, killing her, and her body was left exposed. Her entire body was scorched and rotten, with earthworms eating her. The stone cracks closed as before.

    97

    非非子曰：不孝之罪，上通于天。雷神殛之，易易耳。第妇当诛而洪宜有后，洪贫不能再娶也。洪贫不能更召乳媪也，则子必死而洪无儿，足诛其妇并杀其子，杀其子竟绝其嗣矣。非所以报孝子也。于此见天之妙用，于此见雷之苦心。
    　　望都宰
    　　痘神何神也姑勿深考。或曰：居峨眉山，姐妹三人，身著麻衣，盖女仙之流，主人间痘疹之疾，人呼为麻娘娘云。神甚灵显，而严于小节。病痘之家为位奉之，言语稍不检，衣物稍不洁，及诚敬少懈者，病者辄作神言语呵谴之，虽私隐无不昭揭。其甚者，痘或不治，为得罪于神也。灵界之迹不可胜纪，然亦非妄祸人者。
    　　吾乡陈君洪书，儿时以痘死，置于东厢。其母抚而哭之，坐于户限，倦面假寐。见三麻衣妇人入室，视儿惊曰：“向几误！此望都宰也，可放还。”言毕，出户去。母惊觉，儿已苏矣。后果任望都县令。罢官归，今犹在。

    Explanation

    97

    非非子曰：不孝之罪，上通于天。雷神殛之，易易耳。第妇当诛而洪宜有后，洪贫不能再娶也。洪贫不能更召乳媪也，则子必死而洪无儿，足诛其妇并杀其子，杀其子竟绝其嗣矣。非所以报孝子也。于此见天之妙用，于此见雷之苦心。

    望都宰

    痘神何神也姑勿深考。或曰：居峨眉山，姐妹三人，身著麻衣，盖女仙之流，主人间痘疹之疾，人呼为麻娘娘云。神甚灵显，而严于小节。病痘之家为位奉之，言语稍不检，衣物稍不洁，及诚敬少懈者，病者辄作神言语呵谴之，虽私隐无不昭揭。其甚者，痘或不治，为得罪于神也。灵界之迹不可胜纪，然亦非妄祸人者。

    吾乡陈君洪书，儿时以痘死，置于东厢。其母抚而哭之，坐于户限，倦面假寐。见三麻衣妇人入室，视儿惊曰：“向几误！此望都宰也，可放还。”言毕，出户去。母惊觉，儿已苏矣。后果任望都县令。罢官归，今犹在。

    98

    由是观之，痘殇者非尽神之为政也，其亦数之前定者与
    　　上宫完古
    　　溪北上官完古，尝行远村。归值日暮，投止无所。至一山，见远灯明灭林外。寻灯而至，乃一小村，数十户，户各一灯，灯色甚绿，有异常火，而哭声甚哗，比屋皆然。未便遽入，隐身缓步听之。
    　　一妇哭曰：“乌乎，吾不图尔之至于斯也！”一妇哭曰：“哀哉吾夫！痛哉吾夫！”一妇哭曰：“天乎，何夺吾二男之速乎！”一男子哭曰：“吾弟素勤谨，不意亦罹横祸。”一老人哭曰：“吾经营数十年，仅足衣食。尔破我产已甚矣，又役我子而死之，此恨宁有终极耶”又有男女二人共哭曰：“阿父阿父，衰朽之年犹不能兔于沟墼！”其余声情哀抑，听不能彻。

    Explanation

    98

    From this perspective, those who die from smallpox are not all under the rule of the gods; perhaps they are also determined by fate.

    Shangguan Wangu of Xibei once traveled to a distant village. Returning at dusk, he had nowhere to stay. He saw distant lights flickering in the woods. Following the lights, he arrived at a small village with several dozen houses, each with a greenish light and loud crying, indicating that all the houses were experiencing the same situation. He did not enter immediately but hid and listened carefully.

    One woman cried, "Alas, I did not expect you to come to this!" Another woman cried, "Alas my husband! Painful my husband!" Another woman cried, "Heaven, why did you take my two sons so quickly!" A man cried, "My brother was always diligent, but unexpectedly he also suffered a misfortune." An old man cried, "I have worked for decades, barely enough for food and clothing. You have ruined my property, and now you have made my son work to death, can this hatred ever end?" Two people cried together, "Father, father, even in our old age, we cannot escape the grave!" The other cries were full of sorrow and could not be fully heard.

    99

    又一家不闻哭声，但闻男女数人窃窃私语，乃有人数四伺门外，若谋机事恐人觉者。完古甚怪，入而问之曰：“是何哭声之多也”男女乍见，皆惊避。旋闻人语云：“客也，何避为”
    　　有数男子出，衣履甚敝，椎鲁类农夫，因先闭其门，谓完古曰：“客远来未知，此地官长自丞簿以上贪残如狼虎，数年工役繁兴，科派乡里，日役丁男敷千，而少给其食。小民失业，劳苦吁天。饥馑洊臻，道殣相望。无以供租税，悍吏日来吾乡，叫嚣隳突，鸡狗不宁。乡民流离，死丧殆尽。今副使且至矣，闻县官捉人除道。副使爪牙千百，所在骚扰。不死于劳悴，则死于敲扑。仓卒之间，未能远徒，且谋宵遁耳。”

    Explanation

    99

    In another household, there was no sound of crying, but only whispers of a few men and women. Outside the door, several people were waiting, as if they were planning secret matters and feared discovery. Wan Gu found this very strange and entered to ask, “Why are there so many cries?” Upon seeing him, the men and women were startled and retreated. Soon, they heard someone say, “You’re guests, why retreat?”

    Several men then came out, dressed in worn clothes, simple and rustic, like farmers. They closed the door and said to Wan Gu, “You must not know, as you are a guest. The officials here, from the county magistrate down, are as greedy and cruel as wolves and tigers. For several years, public works projects have been numerous and frequent, imposing levies on the villages. Thousands of male adults are drafted daily, with little food given to them. The common people have lost their jobs and are suffering, crying out to heaven. Famine has repeatedly arrived, and dead bodies are seen on the road. Unable to provide for rents and taxes, brutal officials come to our village daily, shouting and causing chaos, making neither chickens nor dogs at peace. Villagers are displaced, and death and loss are almost complete. Now the deputy governor is about to arrive, and we hear that the county officials are catching people to clear the roads. The deputy governor has countless henchmen, causing trouble everywhere. Not dying from exhaustion, then dying from beatings. In a hurry, we cannot flee far away, and we are planning to flee at night.”

    100

    完古慰之曰：“古有力役之征，民为上劳，亦其职也。”农夫皆怒曰：“公若为吏，当不减县官之酷。”室中有妇人呼曰：“此何时，尔辈尚闲话，不畏县里公差耶？”言未已，忽闻扣门声甚急。男女皆吞声。一妇人悄语曰：“吏至矣，吾当应之。尔辈且从后门出。”遂启门而奔，完古亦从之。诸人行甚疾，顷刻相失。完古道黑不能前，乃伏于丛莽之内，屏息伺之。
    　　须臾，见数吏持火炬，挟锒铛绳素，呵叱而来，搜索甚急。
    　　映火而窥，有一吏似是族子某，盖数年前已死者，而声情逼肖。欲出问，恐有误，因念此岂阴府耶且官吏酷虐如此，乡里死徙如此，向何不少闻耶？旋闻一吏曰：“不知从甚路去好”一吏曰：“歧路难寻，当分道索之。”且行且语，亦顷刻不见。
    　　乃出寻路，至旧村，而妇人之哭如故也，男子则皆逃矣。完古欲求宿，妇人皆曰：“吾寡妇，不便留客。”使于门外檐下坐。

    Explanation

    100

    Wangu comforted them and said, "In ancient times, there was a system of corvée labor, and the people were the most laborious, which is also their duty." The farmers all became angry and said, "If you become an official, you will not be less cruel than the county officials." A woman called out, "What time is this? You are still idle chatting, do you not fear the public officials from the county?" Before she finished speaking, there was a sudden knock at the door. Everyone was silent. One woman whispered, "The officials have arrived. I will answer them. You all should go out the back door." She opened the door and ran out, and Wangu followed her. The others walked very quickly, and in a short while, they lost each other. Wangu was walking in the dark and could not move forward, so he hid in the thick undergrowth and held his breath, waiting.

    In a short while, he saw several officials holding torches, carrying chains and ropes, shouting and coming, searching very urgently. Looking by the light of the fire, he saw an official who seemed to be a relative of someone, who had died several years ago, but his voice and demeanor were very similar. Wangu wanted to ask, but he was afraid of making a mistake, so he thought, "Is this the underworld? And the officials are so cruel, and the people are fleeing so much, why didn't I hear about it before?" Then he heard one official say, "I don't know which way to go." Another official said, "The paths are difficult to find, we should search in different directions." They walked and talked, and in a moment, they were gone.

    Wangu then went out to find the way and reached the old village. The women were still crying, and the men had all fled. Wangu wanted to stay overnight, but the women all said, "I am a widow, it is not convenient to keep guests." They made him sit under the eaves outside the door.

    101

    夜久门闭灯灭，哭声亦寂。
    　　迟明视之，乃坐坟冢间耳。
    　　非非子曰，先贵溪公少时，舍于东里饶氏。孤馆空山，竹树阴翳。有哭声起于山间，呦呦然，呜呜然，如怨如诉，若远若近，虽桓山之禽、泰山之妇，不足喻其哀切也。卧则闻之，起则寂然。每夕皆然，辄为心恻。后以询其居人，乃知有孀妇贫而无子，以冻馁死，实葬此山。公悲其遇，哀其情，为作《鬼哭赋》。呜呼！穷民无告，虽魑魑泉台，犹无泪干时耶今观于上官氏之所闻，乃知长梦之人更有新安，石壕之苦也。
    　　报仇蛇
    　　金溪彭坊村妇李氏，年三十馀。夜梦一人告曰：“若，吾仇也，乃在此。吾将报若，若勿避，”觉而异之。
    　　次日午晌，妇治蔬厨下。家人惊报：“有巨蛇见于寝室。”妇心动，知其为梦中人也，操刀而往。见蛇尾在楼而首垂地，遽前斩之。蛇奔出于外，妇逐而剐之，刃创数十，而蛇竟毙。妇亦昏碚，有顷乃苏。

    Explanation

    101

    Translation

    The night deepened, the door closed, the lights went out, and the crying sound also became silent.
    Late in the morning, she looked and saw that it was a woman sitting in the middle of the tomb.
    Nononzi said: When the former nobleman of Guixi was young, he stayed at the eastern Li family of Rao. In a lonely inn in an empty mountain, with bamboo and trees casting dense shade, a crying sound arose from the mountain, sounding like "yōu yōu" and "wū wū," as if a complaint and a lament, as if far and as if near. Even the birds of Huanshan and the women of Taishan could not express the depth of her sorrow. When lying down, she could hear it; when getting up, it was silent. Every night was like this, and it always moved his heart. Later, he asked the locals, and he learned that there was a widowed woman who was poor and childless, and she died of cold and hunger, actually buried in this mountain. The nobleman felt pity for her fate and sorrowed over her plight, and composed the Ghost Cry Rhapsody. Alas! The poor people have no one to turn to, even if they are ghosts in the underworld, their tears still do not dry. Now, seeing what the Shangguan family heard, it is known that those in a long dream have even more suffering in Xin'an and Shihao.

    Revenge Snake
    Li, a woman from Pengfang Village in Jinxi, was in her thirties. At night, she dreamed of a person who said to her, "You, I am your enemy, and I am here. I will take revenge on you, so do not avoid me." She was startled upon waking.
    The next day at noon, while she was preparing vegetables in the kitchen, her family reported, "There is a giant snake in the bedroom." The woman's heart stirred, knowing it was the person from her dream, and she took a knife and went to confront it. She saw the snake's tail on the second floor and its head hanging down, so she rushed forward and cut it. The snake rushed out, and the woman chased and mutilated it, cutting it dozens of times, and the snake finally died. The woman also fainted, but after a while, she regained consciousness.

    102

    后遂怔忪得悸疾，死而复苏者岁辄数四。至今尚在。
    　　非非子曰：怨毒之于人甚矣！蛇仇于人，犹思报焉。然预以告人，使知为备，蛇亦愚哉！或曰：非蛇也，鬼物也。若是，即谓之愚鬼。
    　　徐太保
    　　前明吾乡徐太保，少时偶行绿野。前临石濑，深不及骭，褰裳可涉。已解其履矣，忽见—女子婷婷而至。立而望之：年可十七八，丰态绝妍，临流不语，怅望夷犹，若欲济而恨无粱者。徐曰：“美人欲涉乎”女子微笑。徐曰：“美人欲涉，奈罗袜之难凌波何”女子乃低头自顾，仍微笑而羞涩不胜。徐笑曰：“书生之鹏背，可作舟楫乎？”女子颔之，乃以双袖附徐肩。徐以两手挽其足，载之中流。依依丽影，清涟可鉴，不觉微吟曰：
    　　“玉女渡银河，红裙罩绿波。”
    　　诗未成，已登彼岸。女子下而续之曰：
    　　“只因诗二句，夺却状元科。”

    Explanation

    102

    Afterward, she suffered from palpitations due to fright, dying and reviving several times a year. To this day, she still lives.

    Master Feifei said: "The grudge and poison are very great to people! Even a snake's grudge against a person, it still thinks of revenge. However, if one were to warn others in advance, so that they know how to prepare, would the snake also be foolish? Some say: It is not a snake, but a ghostly entity. If so, it should be called a foolish ghost."

    Master Xu, the Protector

    During the Ming dynasty, in my hometown, there was a young Master Xu. One day, he was walking through a green field. In front of him lay a rocky stream, deep but not reaching his shank, and he could cross by pulling up his skirt. He had already removed his shoes when he suddenly saw a graceful woman approaching. She stood by the stream, about seventeen or eighteen years old, graceful and beautiful beyond compare, standing silently by the water, looking wistfully and hesitantly, as if wanting to cross but regretting there was no bridge. Xu asked, "Do you want to cross?" The woman smiled. Xu said, "If you want to cross, what to do with the red socks that are hard to wade through the water?" The woman then looked down at herself, still smiling and blushing. Xu laughed and said, "Can a scholar's back like a great bird serve as a boat?" The woman nodded, and then held onto Xu's shoulders with her two sleeves. Xu took her feet in his hands and carried her across the stream. Her graceful and beautiful figure reflected in the clear, mirror-like water, and he could not help but gently recite:

    "A jade maiden crossing the Milky Way, a red skirt covering the green waves."

    The poem was not yet finished when they had already reached the other side. The woman stepped down and continued the poem:

    "Just because of these two lines of poetry, the top scholar title was taken away."

    103

    言罢不见。
    　　徐惊且悔，悟神人之试己也。后登天顺己丑进士第二名，官至太子太保。
    　　耳食录卷五
    　　紫溪洞长
    　　金陵李翁，——逸其名，——有城两别业，多妖异，键其户有年矣。一日，故人子黄生来假寓。李告以异，而黄固言不畏，不得已，从之。
    　　居数日，有叟自内出，鬚鬓皓朗，衣冠甚古。黄知其妖也，叱之曰：“若何人来此何为”叟曰：“以君独处，欲来共破寂寥耳。君固解事者，何乃效嵇生灭灯耶我紫溪洞长也，君第安之。无所苦。”自是辄与谈论终日，词义风骞，语多奇奥。黄甚厚之，而秘不以告李。
    　　李使仆觇焉。及其门，无所睹，但见黄端坐。语刺刺不休，若与宾客偶谈状，归以告李。李召黄诘焉，则曰无之。李曰：“是必有异，不去且有患。”赠以白金，遣之归。黄不欲，至谓李无主人情，乃下逐客令，殆厌我不输僦屋资，故反以金诮我也，乃辞其金，并益以己金还之，求僦别业以终岁。

    Explanation

    103

    After speaking, he disappeared.

    Gradually, he was surprised and regretful, realizing that it was a divine being testing him. Later, he passed the imperial examination in the year Tian Shun Jichou as the second-place winner and reached the position of Grand Tutor to the Crown Prince.

    From the Fifth Volume of the Ershi Lu (Record of Heard Matters)

    The Master of the Zixi Cave

    An elderly man from Jinling, whose name is unknown, had two properties in the city, both of which were often plagued by strange and supernatural occurrences. The doors had been locked for many years. One day, an old friend's son, Huang Sheng, came to stay temporarily. The elderly man told Huang about the strange events, but Huang firmly stated that he was not afraid, and so the elderly man allowed him to stay.

    After staying for several days, an old man emerged from inside, with a white beard and ancient attire. Huang recognized him as a spirit and scolded him, saying, "Who are you, and what brings you here?" The old man replied, "I came because you are alone, and I wish to break your loneliness. You are someone who understands matters, so why did you extinguish the lamp like Ji Sheng? I am the Master of the Zixi Cave. Just rest easy; there is nothing to worry about." From then on, they would talk all day, and the old man's words were lofty and profound, often filled with strange and profound meanings. Huang treated him very kindly but kept his identity a secret from the elderly man.

    The elderly man sent a servant to spy on the situation. Upon reaching the door, the servant saw nothing but Huang sitting calmly, talking incessantly as if chatting casually with guests. He returned and reported this to the elderly man. The elderly man summoned Huang and questioned him, but Huang denied it. The elderly man said, "There must be something unusual; if you don't leave, you will be in trouble." He gave Huang a gift of silver and sent him back home. Huang did not want to leave, and he accused the elderly man of lacking the courtesy of a host. The elderly man then issued an order to expel Huang, likely because he was tired of Huang not paying rent, so he used the gift of gold to criticize him. Huang refused the gold, added his own gold to return it, and asked to rent a separate property for the rest of the year.

    104

    李固长者，辞而谢焉，然观其词气之间，颇若迷罔，益患之。乃预买一舟，约舟子曰：“客登即发。”而召黄，醉以酒，遣二仆舁之行，并束其装置舟中，即解缆迳去，比黄觉，已数十里矣。黄念叟甚挚，欲返舟。舟于知其放，勿听也。
    　　行数日，叟忽至舟中，让黄曰：“我与若好友，即去，何得不告我”黄谢之，以情诉。叟笑曰：“固知之。欲返乎易易耳”舟子闻之，揣知妖妄，举篙将击之。叟忽曳黄登岸，飞腾而去，他舟及岸上见者，无不骇绝也。舟子亟回棹白李，使视诸别业，阒其无人焉。
    　　叟既偕黄至旧所，曰：“此地殊扰，吾已别僦一室，盍迁焉”黄从之。行二里许，至—处榛莽塞径，室宇荒秽。复前行，入一小门，越曲巷三四，则回廊曲榭，雕题画壁，广厦数十间。便有童子二人出迎客，而笑语之声自帘幕间出者，复娇韵可听也。叟曰：“此老夫菟裘。非佳客良友，未许至此。”黄谢之。

    Explanation

    104

    Ri Gu, the venerable elder, declined and thanked him, but observing his manner and tone, he seemed somewhat bewildered, which increased his concern. Therefore, he bought a boat in advance and agreed with the boatman, saying, "As soon as the guest boards, set sail." He then summoned Huang, got him drunk with wine, and had two servants carry him along, packing his belongings into the boat. Immediately, he untied the ropes and left directly. By the time Huang woke up, they were already dozens of miles away. Huang thought the old man was very close to him and wanted to return by boat. The boatman knew he was being let go and did not listen.

    After several days, the old man suddenly appeared on the boat and reprimanded Huang, saying, "I am a good friend of yours. How could you not tell me you were leaving?" Huang thanked him and explained the situation. The old man smiled and said, "I knew it already. Wanting to return is easy enough." The boatman heard this and suspected it was a trick, raising his pole to strike him. The old man suddenly dragged Huang onto the shore and flew away. Other boats and those on the shore who saw this were all utterly shocked. The boatman quickly turned back to report to Li, who checked on his other properties and found them empty.

    The old man then accompanied Huang to his old place and said, "This place is particularly noisy. I have rented a different room. Why not move there?" Huang followed him. After walking about two miles, they arrived at a place where thickets blocked the path and the buildings were dilapidated and overgrown. They continued forward, entered a small gate, passed through a few winding alleys, and then there were winding corridors and pavilions, with carved and painted walls, and a dozen or so large rooms. Immediately, two children came out to welcome the guest, and the sound of laughter and conversation came from behind the curtains, which was pleasant to hear. The old man said, "This is my retreat. Not for good guests and friends, are not allowed to come here." Huang thanked him.

    105

    居数日，款留甚厚。是时苗年三十馀，所遇多侘異，睹此豪华，而无聊不平之慨，不觉形于颜色。一日，酒后淡心，叟谓黄：“何郁郁不乐第言所慾，某悉能为君效一臂。”黄笑曰：“吾屡试不售，而愿望颇奢，若得今科状头足矣！”叟踌躇曰：“不可。若次科，即亦无难。”黄曰：“君适言之，何遽有难词，得毋诳乎”叟良久乃曰：“往朝南海，或有济，然终不敢必也。”久之，未有行意。黄促之，与俱往。
    　　及半途，过一神庙，叟惊惧变色，惨然曰：“君累我，祸至交矣！速反避！”黄方欲问故，忽阴霾障天，雨雹如矢，雷电交作，砰訇若崩山塌谷。黄急避入庙，而叟不见。既而雨收天霁。出迹之，见巨鳞长二丈馀，死于庙门外。踉跄而返。
    　　非非子曰：蛇之于友，诚善矣。而卒以妄举触法，君子惜之。嗟乎，名之不可以悻觊也也！一衿之青，犹关禄籍，况于巍科首选乎以是恶蛇之险而嗤黄生之愚也。
    　　宓妃

    Explanation

    105

    After several days, Huang was treated very hospitably. At that time, Huang was in his thirties, and he had encountered many disappointments. Witnessing this luxury, he couldn't help but show his feelings of aimlessness and injustice on his face. One day, after drinking, he became disheartened, and the old man said to Huang, "Why are you so gloomy? Just tell me what you desire, and I will do everything in my power to help you." Huang smiled and said, "I have failed repeatedly in my attempts, and my aspirations are very high. If I could pass the current examination and become the top scholar, that would be enough!" The old man hesitated and said, "That is not possible. If it is the next examination, it will not be difficult." Huang asked, "You just said that, why suddenly be unwilling? Is it not a lie?" After a long pause, the old man said, "Go to the South Sea, there might be some benefit, but I still dare not be certain." For a long time, there was no intention to leave. Huang urged him, and they went together.

    By the halfway point, they passed a temple. The old man suddenly became frightened and changed color, looking grim and said, "You have brought me trouble, disaster is imminent! Quickly turn back and avoid it!" Huang was about to ask why, when suddenly dark clouds blocked the sky, hailstones fell like arrows, and lightning and thunder struck alternately, roaring like mountains collapsing and valleys being filled. Huang hurriedly took refuge in the temple, but the old man was nowhere to be seen. After the rain stopped and the sky cleared, he went out to look for the old man and found a giant fish, two zhang long, dead outside the temple. He returned in a stumbling manner.

    Non-zi said: The snake is indeed good to its friend. But it ultimately acted recklessly and violated the law, which gentlemen pity. Alas, one cannot gain fame through侥幸 (侥幸 means '侥幸') or侥幸 (侥幸 means '侥幸'). A scholar's robe, still depends on official records, how much more so for the top scholar in the prestigious examination! Therefore, one despises the snake's recklessness and mocks the scholar's foolishness.

    宓妃

    106

    有书生家洛水之旁，好义任侠。书斋假寐，梦青衣来告曰：“洛神宓妃使下妾致命，以君之高义，将申不量之诚，已至门外矣。”生亟趋出迎，见洛神飘然降车，服饰姿容果有如曹子建所赋者，侍者十馀辈，率皆妍丽风华。相见礼成，生启曰：“尘凡下士，久企仙颇，无由展谒，何幸凌波之步竞贲蓬庐！将何所命”
    　　妃低鬟敛袂，貌若含愁，半晌乃言曰：“妾以鄙陋，嫠处鲛宫。每虑沧海澜狂，自防如玉。黄初三年，偶逾闲束。税履江皋，邂逅东阿，不及掩避。初未尝流连盼睐，致蹈解珮之嫌。乃东阿词人好为夸饰，妍词艳语，借局抒才，致“惊鸿”“游龙”之谈，为轻薄者所藉齿。而临济刘伯玉者，竟雒诵于其妻段氏明光之前，加以亵语，遂致触怒悍妇，舍命通津，欲效介氏之尤，凭泉羹而为厉。阳侯长者，任其作威，竟得窃据湫潭，役使鳞介。而应以美人得渡者，咸毁容妆，乃占既济。自太始以来，千有馀年，皓齿青蛾未有敢婴其妒鳞者也。

    Explanation

    106

    A scholar lived by the Luo River, known for his love of righteousness and his role as a righteous warrior. One day, while taking a nap in his study, he dreamed of a young man in green clothes who came to inform him, saying, "The goddess of the Luo River, Lady Fu Xi, has sent me to deliver a message. Because of your noble virtue, I will express my unworthy sincerity. She has already arrived at the door." The scholar hurriedly went out to meet her and saw the goddess of the Luo River descend gracefully from her carriage. Her attire and appearance were indeed as described by Cao Zhi in his poem, and there were over ten attendants, all of whom were beautiful and elegant. After the formalities of the meeting were completed, the scholar began to speak, saying, "A lowly scholar, who has long admired the immortals, has no way to pay respects. How fortunate it is that your graceful steps have reached my humble thatched cottage! What do you wish to command?"

    The goddess lowered her head and gathered her sleeves, her face seeming to carry a hint of sorrow. After a while, she spoke, "I am humble and live alone in the palace of the sea goddess. I often worry about the turbulent waves of the vast ocean and protect myself like a precious jade. In the third year of the Huangchu era, I happened to exceed the bounds of propriety. I stepped onto the bank of the river and met Dong'e by chance, without time to hide. Initially, I did not linger or look around, so as not to incur the suspicion of removing my jade pendant. However, the poets of Dong'e often exaggerate, using beautiful and elegant words to express their talents, leading to discussions of 'startled swans' and 'swimming dragons,' which are the subject of idle gossip and ridicule. Liu Boyu of Linji, in particular, recited these verses before his wife Duan Mingguang, adding disrespectful language, which enraged her. She risked her life to cross the river, intending to emulate Jie, and became a spirit of the water, causing harm. Yang Hou, the legendary sea god, allowed her to wield power, and she secretly occupied a deep pool, commanding fish and shellfish. Those who cross the river with the help of a beautiful woman all destroy their appearance, and this is the hexagram of Jiji. Since the beginning of the world, over a thousand years have passed, and no woman with white teeth and green eyebrows has dared to provoke her jealousy."

    107

    “鱼腹馀妖，不自愧耻，渐乃迁怒于妾，飞语横加。初无睚眦小怨，竟成骨髓深仇。妾惟是风马牛之不相及，未虞寇至，曾不以龙武三军当此之时剪除凶牝。优容过当，养祸蓄奸，致滋蔓之难图，悔噬脐之无及。段妇啸聚日多，悍流蜂起。延平六虎，尽为爪牙，狮吼鸠盘，所在响应。蹂躏我边陲，杀伤我将吏。河洛之间安澜日久，刻期征调，惶惑奔逃。采旄桂旗，无以敌虎狼之众，遂使凭陵所至，鳞介之属靡有孑遗。往者发使遮须，告急于国王曹植，且责以文坛不戢，厥口兴戎。曹王愧谢，大诘戎兵，倾国之众克期赴援。妒贼自度不支，闻风宵遁。援师既返，乘间复来。虽曹王念邻衅之由己，恤与国之多难，一介乞师，无役不赴，而寇情诡秘，窃发无时，劳师远来，无功而返，彼既岁疲于奔命，我亦虚縻其供亿。

    Explanation

    107

    The lingering evil of the fish belly did not cause shame, and gradually shifted its anger towards me, adding slandering words. Initially, there was no minor grudge or resentment, but it eventually became a deep-seated grudge. I alone was unrelated, not expecting the enemy to come, and did not use the powerful Dragon Army at that time to eliminate the wicked female. Excessive tolerance nurtured evil and harbored wickedness, leading to a situation that was difficult to control, and regret to the point of self-inflicted harm. The group of women led by Duan gathered more and more, and brutal forces rose like a swarm of bees. The six tigers of Yanping all became pawns, with chaotic and destructive forces responding everywhere. They trampled on our border regions, killing and injuring our generals and officials. The river and Luo regions had been peaceful for a long time, but mobilizing troops at a set time led to panic and fleeing. The flags and banners of the army could not match the fierce and dangerous enemy, so wherever they advanced, no survivors remained among the fish and shellfish. In the past, an envoy was sent to Zhe Xu, appealing for help to King Cao Zhi, and accusing the literary world of not being repressed, starting a war through words. King Cao Zhi felt ashamed and apologized, mobilizing a large army, and the entire nation's forces arrived for assistance at a set time. The jealous bandits realized they could not withstand, fleeing at night upon hearing the news. The relief army returned, but taking advantage of the opportunity, they returned again. The enemy was tired from constant rushing around, and we also wasted resources on provisions.

    108

    “顷闻羽檄驰告，临济之师又将压境，妾欲募召义勇，济师益甲，秉其无备，先发制胜。义旗久建，赴难无人．而海内雕鸷之徒多为敌用，疾风暴浪，可为寒心。事之成败，身之安危，在此役也，先生心存济弱，义在锄凶，故敢特布腹心，觌面之羞所不能避，惟先生图之！”

    Explanation

    108

    I have recently heard the urgent dispatch of military orders, and the army from Linji is about to invade the border. I wish to recruit brave volunteers to reinforce our troops and increase their numbers, taking advantage of the enemy's lack of preparation to gain the upper hand and win the battle. The righteous flag has been raised for a long time, but no one is willing to face danger. Within the empire, the fierce and cunning people mostly serve the enemy, and the sudden and violent storms can be heart-wrenching. The success or failure of the matter, and the safety or danger of one's body, depend on this campaign. You have the heart to help the weak and the righteousness to eliminate evil, so I dare to share my innermost thoughts, and the shame of facing you directly cannot be avoided. Please consider it, Mr. [Mr. X].

    109

    生曰：“凶悍之恶，人有同心。惜玉书生，尤所深疾。苟能仗助，敢惮勤劳？第恐水陆殊途，显晦异迹，虽众，无所用之耳。”妃曰：“不然。昔泾川节度周宝，遣郑承符将兵，赴九娘子之难，使朝冄受缚，善女奠安，古今称其侠烈。柳生仗义，寄书洞庭君。钱塘奋怒，吞噬泾阳，骨肉再合，柳生获卢女之报。书传所载，不可诬也。诚能掉三寸之舌，乞一旅之师，屯戍水滨，为犄角之势，相机策应，进可以攻，退可以守，是先生以齿牙馀论，安全弱孺，而有大造于巾帼也。妾虽不慧，其敢忘德？”生问所需甲马之数，曰：“得轻骑三千足矣，，皆军帖除名，无所复用于人间者。”生故与戎阃相善，计可惜兵，遂许诺。妃谢而去，

    Explanation

    109

    The young man said, "The evil of being fierce and cruel is something that people share in common. I particularly detest scholars who value purity or nobility. If one can rely on assistance, how dare one fear hard work? Only I fear that the paths of water and land are different, and appearances and truths are different. Even if there are many, they are useless." The princess replied, "That is not so. In the past, the governor of Jingzhou, Zhou Bao, sent Zheng Chengfu to lead troops to the rescue of Princess Nine, causing Chao Ruan to be bound and ensuring the safety of a virtuous woman. From ancient times to the present, he is praised for his chivalry and bravery. Liu Sheng stood up for justice by sending a letter to the Lord of Dongting Lake. Qiantang became furious and devoured Jingyang, and Liu Sheng reunited with his family and received a reward from Lady Lu. What is recorded in books and traditions cannot be false or deceitful. If one can use their eloquent speech to request a small army, garrison at the water's edge, form a strategic position, take advantage of opportunities, attack when advancing, and defend when retreating, then the master uses his remaining influence to ensure the safety of the weak and young, making a great contribution to women. Although I am not wise, how could I forget the virtue?" The young man asked about the number of light cavalry needed, and she said, "Three thousand light cavalry would be sufficient, all of whom are former soldiers who have been discharged and are no longer useful in the world." The young man had previously been friendly with the military commandant and, considering the wastefulness of using soldiers, agreed. The princess thanked him and left.

    110

    生倏然而寤，深以为异。先是，水中常有黑风卷浪，势若山崩，岁辄数四，乃悟妒妇之相侵也。遂诣总帅言之。帅素重生，不以为妄。生复思，曰：“妃言须轻骑三千，皆已除名军帖，当是已死者，不然，生人赴水，将何所用耶”帅亦以为然，遂籍已殁军士，得若干，牒送洛水。
    　　越数日，风浪如前。生复梦妃遣青衣来曰：“妃主蒙君恤患，赐以貔貅，悉隶于虎贲将洛子渊戏下。洛君将略非其所谙，又兼新集之众未经简练，骤遇狂氛，仓卒逆战，不能指挥将士、参用机权，夜屯无备，为贼掩袭，三战三北，挫折军锋。妃主忧危，计无所出，故遣下妾请命于先生，抒兹大难。知先生素优韬略，用策如神，久欲斩毒龙、搏脂虎，旁雪不平，为天下快。此正用武之秋，建功之日也，幸勿以他词委焉。”生闻之，怒甚，奋衣而起，谓青衣曰：“有是哉！吾往矣，誓当竭其微力，缚临济幺麽，致之阶下，以雪妃主之恨也！”遂随青衣出。

    Explanation

    110

    The protagonist suddenly awoke, deeply feeling it as strange. Before this, there were often black winds swirling waves in the water, the force was like a mountain collapsing, occurring several times each year. He then realized that it was the invasion by a jealous woman. He went to the general to report it. The general always valued him and did not think it was a fabrication. The protagonist pondered again, saying, "The princess said she needed three thousand light cavalry, all of whom had been removed from the military roster. It must be those who have already died. Otherwise, living people going into the water, what would they be used for?" The general also thought so, so he registered the deceased soldiers, obtained a certain number, and dispatched them to the Luo River.

    After several days, the wind and waves were as before. The protagonist had another dream where the princess sent a maid to say, "Under your care, the princess is suffering. She was bestowed upon fierce beasts and all were under the command of General Luo Ziyuan. General Luo's strategies are not well-known to him, and moreover, the newly assembled troops were not well-trained. Suddenly encountering fierce enemies, they engaged in battle hastily, unable to command the troops or use strategic flexibility. Unprepared for night encampment, they were ambushed by the enemy, suffering three battles and three retreats, breaking the morale of the troops. The princess is in great distress, with no solution in sight. Therefore, she sent me to ask for your help to alleviate this great difficulty. Knowing that you are skilled in military strategy, using strategies like a god, long wanting to slay the poisonous dragon and fight the fat tiger, to avenge injustices, to bring joy to the world. This is the time for martial action and the day to build achievements. Please do not refuse with other excuses." The protagonist heard this, became very angry, stood up, and said to the maid, "Is that so! I will go, vowing to exhaust my meager strength, bind the small and insignificant ones, bring them to the steps below, to avenge the princess's grievance!" He then followed the maid out.

    111

    已有旌节甲马之属在门，须臾而至。翠栋虹楣，台阁玲珑。见妃泪容可掬，生前拜，妃亦答拜，坐生于宾位而陈词曰：“选将不慎，挠败新军，故收合馀烬以待先生，为破釜沉船之计。以先生瑰才胜算，当此妖狐，如扫尘振落耳。”因问剿贼之略。生曰：“我山新集之众，当远涉之师，宜警守以待其弊。子渊意在速战，已违戎经，且又防御不周，为贼所乘，是以有前日之败。今寇已深入，不可复缓，缓将失机。盖新败之后，彼料我怯，谓将退保穷城，防我必怠。若以精卒宵加于彼，可以得志。”妃深然之，因命金瓯取酒，为生壮行。

    Explanation

    111

    Military symbols and equipment, including banners and armored horses, were already present at the gate, arriving shortly thereafter. The palace or hall was adorned with green beams and rainbow lintels, its intricate and elegant structure a testament to its grandeur. Upon seeing the princess's tearful face, the general bowed before her, and she returned the gesture. He took his seat as a guest and began to speak: "The selection of generals was hasty, leading to the disruption and defeat of the newly formed army. Therefore, we have gathered the remaining forces to await your arrival, ready to commit fully to this battle. With your extraordinary talent and strategic acumen, facing this formidable foe is as easy as sweeping away dust and fallen leaves." The princess then asked about the strategy for defeating the rebels. The general replied, "Our newly assembled forces, facing a distant enemy, should maintain vigilance and wait for their weaknesses. The previous commander favored a quick battle, violating military principles, and with inadequate defense, was taken advantage of by the enemy, leading to the previous defeat. Now that the enemy has penetrated deeply, we cannot afford to delay, for delay will result in lost opportunities. After a recent defeat, the enemy likely assumes we are cowardly and will retreat to a besieged city, believing we will become complacent. If we send our elite troops to launch a surprise attack at night, we can achieve victory." The princess deeply agreed with the strategy and ordered the golden bowl to fetch wine, to strengthen the general's resolve before his departure.

    112

    生饮讫，即躬擐甲胄，精选士马。初更之后，犯其前军，人不及门，遂拔之。次日，复整三军，将与决战。贼闻新帅善兵，尽皆胆落，使谍来侦，为逻骑所执，因尽吐彼军虚实。于是分布要害，设状诱之。伪以赢师搦战，诈败而南。贼轻敌无谋，并力前逐。鼓噪一声，伏兵云集，转战夹攻，赋师披摩。斩首数万级，乘胜追袭，猝临贼境。时贼全军尽出，堡戌皆空，历至城守莫不望旗归命。段妇遣使请降，生不许，亲率吏士亟攻，意在生获。围其三面，故解其西，诱其出而擒之。段妇果弃城而奔，匹马潜逃。飞骑前遮，缚于郊野。遂大索其巢，馀党尽获。露布驰闻，临济波臣率众称贺，大犒兵士，振旅而还。

    Explanation

    112

    After drinking, the general immediately donned his armor and helmet, carefully selecting his troops and horses. After the first watch of the night, he attacked the enemy's vanguard, defeating them before they could reach the gate. The next day, he reorganized his entire army, preparing for a decisive battle. The enemy, hearing of the new general's military prowess, all lost their courage. They sent spies to scout, but these were captured by the patrol cavalry, who then revealed all the enemy's strengths and weaknesses. The general then deployed his forces at strategic points and set up a ruse to lure them. He pretended to send a weak force to provoke a battle and feigned a retreat to the south. The enemy, overconfident and lacking strategy, charged forward together. With a loud shout and the sound of drums, the ambush troops gathered like clouds, fighting in successive engagements and attacking from both sides, cutting down the enemy forces. They decapitated tens of thousands, pursuing their victory and suddenly arriving at the enemy's territory. At that time, the enemy's entire army had left their forts and outposts, and every city and stronghold surrendered upon seeing the flag. The woman leader sent envoys to surrender, but the general refused, personally leading officials and soldiers to attack urgently, intending to capture them alive. He surrounded them on three sides, deliberately leaving the west open, luring them out to capture them. The woman leader indeed abandoned the city and fled on a single horse in secret. Fast cavalry blocked the way ahead, tying her up in the countryside. Then, they thoroughly searched their lair, capturing all the remaining followers. A report was sent by express courier, and the officials of Linji and the people celebrated, greatly rewarding the soldiers and returning with their troops in order.

    113

    宓妃率侍女百馀人迎于郊，笑豔欢颇，丰姿愈绝。沩辞慰劳，感谢再三。并辔还宫，与生登楼受俘。引囚于楼前，数其辜罪，唯叩头鸣哀，摇尾乞命。妃殊不忍，遂欲贷以不死。生笑曰：“真神仙之度也！虽然，挑兵首祸，天有常刑。无滋败类，实巡处此。宜论置极典，以彰法纪。”遂命押赴市曹，车裂以徇。其馀凶党，悉皆伏诛。段氏临刑而叹曰：“向怒伯玉，冀得为神以报之。岂料为神，犹有今日。临济晚渡，可得见乎！”闻者嗤之。
    　　论囚既毕，饮至策勋，欲封生以三万户，宝玉之赠不可纪极，诸将士赏赉有差。生辞曰：“排难解纷而无所取者，鲁连之义也。某激于区区之志，攘臂而来，岂为此乎”妃曰：“义哉！虽然，恩之不报，人其谓我何”生不得已，受珍器数事，馀悉却之。妃复以白壁二、水犀一，骊珠四，鲛绡六，托生致总帅，以报其假师之惠。

    Explanation

    113

    宓妃率领一百多名侍女在郊外迎接，笑容满面，气氛欢快，她的美丽更加出众。生辞谢慰劳，再三表示感谢。他们并辔返回宫殿，一同登上楼阁接受俘虏。将囚犯带到楼前，列举他们的罪行，囚犯们只是叩头哀鸣，摇尾乞命。宓妃非常不忍心，想要赦免他们不死。生笑着说：“真是神仙的气度啊！然而，他们挑起战争，制造祸端，天有常刑。不应助长恶人，这里正是他们该受的地方。应该判处最严厉的刑罚，以彰显法纪。”于是下令将他们押送到市集，车裂示众。其余的凶徒，全部被处死。段氏临刑时叹息说：“我曾对伯玉生气，希望能成为神灵来报复他。没想到成为神灵，还有今天的遭遇。临济晚渡，还能见到吗？”听到的人嗤笑他。
    　　审问囚犯完毕后，举行庆功宴，打算封生为三万户侯，赠送的宝玉多得无法计数，将士们也各有赏赐。生推辞说：“排难解纷而不求回报，这是鲁连的义举。我因小小的志向，奋勇而来，难道是为了这些吗？”宓妃说：“真是义士啊！然而，恩惠不报，别人会怎么说我？”生不得已，接受了几个珍贵的器物，其余的全部拒绝。宓妃又赠送了两块白璧、一只水犀、四颗骊珠、六匹鲛绡，委托生带给总帅，以报答他借兵的恩惠。

    114

    于是开筵张乐，极其丰备。妃捧觞而起，为生寿曰：“先生义勇所加，穷涧立涸，枭雄授首。维泽国万世之安，雪玉台千秋之恨。恩同再造，毕世莫酬！”生曰：“上帝彰美刑淫，假手于人，儒者任之。故巫峡之雪、瑶台之露，兰桥碧洞之花月，率皆见于文章，形诸歌咏，天下后世，罔不知闻。虽以妃主之幽贞，无从伺影，而陈思忽然觏止，作赋留传，翠羽明珠，恰传阿堵。此皆天假之缘，使昭其美，而欲世间之知有妃也。至于妒忌之流，役夫若仆，嫉美如仇，持杖窥帘，奏刀发披，呈丑于广众之地，扬秽于远近之口，顿使正士兴叹，词人发忿，口诛笔诮，怒及枯骨。

    Explanation

    114

    Here is the final translation of the source text:

    A grand banquet was set up, and music was played to the utmost in abundance. The lady raised a cup and stood up, toasting to the hero’s longevity: “Through your valor and courage, even the deepest ravines were dried up, and the fierce and cunning yielded their heads. You have secured the eternal peace of the land and cleared the ancient grudge of the Jade Terrace. Your grace is akin to rebirth, and your gratitude is unrepayable for a lifetime!” The hero replied: “The Heavenly Lord punishes evil and rewards virtue, using others as instruments, and it is the duty of scholars to uphold this. Thus, the snow of the Wuxia Gorge, the dew of the Jade Terrace, and the flowers and moonlight of the orchid bridge and emerald caves—all have appeared in writings and been expressed in songs and poems, known to the world and future generations. Though the secluded and chaste lady was elusive, the sudden encounter with Cao Zhi led to the creation of a famous ode that has been passed down, with green feathers and pearls, precisely transmitted through this. All of this is a divine arrangement, meant to reveal her beauty and make the world know of her existence. As for the jealous ones, servants and laborers who hate beauty as an enemy, who peek through curtains with a staff and cut and expose, displaying ugliness in public and spreading filth in distant and near mouths, they have caused the upright to lament and poets to express anger, criticizing with words and writing scathing remarks, with anger reaching the bones.

    115

    虽决西江之水以洗秽，持南岳之山以包羞。岂能喻其万一者哉！若斯之故，是谓天刑。然或由顽钝无耻之夫，薄悻无良之子，激发其豺狼之怒，酝酿其蜂虿之毒，以至于斯极，固未有迁怒神仙，宣威津度，直以一妒上下千古，如段妇明光者也。负固水乡，已历千霜，未遭谴戮，而犹不戢思逞，几欲渎罗袜之尘，罪实贯盈，正宜歼灭。故某得上藉妃灵，下资兵力，捣其巢穴，歼厥渠魁，一鼓而平，无亡矢遣镞之费。天之所命，非人力矣！且身隶幅员，曾不能投鞭拂剑，预截横瀛，扫荡腥秽，而使摇撼帷薄、震恐环佩，皆某之愆也，敢自功乎”乃亦酌酒奉妃。妃为连钦数觥。红妆数百人，皆次第奉生酒，献酬次畅。

    Explanation

    115

    Even if the waters of the West River were drained to cleanse the filth, and the mountains of South Yue were carried to contain the shame, how could one possibly convey even a fraction of the situation! If such is the case, it is called heavenly punishment. Yet, it may be due to the stubborn and shameless, the ungrateful and wicked, who incite the rage of wolves and tigers, brew the venom of scorpions and wasps, to such an extreme, there has never been a case where anger is transferred to the gods, power proclaimed at the river crossings, but rather a single jealousy that spans thousands of years, like the woman of Mingguang. Having endured thousands of frosts in a watery stronghold, yet not yet facing punishment, and still harboring ambitions to act out, almost wanting to defile the dust of the embroidered socks, the crime is so heinous that it warrants complete eradication. Therefore, I rely on the divine power of the goddess above, and supported by military strength below, attack their stronghold, eliminate their leaders, and achieve victory in one decisive battle, without any loss of arrows or casualties. It is the will of heaven, not human effort! Moreover, being part of a vast domain, I could not even cast my whip or unsheathe my sword to preemptively cut off the chaos across the sea, sweep away the stench and filth, and yet cause disturbance and fear, all of which are my faults, how dare I claim credit? Thus, I also pour wine and present it to the goddess. The goddess drinks several cups in succession. Hundreds of women in red attire take turns presenting fresh wine, and the exchange of toasts and drinks becomes lively.

    116

    俄传江妃湘君湘夫人等来贺战胜，皆飚车羽轮，云衣霞佩，咸向生敛衽，美睿之词不可胜纪。已而湘灵为鼓瑟，江妃为起舞，极音节神态之妙，真使苍梧云停、汉皋月白，殆非语言所能喻矣！及暮别去。
    　　生次日亦辞归。妃知不可留，徘徊眷恋，凄然泪落。顾视诸女，亦皆神意酸楚。生于是亦惘惘有恨别之色。妃谓生曰：“后二十年，君当厌弃富贵，服食还仙，此妾与君相见之秋也。君但志之，无探憾于此行。”遂以旌旗甲仗、舆马侍从，送生还家。

    Explanation

    116

    Shortly after, the goddesses of the Xiang River, including the Lady of the Xiang River and the Lady of the Xiang River's Consort, came to congratulate on the victory. They arrived in vehicles that moved like the wind, with feathers or light wheels, and were dressed in clothes made of clouds and accessories made of rosy clouds. They all bowed respectfully to the protagonist. Their beautiful and wise words were so numerous that they could not be fully recorded. Soon after, the Lady of the Xiang River played the se, and the Lady of the Xiang River's Consort danced. The performance was perfect in tone and expression, truly making the clouds over Mount Cangwu still and the moon over Han Gao bright, almost impossible to describe with words. At dusk, they departed.

    The next day, the protagonist also bid farewell and returned home. The goddess knew she could not keep him, so she lingered, reluctant to part, and her tears fell. Looking at the other goddesses, they were all emotionally distressed. The protagonist also showed a somewhat sad and regretful expression at parting. The goddess said to the protagonist, "Twenty years later, you will become disillusioned with wealth and power and choose to live a life of asceticism to achieve immortality. This will be the season when we meet again. Just remember this, and do not feel regret about this journey." She then sent him away with flags and banners, carriages and horses with attendants.

    117

    砉然一声，生乃惊寤。家人环泣，言“已昏睡七日矣，呼之不醒，惟气末绝耳”。生具告之。家人复报东轩有宝物无数，耀目充庭。视之，即妃向所赠，盖妃以生廉、不欲多取，故俟其归而尽致之也。
    　　生后仕至郡守，颇思宓妃言，乃齐官归，行导引之术。一日诀家人，大笑投洛水中死。数年后，有人见生与数丽人游于水上。
    　　周斯盛
    　　周斯盛者，金溪诸生也，有善行。夜睡，辄梦为某县城隍。一夜。方折鬼狱，忽传天榜发。急往视之，白纸墨书，宛如人间乡榜。乃见己名为解首，有朱印盖其姓，模糊不甚可辨。而同乡某某，皆榜中人也。觉后记之，举以语人。已而乡闱揭晓，而捷音不至。后闻解首乃晏斯盛耳，始悟朱印之故。馀悉如言。
    　　跨卫者
    　　东乡诸生陈某，方夜读书，闻东邻妇姑诟谇，邻人归而掴其妇，妇喁喁哭泣不休。少顷，户外池水抨然有声，疑邻妇忿而自溺，亟呼家人救之，果曳一人起。

    Explanation

    117

    A sudden sharp sound startled him awake. His family gathered around, weeping, saying, "You have been in a deep sleep for seven days, calling you did not wake you, only your breath had not yet ceased." He told them everything. The family then reported that there were countless treasures in the eastern pavilion, dazzling and filling the courtyard. Upon seeing them, he realized they were the gifts given by Lady Fu, who, because of his frugality and not wanting him to take too much, had waited for his return to give them all to him.
