To forget oneself to benefit others is the highest ideal; to retreat from the world to preserve oneself is the next best thing. I had no choice but to come out of this state temporarily, why do you envy this? And if you truly tire of the cage-like world, then rectify yourself, focus your will, concentrate your spirit, follow reason and resist desire, preserve your true nature and conform to the Way, how is it not possible to become a sage? I respectfully sweep away the white clouds, waiting for you to sit down. My humble intentions are fully expressed here!"

    Feng nodded in agreement. Xiu then stood up and led Feng out of the house, through a narrow path, winding downward. Looking down, they saw waves and sails filling the river. Xiu pointed to a boat anchored there and said, "You can climb aboard!" After speaking, he suddenly disappeared. Feng dazedly climbed aboard and found it was the old boat. The boatman then said that after Feng left, the boat was driven here by strong winds. They then paddled westward and had already reached the Huai and Si rivers. They returned to Luoyang.

    389

    西坡逸叟
    　　西坡逸叟，渭河狐仙也。元时隐山中。至洪武己巳，采药山阳。见黑风起艮方，须臾四障，天地昏黝，秽气扑鼻。少焉，天宇寥青，红云绚晚，恍惚有物，露爪弩空，巨声雷吼，振动林野。叟乃悚恐，疾走还山。回望西方，浓云复布，猛雨继集，雷訇电烁，冰雹凌风，山谷喧撼。复觉秽气扑鼻如前，叟颇遑遽，倥偬斜行。

    389

    The Recluse of the West Slope

    The Recluse of the West Slope was a fox spirit from the Wei River. During the Yuan dynasty, he hid in the mountains. In the year Hongwu Ji Si (1379), he went to gather herbs on the sunny side of the mountain. He saw a black wind rise from the northeast, and in an instant, the four directions were blocked, the sky and earth became dark and gloomy, and an unpleasant odor filled the nose. After a short while, the sky turned clear and blue, red clouds illuminated the evening sky, and something vague and indistinct appeared, revealing claws in the sky, accompanied by a loud roar like thunder that shook the forest and fields. The recluse was frightened and terrified, and quickly returned to the mountain. Looking back toward the west, he saw thick clouds gather again, heavy rain followed immediately, thunder roared and lightning flashed, hailstones flew in the wind, and the valleys were shaken and roared. He felt the unpleasant odor again, was greatly anxious and hurried, and moved hurriedly at an angle.

    390

    不半里许，遇一道士，手持桃枝，问：“叟何往？此时尚采药乎？”叟未及对，道士即手中擘一小桃枝，授叟曰：“此可当兵。”方欲问讯，道士已杳。叟不胜嗟异，视桃枝已化铁矛，六叉犀利，寒威可栗。霹雳再作，欻有一蛙，大可埒牛，血口盆张，举爪向叟。叟即挥矛当之，蛙被创脑裂，血膏涂地。叟亦颓倦，弛然僵卧。乃见前道士骑龙按虎而来，谓叟曰：“余，孙真人也，今来贺尔！”已闻异音仙籁，缭绕霄汉间。遂有癰缯一幅，斜被叟体。道士又遗一葫芦，内贮神丹，曰：服此长生。”于是凌云摄虚，顷刻已逝。叟亦惊觉，梦中所受，宛然在焉。又于身边得黄纸，大书“助天仙翁”四字，盖以旌其杀蛙之功也。

    Explanation

    390

    Not half a mile away, the old man encountered a Taoist, who held a peach branch and asked, "Where are you going, old man? Are you still gathering herbs at this time?" The old man had not yet answered when the Taoist immediately broke off a small peach branch from his hand and gave it to the old man, saying, "This can serve as a weapon." Just as the old man was about to ask more, the Taoist had already vanished. The old man could not help but marvel, looking and seeing that the peach branch had turned into an iron spear, six-pronged and sharp, its cold presence was terrifying. Thunder roared again, and suddenly a frog appeared, as large as a cow, with a blood-filled mouth wide open, raising its claws toward the old man. The old man immediately struck it with the spear, and the frog was wounded, its brain split, blood and gore covering the ground. The old man was also exhausted and weary, collapsed and lay stiffly. Then he saw the previous Taoist riding a dragon and taming a tiger, coming to him and saying, "I am Master Sun, I have come to congratulate you!" Already, strange, divine sounds were heard, winding around the heavens. Then there was a piece of silk, slanted over the old man's body. The Taoist also left a gourd, containing divine pills, saying, "Eat these to achieve immortality." Soaring through the clouds and void, in an instant he had vanished. The old man also woke up in a start, what he received in the dream was still clearly present. Also, he found yellow paper beside himself, with the four characters "Helper of the Heavenly Immortal Old Man" written in large letters, to commend his achievement in killing the frog.

    391

    叟自是服丹勤修，遂登仙箓。乾隆辛亥四月，某贵家扶鸾，叟降坛自述颠末如此。主人录其语，余为诠次焉。
    　　交物
    　　劳生者，好与物交。尝谓交物莫妙于鸡，莫凶于犬。昔尝交犬，其阴如炙，大病数月，服参蓍乃愈。真畏途也。由是出犬而宠鸡。其他羽蹄雌牝，多充下陈。尝欲汙象，见其庞然大，逡巡退去，象得以贞。
    　　癞　虾　蟆
    　　癞虾蟆，蛙属之至陋者也。然其为用，能啖蜈蚣而囚蚊，不可以貌废也。张口向蜈蚣，蜈蚣伏不动，徐徐入其口中，而出于尻窍。复回身张口向中，又出入如前者三四，遂葬其腹矣。咀嚼之法，未有奇于此者。既啖数头，乃强饲以丹砂，缄其口而倒悬之，以盘承其涎。既尽，放去，取笔濡诞，画圈于纸，着壁上，室中之蚊咸集圈中。去其圈，蚊复飞去。

    Explanation

    391

    The Old Man, since then, diligently took elixirs and practiced spiritual cultivation, and thus ascended to the list of immortals. In the fourth month of the year Xinhai during the reign of Emperor Qianlong, a certain noble family performed a divination ritual with a pendulum, and the old man descended to the altar and narrated the entire process as follows. The host recorded his words, and I arranged them accordingly.

    Interacting with Things
    Those who are weary of life enjoy interacting with objects. I once said that among things, nothing is better than interacting with chickens, and nothing is worse than interacting with dogs. I once interacted with a dog, and its yin energy was like roasting, causing me to suffer a serious illness for several months. I only recovered after taking ginseng and mugwort. Truly, it was a fearful path. Therefore, I got rid of the dog and treasured the chicken. Other birds and animals, male and female, mostly filled the lower ranks. I once tried to defile an elephant, saw that it was huge, hesitated, and retreated, allowing the elephant to remain chaste.

    The Toad
    The toad is the most ugly among the frog family. Yet in terms of its function, it can eat centipedes and trap mosquitoes. It cannot be discarded because of its appearance. It opens its mouth towards the centipede, and the centipede lies still. Slowly, it enters its mouth and comes out through its anus. Turning around and opening its mouth again, it repeats the process several times, and finally buries it in its belly. The method of chewing is nothing more extraordinary than this. After eating several, it was forced to feed it cinnabar, sealed its mouth, and hung it upside down, catching its saliva in a tray. After it was done, it was released, and a brush was dipped in the saliva to draw a circle on the paper, placed on the wall, and all the mosquitoes in the room gathered in the circle. Removing the circle, the mosquitoes flew away again.

    392

    范依
    　　童子范依，从塾师受书。值清明，师归展墓，依窃游郭外。遇少女偕一媪，共坐小车。依悦而尾之。车甚疾，苦弗能及。数里，御者渴，息辙山下，就近村取饮。时微雨新晴，女见杜鹃已开，映山红烂，使媪下折之。依迹至，闻其语，适持一束，遂以赠女，女笑而受之。依挑以微词。女不应，然时时却扇流睐。顷之媪来。见女手中花，讶所从来。女惭，掷之地，而依尚立车右，低徊不去。媪悟，叱之曰：“何物郎子，辄敢贻阿姑花耶？”依惶恐失措，长揖媪前。媪亦笑怜其韶幼，徐问其里居姓名，及执业。依具以实对，女私志之。御者至，遂推车以去。依亦惆怅归塾。
    　　窃告其友，友漫绐之曰：“此近村张朝奉女，名莹者也，闻尚待字，宜求之。”依以为信，欲往访。而师督颇严，久不得闲，乃取尺笺书莹名，置诸枕函，饮食必祝，夜则焚香而礼之。

    Explanation

    392

    A Boy Named Fan Yi

    The young boy Fan Yi studied under a private tutor. On the occasion of the Qingming Festival, the tutor returned home to visit the family tomb, and Fan Yi secretly wandered outside the city walls. He met a young girl accompanied by an elderly woman, who were riding in a small carriage together. The boy was pleased and followed them. The carriage was very fast, and he struggled to keep up. After a few miles, the driver became thirsty and stopped by the roadside at the foot of a mountain to go to a nearby village for water. It was just after a light rain had stopped, and the girl saw the azaleas blooming, their red flowers glowing. She asked the elderly woman to pick some. Fan Yi followed them to the spot and heard what they were saying. Just then, he happened to be holding a bunch of flowers and gave them to the girl. The girl smiled and accepted them. The boy tried to engage her in light conversation. The girl did not respond, but she often glanced at him with a smile behind her fan. Soon the elderly woman returned, saw the flowers in the girl's hand, and was surprised at where they came from. The girl felt embarrassed and threw them on the ground. But the boy still stood on the right side of the carriage, hesitating and not leaving. The elderly woman realized and scolded him, saying, "What kind of young man are you, daring to give flowers to my young lady?" The boy was terrified and at a loss, and bowed deeply before the elderly woman. The elderly woman also smiled and pitied his youth, and slowly asked about his hometown and name, and what he did for a living. The boy answered truthfully, and the girl secretly remembered him. The driver arrived, and they pushed the carriage away. The boy also returned to the schoolhouse in a melancholy mood.

    He told his friends in secret, and they jokingly told him, "This is the daughter of Mr. Zhang from the nearby village, named Ying, who is still unmarried. You should pursue her." The boy believed them and wanted to go visit her. But the teacher was very strict, and he couldn't find time. So he took a small piece of paper and wrote her name on it, and put it in a small box near his pillow. He would pray to her during meals, and at night, he would burn incense and pay respects to her.

    393

    会端午节近，师徒散归。依即如近村，访得张朝奉，果有次女名莹者。潜登其角门，适遇媪。媪识之曰：“若非范生耶？”依应曰：“是也。”媪悄语之曰：“阿姑为汝病矣，宜速以媒来！”依唯唯求见女。媪纳诸曲室，以告女。女不可，便媪贻之帨，促之去，亦属曰：“宜速以媒来！”媪复导依出。
    　　是日朝奉访远亲未还。仆婢皆游河上观竞渡，故依得入出，张氏卒无知者。依既归，不得已自以意白母。母怒诃之曰：“汝好读汝书，固将娶也，终不听汝自求妇。”依羞窘无策，亦成疾。母悔之，为媒诸张氏，张氏弗许。依闻之，疾转笃。女命亦剧。遂同日俱死。已而先后俱苏。张氏喜曰：“女苏矣！”女张目曰：“我依也，非莹也！”依苏，亦曰：“我莹也，非依也。”音情皆不类。盖已互易其舍矣。两家骇极，交验始信焉，遂婚为夫妇，男其情者女其身，女其情者男其形，一时以为异。

    Explanation

    393

    As the Dragon Boat Festival approached, the master and disciples dispersed to return home. Yi immediately went to a nearby village and found Zhang Chaofeng, who indeed had a second daughter named Ying. He secretly ascended the side gate and just happened to meet an old woman. The old woman recognized him and said, "Are you not Master Fan?" Yi replied, "Yes." The old woman whispered to him, "Your aunt is ill because of you; you should quickly send a matchmaker!" Yi nodded and asked to see the girl. The old woman led him into a small room and informed the girl. The girl refused, and the old woman gave her a handkerchief, urging her to leave, and also said, "You should quickly send a matchmaker!" The old woman then escorted Yi out.

    That day, Zhang Chaofeng had not yet returned from visiting distant relatives. The servants and maids were all out on the river watching the boat races, so Yi was able to come and go freely, and the Zhang family remained unaware. After returning home, Yi, unable to bear it any longer, decided to tell his mother. His mother angrily scolded him, saying, "You should focus on your studies, and I will arrange for you to marry; I will never allow you to seek a wife on your own." Yi felt ashamed and helpless, and soon fell ill. His mother regretted her words and arranged for a matchmaker to visit the Zhang family, but they refused. Upon hearing this, Yi's illness worsened. The girl's condition also became severe. They both died on the same day. Later, they both revived, one after the other. The Zhang family was overjoyed and said, "The girl has revived!" The girl opened her eyes and said, "I am Yi, not Ying!" When Yi revived, he also said, "I am Ying, not Yi!" Their voices and emotions were completely different. It turned out that they had switched places. Both families were shocked and, after verifying the situation, believed it to be true. They then married each other, with the man taking the girl's form and the girl taking the man's form, and at the time, it was considered a strange and remarkable event.

    394

    过期孕妇
    　　近传贾人某氏妇，孕十四月矣。其夫梦人语之曰：“若妇是月也，必诞，男也且贵，然若必毋出，出而妇产，妇产必杀儿。若必毋出！”其舅之梦亦云，遂信之。于是弥月皆不出，妇亦不产。亦渐疑其妾，父与子易而出焉。又五六月，如故也，皆曰：“梦妄耳。”遂同出。既出，妇腹痛产儿。儿堕地疾趋，视其面黟然黑也。妇骇甚，举物投之仆。旋起，又投之，复仆。而妇腹又痛，产儿面红色，直前掖黑面者起。妇愈骇，寻刀并斫杀之。腹又痛产儿，妇体已惫甚，不能运视，儿面则白色，行甚缓，就视前二儿皆已死，叹曰：“噫！”遂仆而绝。邻里多见之者。其后亦无他异焉。
    　　三　都　人
    　　某公性迂拙，偶出所居村外，迷不能归。遥见其季父耕田间，不识也。从之问途，而连呼之曰：“农、农。”季父怒且笑，戏应之曰：“血、血。”公即其处歇息，至暮始引归。

    Explanation

    394

    Overdue Pregnancy

    Recently, there was a story about a woman from a merchant family who was pregnant for fourteen months. Her husband had a dream in which someone told him, "Your wife will give birth this month. It will be a boy, and he will be noble, but you must not leave the house. If you do, and your wife gives birth, she will kill the child. You must not leave!" Her father-in-law also had the same dream, and they believed it. So, throughout the entire term, they did not leave the house, and the woman did not give birth. They gradually began to suspect the concubine, and the father and son exchanged places and left. After another five or six months, nothing changed, and they all said, "The dream was false." So, they finally went out together. After they left, the woman felt abdominal pain and gave birth to a child. The child fell to the ground and quickly moved, and it was seen that his face was black. The woman was extremely frightened, and she picked up an object and threw it at the child, who fell down. The child quickly got up, and she threw the object again, and the child fell down once more. Then, the woman felt abdominal pain again and gave birth to another child, whose face was red. The red-faced child walked forward and helped the black-faced child up. The woman was even more frightened, and she quickly found a knife and killed both children. The woman felt abdominal pain again and gave birth to another child, but her body was already very weak, and she could not move or see. The child had a white face and moved very slowly. When she looked at the previous two children, they were already dead. She sighed, "Ah!" and then collapsed and died. Many of the neighbors saw this. Afterward, there were no other strange occurrences.

    The Three Villagers

    A certain gentleman was by nature awkward and clumsy. Once, he went out to a village near his home and became lost, unable to find his way back. From a distance, he saw his youngest uncle plowing in a field, but he did not recognize him. He followed him and asked for directions, but he kept calling out, "Farmer, farmer." The uncle was both angry and amused and jokingly replied, "Blood, blood." The gentleman then rested there and did not return until evening.

    395

    有邻妇冬夜号寒，公意怜之。次日往谓曰：“汝无衾苦寒，盍就我宿？”妇大恚詈。公出，述其故于人曰：“彼妇殊不知恩。”闻者大笑。他日宰某邑，有讼者，三都人也。甫投牒，公怒即杖之。讼者请罪，公曰：“吾作秀才时，三都人屡负吾租，又诮辱我，故耳。”或告曰：“此三都非彼三都。”公悟，徐笑曰：“我真误，天下固有两三都也。”
    　　沈利梁仪
    　　湖州沈利，故笔贾也。后得秘制，遂不复贾，所制笔适用而寿可数十年。尝曰“选豪极难。”兔鼠诸物，望辄辨之，径拔其尤者一二豪，余悉弃去。曰：“豪稚故易损。凡吾所选豪，皆雪霜锻炼已经十年者，柔韧精劲。故卒不败。”累岁积豪，始足制一笔。其诜伐束缚之法，亦与他工异。惜不传矣。沈后不知所终。

    Explanation

    395

    In winter, a neighbor's wife was crying out in the cold, and the official felt pity for her. The next day, he went to her and said, "You have no quilt to keep you warm, why don't you come and stay with me?" The woman was greatly angered and cursed at him. The official left, and when he told others about what had happened, he said, "That woman is truly ungrateful." Those who heard this laughed. Later, when the official became the magistrate of a certain town, a litigant came forward, and he was from the Three Towns. As soon as he submitted his petition, the official became angry and beat him. The litigant asked for forgiveness, and the official said, "When I was a scholar, the people of the Three Towns often failed to pay my rent and also mocked and humiliated me, so I have this grudge." Someone told him, "This Three Towns is not the same as that Three Towns." The official realized his mistake and slowly smiled, saying, "I truly made a mistake; there are indeed multiple places called the Three Towns in the world."

    Shen Li and Liang Yi

    Shen Li of Huzhou was formerly a pen merchant. Later, he discovered a secret method and no longer engaged in trade. The pens he made were useful and could last for several decades. He once said, "Selecting the best hair is extremely difficult." For rabbits and mice, he could immediately distinguish the best ones and select just one or two, discarding the rest. He said, "Young hairs are more prone to damage. All the hairs I select have been tested by snow and frost for ten years, making them flexible, strong, and fine. Therefore, they never fail." It took years to accumulate enough hair to make one pen. His method of selecting and binding the hair was also different from others. Unfortunately, this method was not passed down. Shen Li's later fate is unknown.

    396

    同时有梁仪者，以制砚闻。梁仪者镇江人，或曰姓名盖逸矣。善制砚，严于选石，若沈利之选豪，虽端石略不佳者，辄置弗琢，率数岁乃成一砚。他日游某氏园，止花砌旁，指一石曰：“是可为砚。”某氏取付之。三年不成，一旦成之，命曰：“井田砚。”砚池中刻牛一头，墨堂为“井”字文，隐起如线。以归某氏，某氏睨之笑曰：“奇则奇矣。然分畦画轸，将牵牛而蹊田耶！”梁使试以水，则井字没与石平，牛亦转动如生，水干则复，始讶其神。梁曰：“吾因其材而琢之。不然则废井矣。向窃不得其制，是以久而弗成也。”窃叹精能如二子，所谓技进乎道者欤？李介夫述而称之，余为之合传。嗟乎！选之必精，用之必当，独笔砚乎哉？

    Explanation

    396

    During the same period, there was a man named Liang Yi, renowned for his skill in making inkstones. Liang Yi was from Zhenjiang, and some say his name might have been omitted. He was skilled in making inkstones, and he was very strict in selecting the stone, much like Shen Li in choosing the finest materials. Even if the stone was slightly inferior, he would set it aside and not carve it, and it would take several years to complete one inkstone. One day, while visiting a certain person's garden, he stopped by a flower terrace and pointed to a stone, saying, "This can be made into an inkstone." The owner took the stone and gave it to him. It took three years to complete, and then he completed it in one go, naming it "Well-Field Inkstone." In the inkstone's reservoir, a cow was carved, and the ink-well was inscribed with "well" characters, raised like a line. He gave it to the owner, who looked at it and laughed, saying, "It is indeed strange. But dividing the fields and drawing lines, will it lead to pulling the cow through the fields?" Liang Yi tested it with water, and the "well" characters disappeared into the stone, the cow moved as if alive, and when the water dried, they returned to their original state. The owner was then amazed by its magic. Liang Yi said, "I carved it according to its material. Otherwise, it would be a waste of the well. I could not figure out the design, which is why it took so long to complete." I secretly admired their skill, isn't this what is meant by skill advancing to the level of art? Li Jiefu described and praised them, and I wrote a combined biography for them. Alas! Selection must be precise, and use must be appropriate, is this only true for writing brushes and inkstones?

    397

    蜘蛛
    　　马耳山瞰海州城。有蜘蛛宅山上，不知几何年物也，亦往来云台、伊芦、大伊诸山，能神怪。土人往往见之，或如寒月嵌霄，倏忽上下，大小不常，盖其珠也。间游海中，戏弄海舶。或离水升空，已复在水，而舶中器具，略不摇撼。人亦习之，不为骇异。有吴某尝出于道，见西林黝黑一障，而光烁可鉴。渐近，觉沙石扑面。急伏地，乃闻骤风怒雹，浮身而过，神智迷惑。须臾而定。起视西林，黑光东矣。人曰：“此蜘蛛过也。”视吴面色如傅靛。洗之乃去，而水不加蓝。海州城内，常有大风寒晦，而城外喧旭，草木不摇，或以为亦蜘蛛所为也。

    Explanation

    397

    Spider
    The Ma'er Mountain overlooks the city of Haizhou. There is a spider that resides on the mountain, having lived for an unknown number of years. It also travels among the YunTai, YiLu, and DaYi mountains, capable of performing miraculous feats. The locals often see it, sometimes appearing like a cold moon embedded in the sky, moving up and down suddenly, and varying in size. It is said to be its pearl. Occasionally, it swims in the sea, playing with sea vessels. Sometimes it leaves the water and rises into the air, then returns to the water, while the tools inside the ship remain undisturbed. People have become accustomed to it and no longer find it strange. Once, a man named Wu came across it on the road. He saw a dark, black barrier in the west, but it shone brightly and could be used as a mirror. As he approached, he felt sand and stones hitting his face. He quickly crouched down and heard a sudden gust of wind and hail. The wind and hail passed over him, and his mind and spirit were confused. After a moment, he regained his composure. When he stood up and looked at the west, the black light had moved east. People said, "This was the spider passing by." When they looked at Wu, his face was as blue as indigo. They washed him, and the water did not turn blue. In the city of Haizhou, there are often strong winds and cold, gloomy weather, but outside the city, it is noisy and sunny, and the grass and trees do not move. Some people believe that this is also caused by the spider.

    398

    书吏
    　　山西有书吏，自太原假归，携二仆策蹇负囊。路遇少妇，亦骑驴相先后，从一童子，盖弟送其姊归其夫家者也。稍相问讯，遂与目成。童徐行，见道旁树巅有鹊巢，潜上取騦。既下而妇远矣，度姊已至其家，遂不前而返。妇既谐吏行，乃忘分道，亦不知童子未从也。
    　　日昃抵一村，吏之佃舍在焉，止妇与宿。夜将半，二仆相与谋攫囊橐逸去。绐佃舍佣者曰：“我先归耳。”佣信之。已闻吏所声甚哗，亟起索烛往觇　，则吏与妇并为盗所杀，浴血中得其家剉草刀。惧获罪，即瘗尸郊外。
    　　数日，妇夫迎妇于妇家，家以既归对。诘诸童子，得中途探巢，妇与书吏偕行状。急踪迹之，至佃舍曰：“归矣！”至吏家则讶曰：“未归！”乃共执佣者讼之官。佣吐实，且曰：“必二仆杀之，故逃。”官以为然，亟捕二仆讯之，则坚不承，曰；“窃窜不敢隐，实未杀人。”既往发尸，妇尸已不见，吏与一僧尸耳，而僧尸固无创，莫不骇异。狱遂久不决。

    Explanation

    398

    The Clerk
    In Shanxi, there was a clerk who, after a leave of absence from Taiyuan, returned home accompanied by two servants, leading a slow donkey and carrying bags. On the road, he met a young woman who was also riding a donkey, with a servant boy following behind. It seemed that the boy was escorting his sister to her husband's home. They exchanged brief greetings, and soon their eyes met, forming a connection. The boy walked slowly, noticing a magpie's nest on a tree branch. He secretly climbed up to retrieve the nest. After descending, he found the woman had gone far ahead. He guessed that his sister had already arrived at her home and decided to return without going further. The woman, having agreed to walk with the clerk, forgot to take a different path and was unaware that the boy had not followed her.

    By noon, they reached a village where the clerk's rented house was located, and he stayed there for the night with the woman. Just before midnight, the two servants plotted to steal the bags and escape. They deceived the tenant's worker, saying, "We will go back first." The worker believed them. Soon, they heard loud noises coming from the clerk's place. The worker quickly lit a candle and went to investigate, only to find that both the clerk and the woman had been killed by thieves. In the blood, they discovered a grass-cutting knife from the clerk's home. Fearing punishment, they buried the bodies outside the village.

    A few days later, the woman's husband came to her family's home to fetch her, and they told him she had already returned. When questioned, the boy revealed that he had stopped to explore a nest during their journey, and that the woman had walked with the clerk. The husband hurriedly tracked them down to the rented house, where they said, "She has returned!" But when they arrived at the clerk's home, they were surprised to find, "She hasn't returned!" They then took the worker to court, accusing him of the crime. The worker confessed, saying, "It must have been the two servants who killed them, so they fled." The judge believed him and quickly arrested the two servants for questioning. However, they firmly denied the accusation, saying, "We ran away out of fear, but we did not kill anyone." When the bodies were exhumed, the woman's body was missing, and only the clerk's and a monk's bodies were found. The monk's body had no visible wounds, which shocked everyone. The case remained unresolved for a long time.

    399

    先是，佣者女尝与邻人之子私，既而绝之。其夜邻子复往，值妇与吏寝，疑女别遇，忿甚，索得厩中剉草刀杀之，逃去。既而知其误，复归调女，女不许。邻子怒且骂曰：“恨尔夜不曾杀女！”女诧其语，窃告佣者白官，执邻子，一鞫而伏，终以杀僧无验，又不得妇尸，缓其狱。遣胥挟童子，廉诸他邑。有妇浣溪上，童子乃言直其姊也。妇亦惊涕相问，遂告以由。方妇之瘗郊外也，迟明，有二僧过瘗所，觉土中触触动，掘视得二尸，妇伤刃未殊，已苏矣。一僧欲取为梵嫂，虑此僧见梗。遽扼杀，并吏掩之，负妇归寺中。潜蓄顶发，易衣冠，遁居他邑。至是僧他适，妇出浣衣，获遇其弟云。于是执僧及邻子抵罪，余各论律有差。

    Explanation

    399

    Previously, a woman who was a hired worker had a relationship with the son of a neighbor, but she later broke it off. That night, the neighbor's son returned, and he found the woman sleeping with an official. Suspecting that she had met someone else, he became very angry and found a scythe in the stable, killed her, and fled. Later, he realized his mistake and returned to try to seduce her again, but she refused. The neighbor's son became furious and shouted, "I regret that I didn't kill her last night!" The woman was surprised by his words and secretly told her employer to report it to the authorities. They arrested the neighbor's son, and he confessed after one interrogation. However, the case was ultimately inconclusive regarding the murder of the monk, and they could not find the woman's body, so the trial was delayed. They sent officials with a child to investigate other towns. A woman was washing in the stream, and the child recognized her as his sister. The woman was also surprised and wept, asking what had happened. She then told them the entire story. At dawn, two monks passed by the burial site and felt something moving in the ground. They dug and found two bodies; the woman was injured but not fatally and had already regained consciousness. One monk wanted to take her as his nun-wife and feared that this monk would interfere. He quickly strangled her and the official and covered up the crime, carrying the woman back to the temple. He secretly kept her head shaved, changed her clothes, and fled to another town. By this time, the monk had left. The woman came out to wash clothes and met her brother, who reported it. They then arrested the monk and the neighbor's son and punished them, with the rest being judged according to the law.

    400

    耳食录二编卷五
    　　魏翁
    　　魏翁病革，呼诸子问曰；“视吾何如？”诸子曰：“固无虑。”又问诸姬，诸姬曰：“固无虑。”翁曰：“脱我死，尔曹何为？”诸子泣曰：“万有不讳，翁所教画，必遵必赴，敬承厥志，毋敢贻翁羞！”诸姬泣曰：“恩谊深矣！有生之年，皆公之身也，敢有二志！”翁乃益悲痛。恍惚之间，见二人催请甚急，不禁随之。

    Explanation

    400

    Ear Food Records, Second Compilation, Volume Five

    Wei Elder

    The Wei Elder was gravely ill. He called his sons and asked, "How am I?" The sons replied, "There is certainly no cause for worry." He then asked his concubines, and they too said, "There is certainly no cause for worry." The Elder said, "If I were to die, what would you do?" The sons wept and replied, "If there is any misfortune, we will certainly follow your instructions and carry out your wishes, respecting your commands and not bringing shame to you!" The concubines wept and said, "The bond of affection and righteousness is deep! All the days of our lives belong to you, and we dare not have any other intentions!" The Elder then became even more sorrowful. In a daze, he saw two people urging him urgently, and he could not help but follow them.

    401

    行至一殿廷，有衣冠数人相揖就坐，其一曰：“候公许时，何迟迟其来？”其一曰：“必顾孺弄稚，不能遽舍。”翁唯唯，问：“此是何地，诸公何人也？”其一曰：“地则阴府，吾属冥判耳。”翁始悟已死，大惊投地，哀吁求生。其一曰：“此何负于公，公乃不愿？”翁泣曰：“乐生恶死，万物之情也。夫家之所有，皆得而有之。乃身之所有，反不得而有。死生之判，苦乐悬绝矣。故吾所愿。在彼不在此。”其一曰：“公终不死亦良乐。然公果终不死，则苦乃无尽。公固不思耳！”翁哭且拜曰：“苦乐所不暇计，但使我复生，有感阮怨！”其一忻然语众曰：“此公亦太俗，姑听其还，使尝而后信可也。”众微笑颔之，复命前二人引还。霍然而苏，由是病痊愈，家人亲串走相庆，翁亦私心自幸，谓求而得之者也。

    Explanation

    401

    Arriving at a grand hall, several people in formal attire bowed to each other and sat down. One of them said, "We waited for you, why did you come so late?" Another replied, "You must have been occupied with your children, so you couldn't leave quickly." The old man nodded in agreement and asked, "Where are we? Who are you all?" One of them said, "This is the underworld, we are the judges of the dead." The old man suddenly realized he was dead and was greatly shocked, falling to the ground, and cried out, begging for life. One of them said, "What have we done to you that you don't want to stay?" The old man said, "Enjoying life and hating death is the nature of all things. All that belongs to the family, one can possess. But one's own body, one cannot possess. The distinction between life and death, the extremes of suffering and joy, is vast. Therefore, what I desire is life, not death." One of them said, "Even if you don't die, it's still good. But if you truly don't die, the suffering will be endless. You just haven't thought about it." Crying and bowing, the old man said, "I don't have time to consider suffering and joy, but if I could be reborn, I would be grateful and relieved." One of them said, "This man is too worldly, let him go back and experience it himself before he believes it." The others smiled and nodded in agreement. They ordered the two people who had brought him here to take him back. He suddenly woke up. As a result, his illness was cured, and his family and friends rushed to congratulate him. The old man also secretly felt fortunate, believing that what he had desired was now his.

    402

    是岁长子举于乡，明年成进士。仲子、季子相继青其衿，食饩于庠。诸子妇皆孙，以男以女，门闾寖兴。翁步履益健，诸姬多孕，连举数子，鸡豚牛马之畜，硕大蕃滋。其姻娅连结，皆贵家巨姓，乡邑间号称鼎盛。

    Explanation

    402

    This year, the eldest son was selected in the local examination, and the following year, he passed the imperial examination to become a Jinshi. The second and youngest sons subsequently wore green robes (indicating they were scholars) and received stipends at the school. The family's prestige gradually increased. The old man's steps became more vigorous, and many of his concubines were pregnant, with several children being born. Poultry, pigs, cattle, and horses grew large and numerous. Their relatives and connections were all from prominent and wealthy families. In the local area, they were considered to be at the height of their power and prosperity.

    403

    然翁之季年，食不充口，衣不周体，疾病无药饵，其死也以缢。茕茕妇孺不能为丧，衾冒棺椁之薄，犹　于邻里。七日而后敛，行路哀之。问其故，则长子死于官矣；仲子奔之，遇盗杀而尸诸途。季子邪而侈者也，健讼而好博，与吏胥相倚，为乡曲害，人避而畏之若蜂虿。翁不能禁，后卒以得罪伏法。因是家亦倾。长孙畏贫，从其外舅贾于黔，寻客死。次孙齿未冠，失业游惰，忽亡去。于是魏氏一门丁壮殆尽矣。翁既累遭祸败，惊忧愁苦，始不乐生，而家室怨叹声不绝耳。诸姬以冻馁求去。翁不得已，竟开阁。而子孙妇之寡者亦相率去帷。其存者，藐焉孩稚，及仲子之妇已。翁是时年几八十，追维今昔，恍若两世，身经众故，魂伤貌悴，乃悟冥司之言有以也。每以述于人，而悔其向之不达，故缢而死。

    Explanation

    403

    In the old man's later years, he went hungry, his clothes were inadequate, and he suffered from illness without medicine. He died by hanging. The widows and orphans were too destitute to perform proper funeral rites, and he was buried in a thin shroud and a simple coffin, still relying on the neighbors. Seven days after his death, the body was finally buried, and the passersby mourned him. When asked the reason, it was revealed that his eldest son had died in office; the second son went to meet him and was killed by bandits, his body left on the road. The youngest son was debauched and extravagant, fond of litigation and gambling, and relied on officials and clerks, becoming a scourge to the village. People avoided and feared him like a scorpion or a wasp. The old man could not restrain him, and he was eventually punished by law. As a result, the family also declined. The eldest grandson feared poverty and went to trade with his father-in-law in Guizhou, soon dying as a guest. The second grandson, not yet of age, was unemployed and idle, suddenly disappearing. Thus, the Hui family's able-bodied men were almost all gone. The old man had suffered repeated misfortunes, filled with shock, worry, sorrow, and hardship, and began to lose the will to live. The sounds of lamentation and complaints from the family never ceased. The concubines left because of hunger and cold. The old man, unable to bear it, finally opened the door. The widowed granddaughters also left the curtains. Those who remained were only young children and the widow of the second son. The old man was nearly eighty at the time, reflecting on the past and present, it seemed like two different lives. Having experienced so many misfortunes, his spirit was broken and his appearance was withered. He then realized that the words of the underworld were true. He often recounted this to others, regretting his earlier lack of understanding, and so he died by hanging.

    404

    女湘
    　　再生事夥矣，莫奇于女湘。湘姓金氏，能记宿世事。尝为士人子，生时有骨横其胸。遇道士相之曰：“此情骨也，吾能蜕之，不尔，将为历劫累。”家恶闻其说，叱而遗之。
    　　稍长，无他慧，雅善伤心。妍花素月，凄风悄雨，皆断肠时也。魂魄缕缕，常在珠箔镜奁间，然一往情深。初不作登徒之想。尝吧曰：“吾不幸形骸之累，瓜李皆兵，死见氤氲司，求生我蛾眉班中。”苑有海棠一株，爱护甚至，花时作紫罗棚幛，覆蔽其风雨，每戏谓经曰：“汝若憔悴，吾当殉汝！”花落，必泣于树下，且泣且诉。泣诉已，必疾病，岁以为常。父疑花之祟也，伐其树，湘大恸，一踊遂绝。

    Explanation

    404

    Nuxiang
    Cases of rebirth are numerous, but none are more extraordinary than that of Nuxiang. Her surname was Jin, and she could recall events from past lives. She had once been the daughter of a scholar, and at birth, she had a bone across her chest. A Taoist priest divined her fate and said, "This is the bone of emotion. I can remove it, but if I do not, it will become a burden across countless lifetimes." Her family disliked hearing this and scolded him, driving him away.
    As she grew older, she had no other talents, but she was exceptionally prone to sorrow. Beautiful flowers, moonlit nights, chilly winds, and quiet rains were all moments that broke her heart. Her soul, thread-like, often lingered in the silken curtains and mirror cases, yet her emotions were deeply entwined. She never entertained thoughts of promiscuity. Once, she said, "Unfortunate is my physical form, where every encounter is like a battle. In death, I will meet the Office of Mist and seek to be reborn among the celestial maidens." In the garden, there was a single hawthorn tree that she cherished dearly. During the blooming season, she would set up a purple silk canopy to protect it from wind and rain. She often playfully said to the tree, "If you wither, I will sacrifice myself for you!" When the flowers fell, she would weep at the base of the tree, crying and lamenting. After her tears and words had spent themselves, she would inevitably fall ill, and this became a yearly occurrence. Her father suspected that the tree was bringing misfortune, so he cut it down. Nuxiang was overwhelmed with grief, and in a single leap, she died.

    405

    湘之始死也，皇皇无所向，觉彩霞满天，溪谷绚映如锦绣。有二女使候于途，随至大第中。列幕甚邃，钗光环照，云璈数声，众报夫人出，玉容端丽，服饰如古妃主状，降席徐言。湘窃左右顾盼，未之闻也。侍者潜曳其衣曰：“夫人问汝。”湘张皇失措，莫知所对，满堂粲然。夫人笑曰：“君候信自痴！”俄有侍者执烛导湘度东廓，启月扉，达于曲房。帷榻衾枕甚雅，数婢拥一女子入，坐榻上，哄然遂散。湘交袖侧身，睇不移睛。女推而远之，遂解衣入衾。湘复移灯窥枕，女赧尔微怒，回身内障。湘周视覆盖。恐风露侵其肌也；下帷蔽光，恐华灯烁其目也；敛衣屏息，枯坐枕端，恐扰其酣眠清梦也。

    Explanation

    405

    As for Xiang at the beginning of his death, he wandered aimlessly without direction, perceiving rainbow clouds filling the sky, the valleys brightly reflected like brocade. Two female servants awaited him on the way, guiding him to a grand mansion. Rows of curtains were deep, jewelry shining, and the sound of a cloud zither echoed, as the attendants announced the lady's arrival. She had a graceful and elegant appearance, dressed in attire reminiscent of ancient noblewomen, descending from her seat and speaking slowly. Xiang secretly looked around, not hearing her words. A servant secretly pulled his clothes and said, "The lady asks you." Xiang panicked and lost his composure, not knowing how to respond, the entire hall brightly lit. The lady smiled and said, "Lord, you are indeed foolish!" Soon, a servant held a candle and led Xiang through the eastern garden, opening the moon door, reaching a secluded room. The bed, curtains, quilt, and pillow were very elegant. Several maids escorted a woman into the room, who sat on the bed, and they all dispersed. Xiang crossed his sleeves and turned to the side, his gaze fixed and unblinking. The woman pushed him away, and he undressed and climbed into the quilt. Xiang moved the lamp to peek at the pillow, the woman blushed slightly with mild anger, turning away to hide. Xiang looked around the covering, fearing the wind and dew would invade her skin; he lowered the curtains to block the light, fearing the bright lights would dazzle her eyes; he gathered his clothes and held his breath, sitting motionlessly at the edge of the bed, fearing he would disturb her deep sleep and clear dreams.

    406

    东方白，女觉而起，微语曰：“君貌如冠玉，何无丈夫气？”湘对曰：“得闻芗泽，于愿至足。臣之好色，不在床第间也。”女微笑唾之，湘钯承以襟；须臾，成海棠一蒂，异而问之，女颦曰：“君未识妾耶？君畴昔所爱树，即我也。感君同死，愿生生世世同作多情物！”言次，夫人促召去。命侍者展绣幡招艭之，飞花搅空，著湘衣袂间，不复脱落。旋有暖风一缕起地上，顿觉身轻如叶，飘飘然惟风所向。顷之，触树而止，身乃与树合，而枝叶动摇，无异臂指之使，盖转生为海棠矣。其旁有桃树，则女托焉，于是相呼乐甚。

    Explanation

    406

    At dawn, the woman woke up and got up, speaking softly, "Your appearance is like a jade ornament, yet you lack the spirit of a man?" Xiang replied, "To hear the fragrance is enough for me. My love for beauty is not limited to the bedroom." The woman smiled and spat at him, and Xiang caught it with his sleeve. Soon, he became a cluster of hibiscus flowers. Wondering, he asked, and the woman frowned and said, "Do you not recognize me? The tree you loved in the past was me. I was moved by your death and wish to be a loving being with you for countless lifetimes!" While speaking, the lady summoned him away urgently. She ordered the attendant to spread out the embroidered flag to call the boat. Flowers flew and stirred the air, sticking to Xiang's sleeves and not falling off. Soon, a warm breeze rose from the ground, and he suddenly felt as light as a leaf, floating aimlessly wherever the wind blew. In a short while, he touched the tree and stopped, his body merging with it. The branches and leaves moved, just like the movements of arms and fingers, and it turned out that he had transformed into a hibiscus flower. There was a peach tree nearby, and the woman relied on it. They then called to each other happily.

    407

    其地朱阑白砌，苔径横斜，缭以短垣，垣有凿坏，通巨宅。盖某贵绅花圃也。圃中花姊妹咸来问讯，款接甚欢。月明风细，辄游戏清池碧草间，情致殊凄宛也。未几桃始花。绅有女雪燕，绝美好，偕诸婢来观，各折枝簪鬓间，谛视海棠，相谓曰：“何尚未蕊？”湘即欲具蕊，女止之曰：“君花期尚在半月后，何遽也？违候而花，将不寿矣？”湘不听，明日花焉。雪燕不意其猝开，数日竟不至。落矣，又开以待之。三开，雪燕来，惊曰：“何遽若此？”徘徊久之，折数枝，作胆瓶供。湘不胜喜。

    Explanation

    407

    The garden is adorned with red railings and white walls, with moss-covered paths winding through it. It is surrounded by a short wall with openings that connect to a large mansion. This is the garden of a wealthy and influential gentleman. The flowers in the garden, personified as sisters, all come to greet each other, and the interaction is very friendly. Under the bright moon and gentle breeze, they play in the clear pond and green grass, creating a serene and joyful scene. The atmosphere is unusually melancholic and tender, hinting at a deeper emotional undercurrent.

    Soon after, the peach trees begin to bloom. The gentleman has a daughter named Xueyan, who is exceptionally beautiful. She comes with her maidservants to admire the flowers, each of them picking branches to wear in their hair. They carefully observe the hawthorn trees and speak to each other. "Why haven't the buds appeared yet?" one of them asks. Xiang, a close friend or companion of Xueyan, wants to make the buds appear. The daughter stops her and says, "Your flower's blooming period is still two weeks away. Why rush? If you make it bloom out of season, it won't last long." Xiang doesn't listen, and the next day the flower blooms. Xueyan was not expecting it to bloom so suddenly and for several days she does not come. It falls, and then blooms again to wait for her. After blooming three times, Xueyan comes, surprised and says, "Why did it bloom so quickly?" She lingers for a long time, picking several branches to arrange in a vase. Xiang is extremely happy.

    408

    次日，绅折简治具召客，花侣闻之皆吊湘。已而车马阗咽，冠履坌集，酒肴洊至，熏腾如毒雾。酒酣赋诗，评赞呶杂，湘不能堪。日暮，各选条折枝而去。于是晚风芆起，落片惊飞。湘叹息曰：“封家姨来何暮也？”是夕遂病，日就槁以死。女感其情，亦从之。见夫人，夫人慰恤之甚厚。复与女同生者数世，事不能详。
    　　一日，夫人谓之曰：“君尝欲现女人身，今当如志。天地绮丽之气，名花美人，分而有之。此行无异登仙也。”湘顿首谢，女导至一楼，以繁香浴之，洒涕而诀曰：“缘深矣，可若何？然情根纠结，何时已乎？请从此判，不复游于人间矣！”言已。遂推湘楼下，乃如自云雾中堕，形顿缩，遂为金氏女湘云。及笄，父母欲婿之，湘坚矢不可。而怜钗惜粉，不异曩时，殊自忘其身之既雌也。尝言作海棠时，被折甚楚，无异创其肢体。雪燕来折，则心悦其丽，不复觉耳。

    Explanation

    408

    The next day, the gentleman sent out invitations and prepared a feast to entertain his guests. Upon hearing this, the flower companions all came to mourn for Xiang. Soon after, the area was filled with the sound of carriages and horses, and people in hats and shoes were densely gathered. Wine and food kept arriving, and the strong smell was like a toxic fog. Drunk, they composed poetry, praised and criticized each other noisily, and Xiang could not bear it. At sunset, each guest selected branches to take away and left. Then, the evening wind began to blow, and leaves fell and flew away in surprise. Xiang sighed, saying, "Why did Aunt Feng come so late?" That night, she fell ill and gradually withered and died. The woman, moved by Xiang's feelings, also followed him. She met the lady, who comforted and showed great concern for her. They were reborn together for several generations, but the details cannot be fully explained.

    One day, the lady told her, "You once desired to appear as a woman, and now it will be as you wished. The beautiful and luxurious atmosphere of heaven and earth, the famous flowers and beautiful women, are divided and shared. This journey is no different from ascending to immortality." Xiang knelt and bowed to thank her. The woman led her to a tower, where she was bathed in fragrant herbs. She wept and said farewell, "The bond is deep, what can be done? But the roots of emotion are intertwined, when will it end? Please let us part, and I will no longer wander in the world!" After saying this, she pushed Xiang down from the building, and she fell as if from a cloud and mist. Her shape suddenly shrank, and she became a girl named Xiangyun of the Jin family. When she reached the age of marriage, her parents wanted to arrange a marriage for her, but Xiang firmly refused. She still cherished her jewelry and cosmetics, just as before, and completely forgot that she had already become a woman. She once said that when she was a peony, being cut was very painful, as if her body was being wounded. When the snow swallow came to cut, she was delighted by its beauty and no longer felt pain.

    409

    年二十余卒。卒之前一日，有比丘尼至其家，湘见如旧识，家人皆莫之识。握拂对语，如参悟状。尼曰：“露珠极明，沾之立碎。霜化至洁，触之即消。”湘曰：“究竟何如？”尼曰：“日里霞光，非空非色；镜中花影，是幻是真？”湘点首者再，尼遂去。翼日湘卒。濒卒，历叙其夙因，命瘗诸海棠之下。
    　　齐　福　喜
    　　雍正中，有大兴县民齐福喜者，好儇弄。其嫂性苦畏，齐谋恐之。夜定，以白纸作冠高如筩，纸钱垂癴两颊间，面傅粉墨，衔猪舌，表羊裘而披之身，将伏厕间以俟嫂。装讫，览镜，忽心动。既如厕，启门，一鬼迎面出，与齐形绝肖，合于齐身，齐大呼倒地。家人奔视：“有鬼，死焉。”烛之，乃齐也。亟舁之床，滌其面，饮之姜汤，姑渐苏。病月余，卒死。或曰：厕之鬼，齐之魂也。引镜心动时，盖离舍而先往矣。然欤？否欤？

    409

    Xiang died in his twenties. The day before his death, a nun came to his home. Xiang saw her as if she were an old acquaintance, but the family members all did not recognize her. She held a whisk and spoke to him, as if in a state of enlightenment. The nun said, "The dewdrop is extremely bright, but it shatters upon touch. The frost is extremely pure, but it melts upon touch." Xiang asked, "What is the ultimate truth?" The nun replied, "The morning glory in the sky is neither empty nor colored; the reflection of flowers in a mirror is both illusory and real?" Xiang nodded twice, and the nun then left. The next day, Xiang died. On the verge of death, he recounted his past causes and ordered to be buried under the crabapple tree.

    As for Qi FuXi, during the reign of Emperor Yongzheng, there was a man from Daxing County named Qi FuXi who liked to play tricks. His sister-in-law was very fearful, and Qi planned to scare her. At night, he made a high cap out of white paper, hung paper money from his cheeks, applied powder and ink to his face, held a pig's tongue in his mouth, and wore a sheepskin coat. He planned to hide in the toilet to scare his sister-in-law. After dressing, he looked in the mirror and suddenly felt a shock. When he went to the toilet, he opened the door and a ghost appeared in front of him, looking exactly like Qi. The ghost merged with Qi's body, and Qi shouted and fell to the ground. The family rushed to see, "There was a ghost, he died." When they lit a candle, it was Qi. They quickly carried him to the bed, washed his face, gave him ginger soup to drink, and he gradually regained consciousness. He was ill for more than a month and eventually died. Some say: the ghost in the toilet was Qi's spirit. When he looked in the mirror and felt a shock, it seems he had left his body and gone first. Is this true? Or not?

    410

    狼狈
    　　海州多狼患。庄民捕得其稚者杀之，或剔目决足，仍纵之去，意以警狼。其后，庄民某暮从他镇返，遭数狼于道。狼似相识，并力而前。某亟走避稻积上，狼不能登，环而守之。夜既深，狼忽散去。某亦不敢下，以待天明，冀行者之助己也。俄而狼大至，有小狼衔大狼尾行。视之，瞎狼也，即某前剔其目者。其来也，将甘心于仇，以快其志。又一狼负一狈至，狈足前短后长，外于狼背。熟视稻积，忽衔稻一束望后掷之。群狼喻意，争衔稻，稻积将塌。会向晨，有荷锄及担者数人来，某大呼救。数人操具奔至，狼乃始解去。

    Explanation

    410

    A Wolf and a Bêi

    In the coastal region of Hai, there were many wolf problems. The villagers caught young wolves and killed them, sometimes plucking out their eyes and cutting off their feet, then releasing them, intending to warn the other wolves. Later, a villager returned from another town at dusk and encountered several wolves on the road. The wolves seemed to recognize him and united their strength to advance. The villager quickly ran to the top of a rice pile, where the wolves could not climb, and surrounded him. As the night deepened, the wolves suddenly dispersed. The villager dared not come down and waited for dawn, hoping for the help of passersby. Soon, many wolves arrived, and a small wolf carried the tail of a large wolf. Upon closer inspection, it was a blind wolf, the one whose eyes had been plucked out by the villager before. It came with the intention of taking pleasure in revenge and satisfying its will. Another wolf carried a bêi, a mythical creature with a short front body and a long back body, on its back. The bêi looked carefully at the rice pile and suddenly carried a bundle of rice and threw it backward. The wolves understood the meaning and competed to carry rice, causing the rice pile to collapse. Just as dawn approached, several people carrying hoes and loads came. The villager shouted for help. The people with tools ran to him, and the wolves finally dispersed.

    411

    由此观之，济狼之恶者狈也，狈策而狼攻。《酉阳杂俎》所载事类此。
    　　何生
    　　山左何生者，富而好义。尝客金陵，遍谒同舍郎。一少年客居西室，首戴长巾，衣甚褴褛，而珠神玉貌如好女子，见人辄扃户避去。何心仪之，投三刺，客瞰亡始来答拜。他日窥其室，曲突无烟，客坐蝇床上，拥败絮而已。退问主人：“客何姓，何许人？”亦弗之知也，乃谓税居半载矣，值弗偿，不能复馆之。何叹息不已，意必避难而穷于途者。乃代为纳值，嘱勿言，间馈之金钱周恤之。客不辞，亦不谢。无何，款其关，则无应；入其室，无人焉，客竟去矣。床下有青布囊，启之，所馈钱悉在，深叹其廉，然心窃怪之。

    Explanation

    411

    From this perspective, it is the "bèi" that assists in the evil deeds of wolves, as the "bèi" plans while the wolves attack. The Yuyang Zazu records stories similar to this.

    Mr. He from Shandong

    Mr. He from Shandong is wealthy and benevolent. Once, he stayed in Jinling (Nanjing) and visited all the officials in the same residence. A young man was staying in the west room, wearing a long headscarf and tattered clothes, but with a beauty like a fine lady, a radiant spirit, and delicate appearance. He always closed the door and avoided people when they came. Mr. He admired him deeply and sent three formal invitations. The guest only came to return the visit after he left. One day, Mr. He peeked into his room and found the fireplace curved with no smoke, the guest sitting on a low, simple bed, covered with old, torn bedding. Later, he asked the host, "What is the guest's name, and where does he come from?" The host did not know either, but said he had rented the place for half a year, the rent was not paid, and they could not continue to house him. Mr. He could not stop sighing, suspecting that he was a fugitive who was destitute on the road. He paid the rent on his behalf, asked him not to tell anyone, and occasionally gave him money to help him. The guest did not decline, nor did he thank him. Soon after, when Mr. He knocked on the door, there was no response; he entered the room, but found no one there, and the guest had disappeared. There was a blue cloth bag under the bed. When opened, all the money he had given was inside. Mr. He deeply admired his integrity, but secretly found it strange.

    412

    未几，何还山左。里人诬讦其阴事。县官索贿赂至巨万，始得理，家由是破。遂南游于楚，称贷其故旧之官湖湘者，薄有所得。旋治任经洞庭之野，忽林间逸出一巨兽，红毛锯齿，当途而立，势将搏噬，莫可逃匿。倏有女子飞骑来，锦衣弓鞋，腰剑挟弓矢，即马上举足勾兽鼻。兽狂吼而奔，女逐之，绝尘而去。何虽幸得脱　，而所驾马惊于兽，鞭之不肯前。不得已，返辔故道，马乃行。

    Explanation

    412

    Not long after, He returned to his hometown in Shandong. The villagers falsely accused him of his secret affairs. The county official demanded bribes amounting to millions before he could clear his name, and as a result, his family fell into ruin. He then traveled south to Chu and borrowed money from his old friends who were officials in Hunan, gaining a little. Shortly afterward, he prepared his luggage and passed through the wilderness of Dongting Lake. Suddenly, a huge beast, red-haired and saw-toothed, emerged from the forest and stood in the middle of the path, about to attack and devour, with no place to hide. Suddenly, a woman came riding a horse, wearing brocade clothes and bow shoes, with a sword at her waist and a bow and arrows in her hands. She immediately lifted her foot to hook the beast's nose. The beast roared wildly and ran, and the woman chased it, leaving dust behind. He was fortunate to escape, but the horse he was riding was frightened by the beast, and whipping it would not make it go forward. Having no choice, he turned back to the original path, and the horse then continued.

    413

    日已迟暮，不及宿，独止野庙中，不能成寐。夜深月出，起步庙门外，微艱拂面，隐隐闻笛声，悠扬纤妙。且听且行，笛声止而笑声起，则灯火烂然，甲第大辟，有摄华衣冠者迎门外，揖何而进之，抗宾主之礼。何怀疑，不测其由，乃征其氏族，其人曰：“君忘金陵西室之人与？即我也。”何审视，果是，因问：“向者何去之密，今何遇之巧？”客曰：“余穷而遁去，投止于此。感君旧谊，故特相俟。”何讶其预知，客唯唯。语次，闻客腕钏触几频有声。何窃左右顾，而见其冠下微露鬓梢，心愈疑而不敢诘。客笑曰：“君疑我耶？日间马上驱猛兽、为君除道者亦我也。”因探去其冠而云鬟见。何大惊，亟拜称谢，呼曰：“神人。”女亦答拜之，曰：“吾雄服游戏人间，以贫自晦，遂不为人识。君独助我，故我亦助君，适以相酬，奚足复齿？”

    Explanation

    413

    The day had already grown late, and he did not have time to rest, so he stayed alone in a wild temple, unable to fall asleep. Deep into the night, the moon rose, and he stepped out of the temple gate. A gentle breeze brushed against his face, and he faintly heard a flute sound, graceful and delicate. Listening and walking, the flute stopped and laughter began. Then the lights shone brightly, and a grand mansion opened wide. A person dressed in fine clothes greeted him at the door, bowing to invite him inside, observing the etiquette of host and guest. He doubted, not fathoming the reason, and then asked about his family lineage. The person said, "Do you forget the person from the western room in Jinling? It is me." He scrutinized and indeed it was, and then he asked, "Why did you leave so secretly before? Why did we meet so conveniently now?" The guest said, "I fled because I was in dire straits, took refuge here. Grateful for your old friendship, so I specially waited for you." He was surprised that he knew in advance, and the guest nodded. During the conversation, he heard the sound of the guest's bracelets hitting the table frequently. He secretly looked around and saw a hint of hair peeking out from under the cap, his heart becoming more doubtful and he dared not ask. The guest laughed and said, "Do you doubt me? The person who rode on horseback and drove away fierce beasts to clear the path for you was also me." Because he removed his cap, revealing the cloud-like hair. He was greatly surprised, quickly bowed and thanked, calling out, "Divine being." The woman also bowed back, saying, "I disguised myself as a man and played among humans, hid my poverty, so I was not recognized by people. You alone helped me, so I also help you, just to repay you, not worthy of mention."

    414

    旋顾谓侍者，呼了奴出。乃十三四岁女子，头作双角髻，短袄窄袖，秀若云霞。女与之语，殊隐跃。了奴曰：“诺。”遂拂檐而去。顷之剑声吷然。了奴已瞥下，反命曰：“毕矣”。女乃命治餐，杯盘立具，极海陆之陈。夜向晨，何不胜酒食，起辞告行。女亦不强留，然眷恋之情溢于颜面。有长须奴探骑候门外，即何所乘马，装资亦在。

    Explanation

    414

    Turning around, she called to the attendant, summoning the maid, Liao Nu, to come forth. She was a girl of thirteen or fourteen years old, with a double-horned bun on her head, a short jacket with narrow sleeves, and a beauty that shone like clouds and rosy dawn. The girl spoke to her, but she was very shy and reserved. Liao Nu replied, "Yes," and then swiftly flew away from the eaves. Soon after, the sound of the sword whirred. Liao Nu had already leapt down, returning to report, "It's done." The girl then ordered the preparation of a meal, and bowls and plates were instantly set up, presenting the most luxurious array of dishes. As night approached dawn, the woman could no longer handle the alcohol and food, rising to say goodbye and prepare to leave. The girl did not insist on keeping her, but the affection and reluctance to part were evident on her face. There was a long-bearded servant on horseback waiting outside the door, and the equipment and supplies were also there.

    415

    何遂别，至家。家人乃言里人及县官一夕死于盗，而并亡其首。问其时日，适何见女之夕也，始悟即了奴所为，愈感之。及理行箧，则益以厚赆，别一小匣，缄甚固。启之，得小剑长三寸许，淬利如霜雪。试削庭前树，未至，树已断；划石，石解。意所向，掷剑，剑辄往，已复还手中，盖飞剑也。何喜甚，宝之匣中，间出而玩之。
    　　岁馀，剑首之环脱。其夜室中如虎啸，有白光拂牖而出，剑乃亡。何惘惘如丧者累日。时沍寒密雪，忽闻门外马嘶声。出视，有骏马止焉，鞍镫悉具。疑亡而逸者，而鬣间隐系绣囊。解视，得莲花一瓣，书曰：“骑至即发”。并不署款识。何颇疑怪，而马数数昂首跪地，若劝驾之状。试跨之，则东南而驰，绝驶，亦不知里数。既至一处，莲花池相续如带。及第三池上，马止而斯，不复行。乃见高墉袤延，立处当朱户，户内人语曰：“至矣”。户乃辟，有小鬟招之曰：“进。”

    Explanation

    415

    He parted ways and arrived at his home. His family then informed him that both villagers and county officials had died by bandits in one night, and their heads were also missing. When he asked about the time and date, it turned out to be the very night when he had seen the woman. He began to understand that it was the servant who had done it, and he felt even more grateful. When he sorted out his traveling trunk, he added a generous farewell gift and separated a small box, which was sealed very tightly. He opened it and found a small sword about three inches long, tempered and sharp like frost and snow. He tried to cut a tree in front of the courtyard, and before he reached it, the tree was already broken. When he carved the stone, the stone was cut. Wherever his mind went, when he threw the sword, it always went there and then returned to his hand. It was a flying sword. He was very happy and treasured it in the box, occasionally taking it out to play with it.

    After more than a year, the ring on the sword tip fell off. That night in the room was like the roar of a tiger, and there was a white light brushing the window and out. The sword then disappeared. He was disoriented like a mourner for several days. At that time, it was extremely cold with heavy snow. Suddenly, he heard the sound of a horse neighing outside the door. He went out to look and there was a fine horse stopped there, with the saddle and stirrups all equipped. He suspected it was a lost and escaped horse, and between the mane, there was a hidden and tied embroidered bag. He unfastened and looked, and got one petal of a lotus flower, which said, "Ride to the place where it will be released." There was no signature or identification. He was quite puzzled and suspicious, but the horse repeatedly raised its head and knelt down, as if encouraging him to ride. He tried to mount it, and it galloped southeast at an incredible speed, and he did not know how many miles they traveled. When they arrived at a place, there were lotus pools in succession like a ribbon. When they reached the third pool, the horse stopped and did not move again. He saw a high wall stretching out, and he stood in front of a red door. Someone inside the door said, "We have arrived." The door opened, and a young girl beckoned him to come in.

    416

    何徐徐步入，遽行至内，则曲室银眞，荧荧发碧，黼帐中有呻而叹者，小鬟前白，曰：“至矣。”则闻铃声锵然，帐徐启，有丽人拥衾而坐，神韵酸楚。何逡巡审视，女也。女见何，讶然色喜，已而跃然起，问：“奚以能来？”何告以故。女顾侍者曰：“此必了奴也。”侍者皆点首微笑。顷之，了奴自外入，红衫翠笠，落花满身，鸦鬟楚楚，已胜雀翘矣。女弹其颊曰：“妹子召客，何得不告我？”了奴笑曰：“吾为姊病甚，趣召之，故不及关白。”女默然低首，已谓何曰：“妾相天下士，每不留盼。云翔电迈，颇亦自豪。不图为君缠绵至此。”于是与何为夫妇。

    Explanation

    416

    何缓缓步入，突然疾行至内室，只见曲室中银光闪烁，荧荧发碧，黼帐中有人呻吟叹息。小鬟上前禀告：“到了。”随即听到铃声清脆，帐幕徐徐拉开，见一丽人拥衾而坐，神情酸楚。何逡巡审视，见是女子。女子见何，惊讶之余神色欢喜，随即跃然起身，问道：“为何能来？”何告知缘由。女子对侍者说：“此人必是了奴。”侍者皆点头微笑。不久，了奴自外而入，身着红衫翠笠，落花满身，鸦鬟楚楚，已胜过雀翘。女子轻弹其颊，道：“妹子召客，为何不告诉我？”了奴笑道：“我因姊姊病重，急忙召你，故未及告知。”女子默然低首，随即对何说：“我相天下士，每不留心。云翔电迈，颇亦自豪。不料君缠绵至此。”于是与何结为夫妇。

    417

    何以失剑告，女责之曰：“吾赠君飞剑，为君能用之，乃秘藏为弄具乎？彼乃神物，岂长处匣中？宜其亡耳。顾此物怪变非常，非得了奴，莫能收摄也。”何固属意了奴，乃请与之俱。女初不听，何固请，女笑曰：“察君之意，殆非为剑也。”何不能隐，以情告。女曰：“吾固欲之，然事不可骤，当说之，以侦其意。”乃谓了奴曰：“郎剑遁，是物不易驯，须妹子一往，使郎佐汝。”了奴曰：“妹自足了当，何以佐为？”女曰：“虽然，亦使郎一睹其状，聊试勇怯耳！”了奴许诺。女窃教何曰：“君与妹子求剑，见有物青色如龙者，剑也，毋怖毋却，然且伪为怖恐者，而匿就妹子，彼为君畏故，将不忍拒也。”

    Explanation

    417

    Why did you lose the sword? The woman reproached him, saying, "I gave you a flying sword, so that you could use it. Did you hide it away as a toy? It is a divine object; how could it stay in the box for long? That's why it's gone. But this thing has strange and unusual transformations. Only if you catch the servant can you control it." He was firmly intent on catching the servant, so he then asked to go with her. The woman initially did not listen, but he insisted. She smiled and said, "I see your intention; it's not just for the sword." He couldn't hide it and told her the truth. She said, "I really want it, but things can't happen too quickly. I should talk to him to gauge his intention." She then said to the servant, "Your sword has gone missing; that thing is not easy to tame. You need me to go there; let your husband help you." The servant replied, "I can handle it myself; why do you need to help?" She said, "Even so, let your husband see its appearance; it's just to test his courage!" The servant agreed. The woman secretly told him, saying, "When you and I go to find the sword, if you see something blue and dragon-like, that's the sword. Do not be afraid or retreat, but pretend to be frightened and hide near me. He will be afraid for you and will not dare to refuse."

    418

    遂同行，至万谷之间，风声肃肃。了奴顾何曰：“剑在是矣。”果有物长五六丈，蜿蜒于层崖之巅。了奴招以手，物即投下入手中，遽已缩小，依然小剑耳。何忆女教，乘其举手，佯惊呼，走抱了奴腰，作战栗状。了奴大笑曰：“姊亦大愦愦，如此薄胆郎，何必教来。”因以剑授何，何故故畏缩不敢受，而抱持益力。了奴两颊渐发赧，若不自持者，何凝睇送意，迫恳之，遂及于乱。

    Explanation

    418

    They continued on their journey together, arriving at the valley of ten thousand valleys, where the wind sounded solemn and eerie. Lianü looked at He and said, "The sword is here." Indeed, there was an object several tens of feet long, coiling at the top of a layered cliff. Lianü gestured with her hand, and the object immediately fell into her hand, quickly becoming smaller, still just a small sword. He remembered the girl's teachings and, taking advantage of her raised hand, pretended to be surprised and called out, running to embrace Lianü's waist, showing a shivering posture. Lianü laughed loudly and said, "Sister, you are also quite foolish, such a cowardly man, why did you teach him to come?" So she handed the sword to He, who deliberately shrank back and dared not accept it, but held on even tighter. Lianü's cheeks gradually turned red, as if she couldn't control herself. He fixed his gaze and conveyed his intentions, urgently and earnestly, and eventually led to a disorderly state.

    419

    及还，女既曰：“妹子尝为吾蹇修，吾今报称焉。”于是了奴亦归何。了奴谓何曰：“吾姊妹皆紫兰宫捧剑侍者。与姊窃戏西圃中，拔剑对舞，误伤守宫之鹤，故谪堕人间，使主游侠之事，遇镜而圆，幸托于君。及瓜而代，又将去汝。此后落花明月，万古相思，殆无相见之期矣！”何大悲，二女亦泣。女谓了奴曰：“妹子故善笛，今盍为郎奏之？”了奴硋笛，为悲凉促遏之音，一时风吼霜飞，肝肠尽裂。乃投笛于地曰：“离绪填胸，安有佳音？不如且已。”遂大恸而别。
    　　何独还乡里，亦能通白猿之术，每为人旁雪不平。或有妖凭魅祟、空宅不靖者，何以剑往，立罻服。
    　　偷儿
    　　某生夜读制艺，往复数百遍犹不熟。漏四下，诵声益喧，意且达旦矣。有胠箧者，伏床下躁甚，突起掴之，曰：“尔非生铁，何顽钝若此？余焉能待？”遽趋出门外，鼓掌而去。

    Explanation

    419

    Upon returning, the woman said, "My sister once served as a matchmaker for me, and now I repay her kindness." Thus, Lenu also returned to He. Lenu said to He, "My sister and I both serve as sword bearers in the Purple Orchid Palace. We secretly played in the western garden, drawing swords and dancing, accidentally injuring the sacred crane of the palace. Therefore, we were exiled to the mortal world, tasked with the affairs of wandering knights, and met to complete our union, fortunate to be entrusted to you. When the melon is ripe, I will take over, and I will leave you again. After this, the fallen flowers and bright moon will symbolize eternal longing, and we may never see each other again!" He was greatly saddened, and the two women also wept. The woman said to Lenu, "My sister is skilled at playing the flute. Why don't you play for the young man?" Lenu began to play the flute, producing a sad and mournful sound. At that moment, the wind howled and frost flew, and their hearts and intestines were torn apart. She then threw the flute to the ground and said, "Sadness fills my heart; how can there be good news? It's better to stop now." They then cried bitterly and parted.

    He returned alone to his hometown, and he too could practice the White Ape's technique, often helping others to right wrongs. If there were cases of ghosts or demons haunting empty houses, He would go with his sword and immediately resolve the issue.

    There was a thief.
    One night, a student read a literary composition, repeating it hundreds of times but still not mastering it. The fourth watch had passed, and his recitation grew louder, and he intended to continue until dawn. A thief was hiding under the bed, growing increasingly agitated. Suddenly, he leaped up and slapped the student, saying, "You're not made of iron; why are you so dull? How can I wait?" He quickly ran out the door, clapping his hands and leaving.

    420

    柏　秀　才
    　　邓州柏生，授馆他郡。岁晏归其里　，道逢妇人携幼子哭于水滨，问其故，曰：“妾夫博而负，其侪索资，将鬻妾以偿。妾宁死不愿，因将溺也，而不忍其子，是以哭。”柏止之，请其金数，曰：“五十。”柏计囊中金适足，见其夫，使召诸博徒，代偿而去。
    　　坐是稽迟，不及村店，已曛黑，乃宿野庙中西阶之下。恍惚见两卒坐于其左，其一曰：“柏秀才，何人也？乃令吾二人守候，为呵禁蛇虺。夜寒衣薄，不得休息，心窃不甘。”其一曰：“阿六，尔又作醉语。顷褚虞侯言：彼乃文人，又新有盛德事，故将军敬之。而不闻耶？幸勿多言，言将笞尔！”于是寂然。心知为鬼役，亦殊不畏。既而门外呵异声甚哗，云“有贵使至。”伏而窥之，见一神蛾冠盛服，仪卫赩赫，皆古时装束。一神甲胄迎入内，语少时，使者旋去。则闻鼓角轰震，士马奔集，旌旂铠仗，行列严整，略如人世行师状。传呼而起，顷刻已遥。

    Explanation

    420

    Bai the Scholar

    Bai, born in Dengzhou, taught in another prefecture. At the end of the year, he returned to his hometown. On the road, he met a woman carrying her young child, crying by the water's edge. He asked her why she was crying, and she replied, "My husband gambled and lost. His companions demanded money, and they will sell me to repay the debt. I would rather die than do so, because I was about to drown myself, but I cannot bear to leave my child, so I am crying." Bai stopped her, asked for the amount of money, and she said, "Fifty." Bai counted the gold in his bag and found it was just enough. He saw her husband and asked him to summon the other gamblers, who paid the money and left.

    Because of this delay, he did not reach the village inn, and it was already dusk. So, he stayed at the west step of a wild temple. Vaguely, he saw two soldiers sitting on his left. One said, "Bai the Scholar, who are you? Why are you making us two wait, to banish snakes and poisonous lizards? It is cold at night and our clothes are thin, we cannot rest, and our hearts secretly resent it." The other said, "Ah Six, you are speaking drunkenly again. Recently, the commander of the guards said: He is a scholar, and he has just done a great good deed, so the general respects him. Didn't you hear? Please do not speak too much, if you do, you will be beaten!" Thus, it became silent. Bai knew they were ghosts' servants, but he was not at all afraid. Soon, there was a loud commotion outside, saying, "There is a noble envoy coming." He crouched and peered out, seeing a divine figure in splendid attire, with a retinue of ancient dress and splendor. A god in armor welcomed him inside, and after a short conversation, the envoy left. Then, he heard the sound of drums and horns, soldiers and horses gathering, banners, armor, and weapons, all in strict formation, resembling an army on the march. He was summoned and rose, and in a moment, it was far away.

    421

    复闻前二卒相语，其一曰：“失马安知非福？吾二人守护柏秀才，乃不与此役，岂非厚幸？”其一曰：“不然，师出而功成，犹可博一头衔，为侪辈不耀，今则已矣！”其一笑曰：“沙场危险，还者几人？尔醉中愦愦，已算定入凌烟阁，大是异事！”其一怒曰：“兄缩项如龟，亦太畏死！男子头颅如许珍重耶？”争攘久之。忽有数骑驰还，遥问曰：“柏秀才尚在此否？”卒应曰：“在。”一军官下马趋入，致礼于柏曰：“将军荐秀才于帝君矣，遣余奉迓。”柏诧曰：“人神异道，何得相干？”军官曰：“帝君命，不可违也！”即请登道，强掖之上马。

    Explanation

    421

    After hearing the two soldiers talking, one said, "Who knows if losing a horse isn't a blessing? By guarding Scholar Bai, we avoided this mission, which is surely great good fortune!" The other replied, "Not so. If the army goes out and achieves success, one can still earn a title, but now we will not shine among our peers!" The first laughed and said, "The battlefield is dangerous, how many return? You're muddled in your drunken state, already planning to be enshrined in the Lingyan Pavilion—that's quite an unusual thing!" The second became angry and said, "Brother, you're drawing in your neck like a turtle, too afraid of death! How precious is a man's head?" They argued for a long time. Suddenly, several riders galloped back and asked from a distance, "Is Scholar Bai still here?" The soldier replied, "He is." A military officer dismounted and hurried inside, bowing to Scholar Bai and saying, "The general has recommended you to the Jade Emperor, and I have been sent to escort you." Scholar Bai was startled and said, "Mortals and deities are separate paths, how can they interfere with each other?" The officer replied, "The Jade Emperor's command cannot be disobeyed!" He then urged Scholar Bai to mount the road and forcibly helped him onto the horse.

    422

    俄至一山，殿阁宏峻。前将军者候于门，引伯进谒。帝君坐于上，丰颐秀髯，颜色和霁，谓伯曰：“迷同国犯境，将加挞伐，或曰降之便，宜先檄之。帐下无能秉笔者，敬授简牍，敢以勤先生。招携服叛，惟先生之赐！”柏谢曰：“宣播威德，义尊辞顺，但书生柔翰，当此钜制，覆　弗胜，恐辱明诏。”帝君曰：“幸勿谦让！”
    　　柏乃坐于旁，草檄云：“蠢尔迷同，栖非岩邑，庇在坰疆。广圃遗芽，天厨剩脔。是以边桑听守，贡茅不徵，惟沧海之容鲕，岂泰山之让卵。迩闻囚首，肆启戎心，螳斧思攻，蜂芒恃螫，践我草木，触我戈　。将驱虎奋之军，立扫鼪藏之穴，关弓尚挂，磨盾先闻。果其风鹤知惊，沙虫自化，仁能大宥，义不穷诛。尚全杵血于降城，毋藏舆尸于京观。”帝君览之，甚嘉叹。

    Explanation

    422

    Soon they arrived at a mountain, where the palace halls were grand and towering. A general was waiting at the gate, who led Ber inside to pay homage. The emperor sat above, with a full face and beautiful beard, his complexion gentle and clear. He said to Ber, "The country of Mitong has violated our borders, and we will punish them severely. Some have suggested it would be better to submit, and it is appropriate to issue a proclamation first. There is no one in the tent who can write, so I respectfully give you the writing materials. I dare to trouble you, sir. To attract and pacify those who have rebelled, only by your grace!" Ber replied, "To spread the emperor's power and virtue, the words are respectful and appropriate, but I am just a scholar with soft hands. For such a grand undertaking, I fear I cannot handle it, and I fear I will dishonor the clear command." The emperor said, "Please do not be modest!"

    Ber then sat beside him and wrote a proclamation, saying: "The foolish people of Mitong, living in a place not suitable for a city, sheltering within the borders. Abandoned fields with leftover sprouts, leftover delicacies from the imperial kitchen. Therefore, the border guards are lax, taxes are not collected. Like the vast ocean that can tolerate small fish, unlike Mount Tai that does not allow eggs to pass. Recently heard that they have bound their heads, openly shown their military intentions. Like a mantis with a knife, thinking to attack, like a bee with a stinger, relying on its sting. Trample on our grass and trees, touch our spears. Will lead a fierce tiger-like army, immediately sweep away their hidden dens. Bows are still strung, shields are polished, heard before the battle. If even the wind and cranes make them wary, sandworms will transform themselves. Mercy can grant great forgiveness, righteousness does not seek endless punishment. Still preserve the blood of the fallen in the surrendered city, do not bury the bodies of the dead in the victory mound." The emperor read it and greatly praised it.

    423

    檄下，迷同犹弗顺。帝君震怒，命将军徂征，以柏参其军政，大选车徒，决机进剿。摧锐捣虚，云飞电扫，不及旬日，迷同破陷，亲属党羽，尽俘以还。帝君命将军磔其长，余悉斩以徇。其长有女绝姣好，频频顾柏而泣。柏怜之，说将军曰：“太公斩妲已，高腛诛张丽华，虽曰刚正，亦殊杀风景，况此乃其息女，非祸水之比，罪人不孥，惟将军怜愍。”将军笑曰：“秀才有爱于彼耶？当为秀才留之。”乃白帝君免其女，即以女赠柏，更欲授柏显秩。柏辞曰：“某虽从帷幄，寸策未献，敢冒爵赏，况游鳞散羽，志在池薮，置之樽俎，反为不详。至于俘女之救，情良不忍，亦非辞封侯之印，而觅爱卿者，岁暮思归，幸即放还，受赐多矣。”帝君踌躇曰：“既先生志行恬退，亦未敢强留。”命具马乘送柏归。

    Explanation

    423

    The edict was issued, but迷同still refused to comply. The divine ruler was greatly angered and ordered the general to launch a campaign, appointing柏to assist in military affairs. A large number of chariots and troops were selected, and decisive strategies were made to advance and suppress. With a force as swift as clouds and lightning, they defeated the strong and struck at the weak within ten days.迷同was defeated and captured, and his relatives and associates were all brought back. The divine ruler ordered the general to dismember the leader and behead the rest, displaying them as examples. The leader had a daughter who was extremely beautiful, and she repeatedly looked at柏and wept.柏pitied her and spoke to the general, saying, "Jiang Ziya slew Daji, and Yang Guang executed Zhang Lihua. Although it is said they were upright and just, it greatly ruined the scenery. As for this, she is his daughter, not comparable to a disaster. The principle is that the guilty are not punished by association; only the general should show mercy." The general laughed and said, "Do you have affection for her, Scholar? I will keep her for you." He then reported to the divine ruler to spare her daughter and gave her to柏. He also wanted to grant柏a high rank.柏declined and said, "Although I have served in the council, I have not yet contributed a single strategy. How dare I accept titles and rewards? As for my free spirit, my aspirations lie in the natural world. Placing her in a confined or domestic life would instead be inauspicious. As for the rescue of the captured daughter, I truly could not bear it. It is also not that I am declining a title of nobility in search of a beloved. At the end of the year, I long to return home. I hope to be released and returned; I have received much favor." The divine ruler hesitated and said, "Since you have a peaceful and retiring disposition, I也不敢force you to stay." He ordered horses and carriages to be prepared to send柏home.

    424

    及于里门，柏忽坠马下，乃如梦觉，人物乌有矣。仓皇至家，若忘若遗。妻问之，秘不以告。夜梦女来曰：“儿之躯命，由君再造，请于帝君，誓从君子，而身形渺茫，不能明奉巾栉。方自悼痛。帝君仁恩，不可思议，使儿附夫人之体，而转移其间。久而俱化，所以酬君之高勋，报君之善行，而慰儿无穷之情也。故特来相就。”言已，遂登床而灭，觉而异之，始缕述于妻，妻弗信也。妻貌故平平，自是乃渐妍丽，不及半年，则神姿逸态，宛然肖迷同之女，见者皆惊。妻往往窥镜，亦自失也。
    　　柏后谒某官，乃绝类庙中将军，探以前事，茫然弗知。及柏归，某官厚赆之，皆神明假借，以彰报施云。
    　　龙虱
    　　有童男女兄妹者戏于庭。空中堕一物，状类鱼。共烹食之。明日，男妇皆暴长丈余，瘦如木，遂废不能起，或以所食龙虱也。

    Explanation

    424

    When he reached the village gate, Ba suddenly fell off his horse, as if waking up from a dream, and there were no people or things around. Hurriedly returning home, he seemed to be in a state of forgetfulness or disorientation. His wife asked him about it, but he kept it secret. That night, a woman came to him in a dream and said, "My body and life were restored by you. I asked the Heavenly Lord to swear to follow the gentleman, but my form is vague and I cannot clearly serve you. I am now grieving and in pain. The Heavenly Lord's benevolence is incomprehensible, making the child attach to your wife's body and transform within. Over time, we both transform, to reward your great deeds and good actions, and to console the child's endless feelings. Therefore, I specially come to join you." After speaking, she climbed onto the bed and disappeared. He woke up and found it strange, so he began to tell his wife in detail, but she did not believe him. His wife's appearance was ordinary, but from then on, she gradually became beautiful. Within half a year, she had a divine and graceful appearance, resembling the girl from the temple, and those who saw her were all surprised. His wife often looked in the mirror and was also surprised.

    Later, Ba visited a certain official, who resembled the general in the temple. He inquired about the previous events, but the official was completely unaware. When Ba returned, the official gave him a generous gift. All these were done by divine intervention to show the reward for good deeds.

    There was once a brother and sister playing in the courtyard. Suddenly, something fell from the sky, resembling a fish. They cooked and ate it together. The next day, both the boy and the girl grew suddenly to over ten feet tall, becoming thin like wood, and they became unable to move. Some believed that they had eaten the dragonfly.

    425

    华广
    　　华广病，梦徐生来谓曰：“顷遇赵君某，言近为鱼梁之游。渔梁，海内胜迹也。赵君招我，嘱我更致君，君盍行乎？”华素爽迈，欣然往。
    　　至深谷之间，溪水黝黑，鉴人无影。渐行渐广，有飞桥跨空，袤延矢矫，莫知所属。桥上行人如蝇，累累不绝。乃见赵俟于桥侧。相揖数语，徐、赵乃登桥，华亦继之。甫举踵，旁一卒叱曰：“勿过！”即横棒拦之，华怒，奋臂与争。卒终不听其前，而徐、赵已去远矣。不得已，拂袖而返，意甚怏怏。道遇伟丈夫，笼群鸟，鸟鸣声甚哀。华恻然，止而说之曰：“羽族志在霄汉，何故笼之？”丈夫曰：“不笼则飞去。”华笑曰：“天传之翼，因当飞去也。”丈夫曰；“公不知此非鸟也，皆罹罪罟之人耳，然公意甚仁，今为公纵之。”乃次第开其笼，独留一大鸟不放。华曰：“何故？”丈夫笑不答，携之而去。

    Explanation

    425

    Hua Guang
    　　Hua Guang was ill and dreamed that Xu Sheng came to him and said, "Recently, I met a certain Lord Zhao, who told me about his recent trip to Fishing Bridge. Fishing Bridge is a famous place within the seas. Lord Zhao invited me, asking me to bring you. Why don't you go?" Hua Guang, who was always spirited and adventurous, went happily.
    　　Upon arriving at a deep valley, the stream water was dark, reflecting no shadow. Gradually, the path became wider, and there was a flying bridge across the sky, stretched out, straight and graceful, with no one knowing where it belonged. People on the bridge were like flies, countless and unending. He then saw Zhao waiting by the side of the bridge. They bowed and exchanged a few words, and Xu and Zhao then ascended the bridge, with Hua following them. Just as he lifted his foot, a soldier nearby shouted, "Do not pass!" and immediately blocked the way with a stick. Hua became angry and raised his arm to argue. The soldier still did not allow him to proceed, but Xu and Zhao had already gone far away. Unable to do anything, Hua waved his sleeve and returned, feeling very disappointed. On the road, he met a tall man with a cage of birds, whose cries were very sad. Hua felt compassion and stopped to speak to the man, saying, "Birds' nature is to soar in the sky. Why are they caged?" The man replied, "If they are not caged, they will fly away." Hua smiled and said, "Given wings by heaven, they should fly away." The man said, "You do not know these are not birds; they are all people who have been ensnared by crime. But your intention is very kind, I will release them for you now." He then opened the cages one by one, leaving only one large bird behind. Hua asked, "Why?" The man smiled and did not answer, taking the bird away with him.

    426

    鸟既出，皆化为人，其一，华故族兄也，泣谢曰：“幸弟援我，然弟亦宜亟归，此不可留也。见我家人，乞为我寄声。”华诺之，别而行。
    　　过高台之下，梯而登焉。俯见城郭室庐，栉比鳞次，村墟烟火相续，树木丛萃，不知是何处。惘然下台。过一市，觉渴，就酒肆呼酒独饮。兴发哦诗曰：“酒魄诗魂落半天，肘生杨柳舌生莲。长松瘦杀千年鹤，飞入春城万灶烟。”忽见族兄至，惊曰：“汝尚饮酒吟诗耶，宜亟亟归，缓则无及矣！”华笑而起，佣保索酒钱，无以应，则持华袂不得行。族兄呵曰：“安得尔？”亟为偿之，送华归。至门，推之入，霍然而苏。
    　　已死逾日，将敛矣。病寻愈，惟胸间闷然者数日，乃以酒故也。徐、赵皆华旧识。时赵死月余；数日，徐讣亦至。

    Explanation

    426

    After the birds had flown away, they all transformed into people. One of them was Hua's brother from the same clan, who wept with gratitude and said, "Thank you for saving me. However, you should hurry back too, as this place is not suitable for staying. When you see my family, please convey my message." Hua promised and they parted ways.

    As Hua climbed the high platform using stairs, he looked down and saw houses and buildings arranged closely together, like fish scales or combs. Villages with continuous smoke from cooking fires and dense forests filled his view, but he did not know where he was. Feeling lost, he descended from the platform. Passing through a market, he felt thirsty and went to a tavern to drink alone. Inspired, he composed a poem:

    "Drunk, the spirit of wine and poetry falls halfway,
    Arms as graceful as willow branches, tongue as delicate as a lotus flower.
    The tall pine tree makes the thousand-year-old crane look thin,
    Flying into the spring city with thousands of cooking fires."

    Suddenly, his brother-in-law appeared and said in surprise, "You are still drinking and composing poetry? You should hurry back, if you delay, it will be too late!" Hua laughed and stood up. The tavern keeper demanded payment, but Hua had no way to pay. His brother-in-law scolded, "How can you?" and quickly paid the debt, sending Hua back. Upon reaching the door, he pushed Hua inside, and Hua suddenly woke up.

    Hua had been dead for more than a day and was about to be buried. Soon after, his illness recovered, but he felt uncomfortable in his chest for several days, due to drinking. Xu and Zhao were both old acquaintances of Hua. At that time, Zhao had been dead for more than a month; after a few days, news of Xu's death also arrived.

    427

    陈著
    　　陈著，富室子也。少时，家遭疫，惟著仅存，一老仆执炊而已。著尝从蒙师受学，颇识字，仆因劝之读，且曰：“他日当不可量。”著深然其言，出钱使市书。
    　　仆至书市，尽买肆中书以归。著乃键户下帷，无寒暑昼夜，挟册呻吟，几破千卷，然略不解文义，虽邸抄公檄与盲辞稗说之类，讽诵如经史。终岁不出户庭间，出则低头背诵，刺刺不休，往往头触墙壁，觉痛则大叫，叫已复诵。或窃窃听所诵，乃颠倒拉杂，音渎讹舛至甚，讪笑之，不顾也。年二十余，未尝与人通酬酢，牛马菽麦不辨。
    　　一日诵书门外，有少妇过之，著未之见也，且行且诵，竟抵其怀。妇大骇且怒。著惶惶恐，遽前抚之，为摩挲其两乳。妇愈益羞怒，面发赤，诟詈而去。著谓人曰“彼何为者？一怒遂不可解乎？”人怜其礸，谕之曰：“男女有嫌，奈何辱之！”著愕然，徐悟曰：“彼殆书所称女子者耶。”人笑颔之，著乃狂喜叫跃，以为得解。

    Explanation

    427

    Chen Zhuo
    Chen Zhuo was the son of a wealthy family. When he was young, his family suffered from an epidemic, and only he survived, with only an old servant left to cook. Chen Zhuo had studied under a private tutor and was fairly literate, so the servant encouraged him to read, saying, "One day, you will be of great importance." Chen Zhuo deeply believed this and spent money to buy books.

    The servant went to the book market and bought all the books in the shop to bring back. Chen Zhuo then closed the door and hung up the curtain, studying day and night, regardless of the seasons. He held books and moaned, almost breaking through a thousand volumes, but he barely understood the meaning of the texts. Even official documents, memorials, and obscure writings were recited by him as if they were the classics and histories. He never left his home all year round; when he did go out, he would recite books while looking down, chattering incessantly, often bumping his head against the wall. When he felt pain, he would shout, and after shouting, he would continue reciting. Sometimes, people would listen secretly to what he was reciting, and it was all jumbled and disordered, with many mispronunciations and errors, causing them to mock him, which he ignored. By the time he was in his twenties, he had never engaged in social interactions and could not distinguish between cattle, horses, soybeans, and wheat.

    One day, while reciting books outside, a young woman passed by. Chen Zhuo did not see her and continued reciting as he walked, eventually bumping into her chest. The woman was greatly startled and angry. Chen Zhuo was terrified, quickly approached her to comfort her, and began to stroke her two breasts. The woman became even more embarrassed and angry, her face turning red, and she cursed at him and left. Chen Zhuo said to someone, "What did she do? Why is she so angry and cannot be appeased?" The person pitied his simplicity and told him, "There is a taboo between men and women. How can you humiliate her?" Chen Zhuo was startled and slowly realized, "She must be the woman mentioned in the books." The person nodded with a smile, and Chen Zhuo cried out joyfully and leaped around, thinking he had understood.

    428

    他日读《毛诗》，至“女子善怀，亦各有行。”点首叹曰：“书言之矣，昔者女子　行而我触其怀，宜其怒耳。书义深远不可背如此。”三复不已。由是读书，每冥索其解，解多类是。
    　　又日坐门外，遇物辄咨访于人，冀博识其名与状，似佐证其所读。有豕触藩，出视之，不识也，惧而去走。或告曰：“猪耳，何畏？”著误以为珠，迫而视之，恍然曰：“物不经见，固难悬揣。始吾以珠小物耳，今而知珠能行也。”即问曰：“鬻乎？”或为质主人。主人故昂其值，乃以三十千市之。著窃喜，以为书言珠价之贵，今乃贱获焉，大利也。于是谲者利其值，竞以猪来售，至则买之，无论大小准前价。老仆力谏，卒不听。期年得猪数百头，栏栅不能容，秽籍纵横室。佣数人饲之，日不暇给。豕声□□然，昼夜与书声相乱。著亦渐不能堪，幡然曰：“昔人宝珠，殊不可解。”命悉逐去之。计所耗费，殆累数百千。家以是少倾焉。

    Explanation

    428

    One day, while reading the Mao Shi, he came across the line "Women are good at harboring feelings, and each has her own way." He nodded and sighed, saying, "The text speaks of this. Once, when a woman was walking and I accidentally touched her belly, it was natural for her to be angry. The meaning of the text is profound and cannot be contradicted like this." He repeatedly reviewed the passage, unable to stop. From then on, whenever he read, he would deeply ponder its meaning, and many of his interpretations were similar to this.

    Another day, he sat outside, encountering things and immediately consulting others, hoping to broaden his knowledge of their names and appearances, as if to support what he had read. A pig bumped into a fence, and he went out to see it, not recognizing it, and was afraid, running away. Someone told him, "It's just a pig, what are you afraid of?"
