Entering the bag and hitting the bed, it disturbs the quiet sitter; turning over pots and peering into jars, it further disrupts clear sleep. In the courtyard and on the roof beams, it climbs and fights fiercely; in hats, boxes, and chests, it destroys and wanders. This is already intolerable, and I still don't ask. Even to the point of leaving dust on the bed, it spreads to the drawings and signs in the connected houses. High shelves and cloud-like books, it uses the wall as a ladder. Red and yellow colors peeling off, I am suffering from the errors of pigs and boars; blue and green colors fading away, you further assist the harm of the bookworms. Though hundreds of cities are empty, it cannot avoid being shameful; and frequently forgetting the three chests, how can there be no regret?

    511

    呜呼！乌圆不作，白老难求，方幻化之无穷，讵鸱衔之可尽？发机匪易，掘隧仍难。遂以丸而旋来，却以刀而不畏。寸光昼逞，万状宵兴。跳梁已过于悬猱，营窟还多于狡兔。见忘吐肠之悔，稔恶不悛；即置剖腹之刑，馀辜莫逭。惟尔猫奴，实称鼠将。循名核实，非徒夸饭鸭之能；积事程功，宁虚有衔蝉之表？况乎修鲁直之聘，礼数良优；护放翁之书，职司攸重。岂其花阴趁蝶，雅好清闲；楸局翻棋，徒供戏弄。

    Explanation

    511

    Alas! The round bird is no more, and the white old one is hard to find. In the infinite transformations, how could the carrying of owls exhaust it? It is not easy to set the mechanism in motion, and digging a tunnel is still difficult. Thus, with a mouse in hand, it returns; yet it fears not the knife. It flaunts its brilliance in the daytime, and in myriad forms it rises at night. Its agility surpasses that of a hanging monkey, and its dens are more numerous than those of a cunning rabbit. It forgets the regret of spilling its guts, and its evil is entrenched; if punished with a belly-splitting penalty, its remaining guilt cannot be avoided. You, the cat's servant, truly deserve the title of mouse commander. Following the name and verifying the reality, it is not just to boast of the ability to catch ducks; accumulating deeds and measuring success, it does not merely have an outward appearance of holding a cicada? Moreover, in delivering the message of Lu Shizong, the etiquette is excellent; in protecting Lu You's books, the responsibility is significant. Is it merely to chase butterflies in the shade, enjoying leisure; or to play Go, just for amusement?

    512

    以致室无完器，案有残笺，听若辈之公行，如强邻之逼处。甚或薄荷沉醉，苦竹横陈，纵夺食而无争，便同眠而不拒。扼喉真俟于来世，锯耳定卜于何年？虽曰慈悲，得毋懒惰？尚及全更鸡德，大奋虎威；暂开似线之眸，速掉如蛇之尾。罻兹宵小，歼厥渠魁。庇及椸枷，勋存几席。途原非远，姑同入灶之行；味即不佳，聊当餐鱼之饭。庶几眠毡藉毯，略用武于爪牙；亦免撤瓦张罗，差解嘲于耳目。噫嘻！诘猫无计，将求许迈之书符；磔鼠惟文，窃比张汤之断狱。檄下，如律令。
    　　忘误
    　　某夜梦邻人招饮。旦而诣之曰：“公何事召客？”主人讶然。某亦徐悟曰：“殆梦耶！”大惭欲出，主人笑留之，为具食。他日，邻真召之饮，某疑亦梦也。使者敦促至再，始敢赴。

    Explanation

    512

    To the point that the room had no intact objects, and the desk was scattered with torn notes, hearing the public behavior of these people was like being pressured by a powerful neighbor. Even to the point of being intoxicated with thin herbs, and having bitter bamboo laid out, allowing them to take food without resistance, and even sleeping together without refusal. Truly waiting until the next life to choke their throats, and deciding on what year to saw off their ears? Though called compassion, isn't it perhaps laziness? Still, to fully embody the virtue of the rooster and greatly unleash the tiger's might; temporarily open eyes like threads, and swiftly flick tails like snakes. Eliminate these small-time criminals and their leaders. Protect even the clothes on the stand and the seats, and preserve merit on the tables. The road is not far, so we might as well take the same route to the stove; if the taste is not good, it can still be considered a fish meal. Perhaps we can sleep on mats and blankets, using our claws and teeth sparingly; and also avoid tearing tiles and spreading nets, which would be a bit of a joke to the ears and eyes. Ah! To find no way to deal with cats, I will seek the magical talisman of Xu Mai; to judge mice, I will compare it to the judgment of Zhang Tang. Issue the decree, as by law.

    A Mistake

    One night, I dreamed that a neighbor invited me to a meal. In the morning, I went to his house and asked, "Why did you invite guests?" The host was surprised. I slowly realized, "Was it a dream?" I felt greatly embarrassed and wanted to leave, but the host laughed and kept me, preparing a meal. Another day, the neighbor truly invited me to a meal, and I doubted if it was also a dream. The messenger repeatedly urged me, and I finally dared to go.

    513

    又有某公者，尝自外入，见其妻共男子款语，大怒，更不审视，遽上常叱曰：“何物狂子，白昼公然调人妇！”妻诟曰：“瞽也，何妄言之甚？”某因谛视之，妻弟也，惶恐笑谢。后其妻私一少年，值某于寝门，奔去，某愕然，徐忆前事，以为妻弟也。诘妻曰：“舅何一匆遽？”妻因绐曰：“恐复见叱耳！”某信之，亦更不忆面目之不似。
    　　又李某者，性纰缪。里中岁暮家书邮至，诸商于外者，其家各就邮索书。李遽闻之，亦往索。邮问：“公何人在客？”李恍然曰：“固无之。”一笑而返。
    　　又某公者，尝昼寝，同侪者戏剪其髯，仅存萌蘖。某醒亦殊忘之，妻见而大笑，问公髯安在？某台探颐，记向果有髯。适有剃发者过其门，遂疑髯为所薙去，径执而拳之。其人骇问，得其故，力辨乃解。

    Explanation

    513

    There was once a certain official who returned from outside and saw his wife conversing intimately with a man. He became very angry and did not look closely, immediately shouting, "What kind of crazy person, openly flirting with another man's wife in broad daylight!" His wife scolded him, saying, "You're blind, how wildly you speak!" The official then carefully looked at the man and realized it was his wife's brother, who was frightened and laughed in apology. Later, his wife secretly met a young man, and the official happened to be at the bedroom door. She ran away, and the official was stunned, slowly recalling the previous incident, thinking it was his wife's brother again. He questioned his wife, "Why are you so hasty, uncle?" The wife then lied, saying, "I'm afraid you'll shout at me again!" The official believed her and did not remember that the face was not similar.

    Another person named Li had a peculiar nature. At the end of the year, family letters arrived by post, and the families of those who were away from home each went to the post office to collect their letters. Li suddenly heard about it and also went to collect his letter. The postman asked, "Who is the gentleman who is a guest here?" Li replied vaguely, "I don't know anyone." He laughed and returned.

    Another official once napped during the day, and his peers playfully cut off his beard, leaving only stubble. When he woke up, he completely forgot about it. His wife saw this and laughed, asking where his beard was. The official probed his memory and recalled that he indeed had a beard. Just then, a barber passed by his door, and he suspected that the barber had shaved it off. He immediately grabbed and punched the barber. The barber was startled and asked what happened. After hearing the reason, he vigorously defended himself, and the official finally understood.

    514

    或假某公衣数日，送还之。某已不记，但问曰：“欲质耶？估耶？”或因诡应曰：“亦估耳。”与往复竞价，竟以数千钱买之。
    　　某生就傅于外，数归视其妻。一日者。又将归矣，其友伺其睡，戏取灶煤画圈于其腹，生固弗觉也。及生来，友故避而出于外，迟回而后入。生问曰：“公何之？”友故不即答，又故作忸怩之色。生诘之，友乃长揖曰：“公素长者，又厚昵于我，我不忍复欺公，然公不罪我，我乃敢相告！”生曰：“诺。云何？”友曰：“适访公于家，公已　出，暂遇贤夫人，蒙其眷爱。”生骇然未信，友曰：“其脐下有圈，吾所画也。”生大怒趋归，见其妻，亦更不他语，趣解衣而验腹焉，果有圈，始数而诟之，拂袖竟出。偶就溺，见己腹有圈，始悟其印也。复归，妻已挂梁间几死。

    Explanation

    514

    Someone borrowed Mr. X's clothes for several days and returned them. Mr. X had already forgotten about them, but he asked, "Do you want to pawn them? Or sell them?" The person casually replied, "Just appraising/selling them." They bargained back and forth, and finally bought them for several thousand coins.

    Mr. X studied outside the home and often returned to see his wife. One day, he was about to return home. His friend waited until he fell asleep, playfully took some kitchen coal, and drew a circle on his belly. Mr. X did not notice it at all. When Mr. X woke up, the friend deliberately avoided and went out, delaying and then entering. Mr. X asked, "Where are you going?" The friend deliberately did not answer immediately and made a shy expression. Mr. X questioned him, and the friend then bowed deeply and said, "You are always a gentleman, and you are very close to me. I couldn't bear to deceive you again, but if you don't blame me, I will dare to tell you!" Mr. X said, "Okay. What happened?" The friend replied, "Just visited you at home, you had already left, and briefly met your virtuous wife, who received her favor." Mr. X was startled and did not believe it, so the friend said, "There was a circle on his navel, which I drew." Mr. X became very angry and rushed home. He saw his wife, did not say anything else, quickly undressed, and checked her belly. Sure enough, there was a circle, so he started counting and scolded her, waving his sleeve and leaving. Accidentally, he went to the toilet and saw a circle on his own belly, finally realizing it was an imprint. He returned, and his wife was already hanging from the beam, nearly dead.

    515

    某氏女将嫁，其母戒之曰：“婿家不可深恃也，须自计以防厥后。”女曰：“诺。”既嫁，数盗钱谷藏母家。姑觉而出之。母乃谓女曰：“吾固曰不可恃也。”
    　　县中代人受杖者曰毛鬼。某乙闻而慕之，乃代某甲杖，与之二金。既受杖，楚甚，急以二金赂行杖之隶，杖乃轻。乙出谢甲曰：“非公金为赂，杖几死。”
    　　虾蟆作雹
    　　京师某公，尝参喇嘛章嘉师。适雨雹，问雹何以成？师漫应曰：“虾蟆所作耳。”某公意其诞，师曰：“姑志之，异日见之当信耳。”后某公以事西出嘉峪关，值天昏欲雨，止野庙中，见土人聚观河上。问何故，曰：“视虾蟆作雹。”某公顿忆师语，近观之，见虾蟆千万衔岸上土少许，复饮水河中，已，张口岸上，口中皆雹也。大者成大雹，小者成小雹，须臾吐之，风捲而去。

    515

    A Certain Woman

    A woman from a certain family was about to be married, and her mother warned her, saying, "Do not rely too much on your husband's family; you must plan for yourself to prevent future problems." The woman replied, "Yes." After she was married, she often stole money and grain and hid them at her mother's house. Her grandmother found out and expelled her. The mother then told the woman, "I said so already, you cannot rely on them."

    In the county, the person who takes beatings for others is called "Mao Ghost." Mr. Yi heard of this and admired it, so he took the beating for Mr. Jia and gave him two gold pieces. The beating was severe, so he quickly bribed the person administering the beating with the two gold pieces, and the beating then became lighter. Mr. Yi thanked Mr. Jia, saying, "It was not your money that was used as a bribe; the beating almost killed me."

    Frogs Make Hail

    A certain official in the capital had attended the Lama Zangjia's teacher. One day, it rained hail, and he asked how hail is formed. The teacher casually replied, "It is made by frogs." The official thought the teacher was joking, but the teacher said, "Just note it; you will believe it when you see it one day." Later, the official went west out of Jiayu Pass, and it was dusk and about to rain. He stopped at a temple in the countryside and saw the locals gathering by the river. He asked why, and they said, "We are watching the frogs making hail." The official suddenly remembered the teacher's words and approached to observe. He saw thousands of frogs carrying small amounts of soil from the shore, then drinking water from the river. They opened their mouths and spat on the shore, and all the hail was in their mouths. The larger ones made larger hailstones, the smaller ones made smaller hailstones. In a moment, they spat it out, and the wind swept it away.

    516

    水先生
    　　顺治中，虎贲某公者，延水先生傅其子。水盖越人，年可四十馀，风貌冲蔼。某休退之暇，常与晤言，颇契洽，盖宾而友之者也。水每值三六九日，必出访友人。积二年。某偶宿斋中，与水对榻。一夕漏下俱寝矣。夜中某觉，见水坐灯下，身已急装，匕首照人，气若鬼神，非复故态。乃佯寝以侦其变。俄焉门启，剨然遂去。某骇而俟之，将曙，门复启，水至。提人首累累滴血，徐取药弹之，皆缩小，尽纳口中，灭烛就枕睡。某悸甚。明日，水问曰：“夜来须见否？”某讳之。水笑曰：“形迹既露，敢不告公？昔闯贼寇乱，某从其副小红狼，知其无能也，去之。贼乃恨我，诱杀我父母妻子，我方欲报之，会大兵入关，妖孛溃除。知此贼遁去，廉之数年，今始毕之，向之屡出，良为此耳。公遇我殊厚，然不可留。”乃别而去。

    Explanation

    516

    Mr. Shui
    During the reign of Shunzhi, a certain retired official from the Tiger Guard invited Mr. Shui to tutor his son. Mr. Shui was from Yue, about forty years old, with a calm and gentle demeanor. In his leisure time after retirement, the official often conversed with him, and they were quite compatible, considering him both a guest and a friend. Mr. Shui would always visit his friends on market days. After two years, the official once happened to stay in the study, sharing a bed with Mr. Shui. One night, they both fell asleep after the clock struck. During the night, the official woke up and saw Mr. Shui sitting by the lamp, dressed in a hurry, with a dagger shining in the light, exuding an aura like that of a ghost or deity, no longer the same demeanor. The official pretended to sleep to observe any changes. Suddenly, the door opened, and Mr. Shui left abruptly. The official was greatly frightened and waited for him, just before dawn, the door opened again, and Mr. Shui returned. He carried a head dripping with blood, slowly took out a medicine, reduced the size of the heads, swallowed them all, extinguished the candle, and went to bed to sleep. The official was greatly frightened. The next day, Mr. Shui asked, "Did you see anything last night?" The official evaded the question. Mr. Shui laughed and said, "The signs have already been revealed, how dare I not tell you? In the past, when the rebels caused chaos, I followed the deputy Xiao Honglang, knowing he was incapable, I left. The rebels then hated me, lured and killed my parents and wife. I was about to avenge them when the great army entered the pass, and the evil was eliminated. Knowing this rebel had fled, I investigated for several years, only now have I completed it. The previous frequent outings were all for this reason. You treated me very well, but I cannot stay." He then bade farewell and left.

    517

    陶　金　铃
    　　姑苏小伶陶金铃，本良家子。少业儒，尝赴郡应童子试，旅于城南卖酒家。夜梦某观察宴客，召梨园长乐部佐酒，演《玉簪记》，所谓潘必正，陈妙常者也。金铃故不习优，亦殊自忘之，扮妙常而登场焉。管弦金鼓之间，进止合度，而声情特妙。
    　　乐阑宾散，诸伶皆退。观察独召之入内，小酌于媚香之楼。翠钿红袖，姬侍如云。金铃是时年十有五矣，杂坐其间，星眸环照，莫敢谁何。一名绣云者尤丽，其属意金铃也亦尤厚。于是次第度曲，竞斗歌喉，间有误处，使金铃正之。后堂丝竹，视当声为胜。

    Explanation

    517

    Jinling, the Young Performer

    Jinling, a young performer from Suzhou, was originally from a respectable family. As a youth, she studied Confucianism and once traveled to the southern part of the city to take the examination for young scholars. One night, she dreamed of an official hosting a banquet for guests, summoning the Longle Department of the Imperial Theater to entertain with wine. They performed The Jade Hairpin, a play featuring the characters Pan Bize and Chen Miaochang. Though Jinling had never trained in the performing arts, she forgot herself and took the stage dressed as Chen Miaochang. Amidst the music and drums, her movements were graceful and well-coordinated, and her voice and emotion were particularly excellent.

    As the music ended and the guests dispersed, all the performers left. The official alone called her into the inner chamber for a small drink at the Fragrant Pavilion. There, beautiful women with ornate hairpins and red sleeves surrounded her like clouds. Jinling was then fifteen years old, mingling among them with her starry eyes gazing around, and none dared to question her presence. One woman named Xiu Yun was especially beautiful, and she had a particular fondness for Jinling. They took turns performing songs, competing in their singing, and if there were any mistakes, Jinling would correct them. In the back hall, the music was played, and the sound was considered superior.

    518

    已而观察曰“旧曲习听，宜各奏新声。”一姬乃唱曰：“袅袅腰肢细，是楼外垂杨，教人旖旎。晓鬟偷学暮鸦飞，更琼梳小掠春云腻。新月纤纤，刚描一线，赛不守两弯眉翠。问秋千锦索系罗衣，直恁莲勾飞起，为前日双燕来时，斗他剪水凌风戏。单消受不惯香醪滋味，倩郎君转倩桃花，替侬家今夜为郎沉醉。”观察顾金铃笑曰：“汝权为桃花可也。”遂酌以饮之。金铃亦取大斗，引满奉观察。一姬继唱曰：“烛花儿分外光荧，酒波儿分外香馨。宫纱扇子裹著袖儿擎，背面儿漏出梅花影，闪烁了郎的眼睛。偷觑了几回，只是不分明。登时恼乱狂蜂儿的性。这一夜是何等恩情，何等光景。到如今隔着纸儿唤不应，对着帐儿呼不醒，敢则是你侬故意儿薄幸。”观察大笑，为连举数觥。

    Explanation

    518

    Afterward, the observer said, "Familiar songs are commonly heard; it is appropriate for each to play a new tune." One of the performers then sang, "Her slender waist is as graceful as a willow tree outside a building, captivating and enchanting. In the morning, her hairstyle resembles evening crows flying, and with a jade comb, she gently smooths her hair like spring clouds. The thin crescent moon, just a delicate line, matches her finely arched eyebrows. Asking about the swing ropes tied to her silk dress, they gracefully rise, just like the swallows that played the day before, cutting through the wind. She finds it hard to get used to the taste of wine, so she asks her lover to entrust the task to peach blossoms, to help her get drunk tonight for him." The observer looked at Jinling and smiled, saying, "You can act as the peach blossom." He then poured and drank with her. Jinling also took a large cup, filled it, and offered it to the observer. Another performer sang, "The candle flames are exceptionally bright, and the wine waves are fragrant. Holding a palace silk fan in her sleeve, a plum blossom shadow is visible on the back, flickering in the lover's eyes. She peeps several times but cannot see clearly. This immediately annoys the lover. What kind of love and scene is this night? Now, she cannot call through a paper screen or wake up through a curtain, perhaps because you are intentionally unfaithful." The observer laughed and raised several cups in a toast.

    519

    一姬又唱曰：“窗纱密密，帘押重重。围住了一楼春梦，透不出一线儿春风。海棠全是旧时的红，盼不上黄昏细雨沾花重，有多少风催雨送，倒不教艳色竟成空。不敢恼公，不敢恼侬，恨孤鸾无故飞入侬的命宫，甚因缘把红丝牵动？”一姬唱曰：“凤箫儿吹得人魂灵飘飘，筝弦儿拨得人情丝袅袅，玉笙儿吸得心花摇，檀板儿拍得泪珠儿掉，一声声都是断肠鸟，唱得樱桃唇焦、莲花舌翘，意思儿仍是没分晓。好模糊的相思曲调，准备着银壶漏尽金鸡叫。”或风情之靡曼，或哀怨之缠绵，金铃斯时若近若远，若危若安，嗒焉坐忘，不疑身在人间也。

    Explanation

    519

    One of the courtesans began to sing again: "The window screens are dense, the curtains heavy. Enclosed within, a dream of spring, unable to let even a thread of spring breeze through. The hawthorn flowers retain their old red hue, yearning for the evening rain to make them bloom anew, yet how many winds and rains have come and gone, only to leave the beauty unfulfilled. I dare not anger you, I dare not anger myself, hating how a solitary phoenix flew into my fate without reason, what karmic bond wove the red thread?" Another courtesan sang: "The flute of the phoenix makes the soul drift, the zither strings evoke tender emotions, the jade flute causes the heart to flutter, the wooden clapper makes tears fall. Each note is a heart-wrenching bird, singing until the lips are parched and the tongue strained, the meaning still unclear. A vague tune of longing, preparing for the silver pot to run out and the rooster to crow. Whether it be the charm of the courtesans or the lingering sorrow in their songs, the sound of the golden bell is both near and far, safe and dangerous, lost in a trance, doubting whether one is still in the mortal world."

    520

    最后绣云发声，声尤掩抑不可听。其词曰：“一抹青螺，一寸横波。甚玉兔化身，浑似嫦娥。饶是聪明，真假雌雄猜不破，一霎时春愁无那。周旋回避，尽教人两般都错。却待恁般才可。料不是闻清歌，唤奈何？小黄鹂飞上花梢坐，花枝忒煞多，怎到得吾侬两个。此意同缄锁。上天日月，下地山河，眼前灯火，只落得侬知他意渠怜我。”时观察已中酒昏然，故然女歌词俱不闻也。”
    　　少顷，这金铃出宿于西轩。金伶甚惆怅，伏枕凝想，恍惚成寐。忽梦一侍儿来请，遂引之至一阁中，香兽氤氲，珠翠溢目。却见绣云宛然在榻，起迎金铃。遽相偎倚。金铃私问：“观察亦安在？”绣云曰：“此时尚关渠事耶？幸复无虑。请君为潘郎，吾为陈姑，复演《窃词》一折耳。”金铃喜甚。方欲搴帷，忽闻帘外鹦鹉连呼：“相公来！”绣云推之，乃惊寐，则身仍卧西轩中。

    Explanation

    520

    Finally, Xiu Yun spoke, her voice particularly subdued and barely audible. Her words went: "A touch of green snail, an inch of crossing waves. How much like a jade rabbit transformed, she looks very much like Chang'e. Even if intelligent, one cannot distinguish between true and false, male and female. In a moment, spring sorrow overwhelms. Evading and avoiding, both ways are wrong. Only by waiting for such a way can it be done. Probably not by hearing a clear song, what to do? A little yellow oriole flies up to perch on the flower branch, with too many flower branches, how can we two reach? This meaning is sealed and locked. Heaven and the sun and moon, earth and mountains and rivers, lights in front of the eyes, only left for me to know his meaning and him to pity me." At that time, the observer was drunk and dazed, so he did not hear the women's lyrics.

    A short while later, Jin Ling went to sleep in the western pavilion. Jin Ling was very melancholy, lying down and pondering, and fell into a drowsy state. Suddenly, he dreamed of a maid coming to invite him, and she led him to a pavilion where the fragrance of incense was thick, and the brilliance of pearls and jade dazzled the eyes. Xiu Yun was still on the bed, getting up to greet Jin Ling. They quickly embraced each other. Jin Ling privately asked, "Where is the observer?" Xiu Yun said, "Does this matter still concern him? Fortunately, there is no need to worry. Please be my Pan Lang, and I will be Chen Gu, and we will perform another act of 'Qie Ci'." Jin Ling was very happy. Just as he was about to lift the curtain, he suddenly heard a parrot outside the curtain calling repeatedly, "The master is coming!" Xiu Yun pushed him, and he woke up, realizing that he was still lying in the western pavilion.

    521

    且悔且忆，而謣然一声，忽复张眼，则身实卧卖酒家，并非西轩也。朝暾射牖，揽衣遽兴。而雀方斗于两檐间，破瓦在地焉。深自嗟讶，盖梦之中又占其梦矣。梦中情事，记之了了。他日以所演《玉簪》，质之梨园，节目皆合。
    　　金铃由是竟善讴。试度他曲，过耳辄能。既而学使者按试，金铃不见录。而闻他郡梨园果有所谓长乐部者。潜往访之，则部中诸伶恍然如旧识。益讶向者之梦良非偶然，殆数也。乃易士而优，隶长乐部，声伎为一时之冠。大江南北，转徙经年。果又有所谓某观察者。一日置酒宴客，果召长乐部奏技。至则台榭犹是也，宾客犹是也。是日果演《玉簪记》。酒阑客散，果召之入内小饮。观察诸姬又皆如旧识。桃源重来，槐安真到，事境虽是，而情转深矣。既而莺簧珠串，歌管皆同；酒盏觥筹，笑言无异。惟绣云玉肌瘦损，蛾黛凄然，终席无一语，不复歌前日之曲，此其小变也。

    Explanation

    521

    Regretting and remembering, suddenly a loud sound, I opened my eyes, and found myself actually lying in a wine shop, not in the Western Pavilion. The morning sun shone through the window, and I hastily got up. Sparrows were fighting on the eaves, and broken tiles lay on the ground. Deeply regretting and wondering, it seemed that even in the dream, I was dreaming. The events in the dream, remembered clearly. One day, using the performance of The Jade Hairpin, I questioned the actors, and all the details matched.

    Jinling thus became skilled at singing. When trying to sing other songs, he could do so after hearing them once. Later, the examiner came to test, and Jinling was not recorded. But heard that in another region, there was indeed a group called the Changle Department. Secretly went to visit them, and the actors in the group seemed familiar, like old acquaintances. Increasingly, I wondered if the previous dream was not accidental, but perhaps fate. He then changed from scholar to actor, joining the Changle Department, and his performance was the best of his time. He traveled throughout the Yangtze River region for many years. There was indeed someone called a certain Observer. One day, he hosted a banquet for guests, and invited the Changle Department to perform. When they arrived, the pavilions and guests were still the same. That day, they performed The Jade Hairpin. When the wine was finished and the guests left, he invited them to have a small drink inside. The female attendants of the Observer were also like old acquaintances. Returning to the Peach Blossom Spring, truly arriving at the Dream of the Southern Capital, the situation was the same, but the emotions were even deeper. Later, the songs and music were the same, and the wine cups and laughter were the same. Only Xiuyun's jade-like skin had become thinner, and her eyebrows were sorrowful, and she remained silent throughout the meal and did not sing the songs from the previous day, this was a small change.

    522

    及小酌既罢，金铃果出宿西轩，欻然入梦，梦入于绣云之寝。心惩前事，不暇他语，欲亟遂幽欢以偿夙愿。而既见绣云殊不自由，转辗之间，竟忘前事，仍问“观察安在”，仍作潘郎，仍闻鹦鹉呼“相公”，仍为绣云所推而觉，仍卧西轩中。瞿然自惊，爽然自失，复哑然自笑。盖是夕之梦，畴昔梦中之梦也。数之前定者，卒不或爽，竟有如此梦中之梦、戏中之戏，变幻于是焉极矣。
    　　金铃本名铎，金铃其小字也。人以其伶也呼之。

    Explanation

    522

    After the light drink had finished, Jin Ling indeed went to sleep in the west pavilion and suddenly entered a dream, in which he dreamed of entering the embroidered cloud bedroom. His heart, punished by past events, had no time for other words, and he wanted to quickly fulfill the secret joy to satisfy his long-held wish. However, having seen Xiu Yun, she was not free, and in the process of turning, he ultimately forgot the past. He still asked, "Where is the observer?" still acted as Pan Lang, still heard the parrot call "Master," and still was pushed by Xiu Yun and woke up, still lying in the west pavilion. Suddenly startled and shocked, he felt lost and disoriented, then silently laughed to himself. This night's dream was a dream within a dream from the past. What was determined by numbers never fails; there was indeed such a dream within a dream, a play within a play, and the changes reached their extreme here.

    Jin Ling's original name was Dui, and Jin Ling was his nickname. People called him by his nickname because he was a performer.
